Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.rules-engine (simple table-driven reminders, eligibility conditions, care-gap predicates) as a lightweight in-process fallback when CQL is overkill. No FHIR awareness; facts are arbitrary JS objects.
register.stack_fit: Zero deps, pure TS. NestJS: inject Engine as provider, load rules from DB at startup. Rules are plain JSON - store in Postgres, edit via builder-core no-code UI. Bun-compatible. Zod validates rule schema at ingestion boundary.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (embedded in-process). No SaaS option.
register.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/cds-sdk, thin wrapper exposing typed RuleEngine factory); codegen emitter generates rule schema JSON stubs per entity type.
register.person_centric_fit: Can drive simple person-facing nudges (immunization due, screening overdue) where full CQL is not warranted. Fast to author new rules for clinical staff via builder-core JSON editor.

register.name: CDS Hooks specification (protocol reference, no mature npm library)
register.what: HL7 CDS Hooks v2.0 is the wire protocol for synchronous, workflow-triggered CDS. Defines hook types (patient-view, order-select, order-sign, encounter-discharge) and JSON Card response format (info, suggestion, app-link). Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (hook dispatch protocol). No maintained npm library exists - implement as a NestJS controller handling POST /cds-services/{hookId}. Existing npm packages (@asymmetrik/node-cds-hooks, cds-hooks) are stagnant.
register.taxonomy: HL7 CDS Hooks v2.0 is the wire protocol for synchronous, workflow-triggered CDS. Defines hook types (patient-view, order-select, order-sign, encounter-discharge) and JSON Card response format (info, suggestion, app-link). Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (hook dispatch protocol). No maintained npm library exists - implement as a NestJS controller handling POST /cds-services/{hookId}. Existing npm packages (@asymmetrik/node-cds-hooks, cds-hooks) are stagnant.
register.stack_fit: Implement CDS Hooks responder as a NestJS controller: validate JWT (JWKS from EHR), parse hook context (FHIR R4 prefetch bundle), call internal rule engines (ZEN / cql-execution / fqm-execution), return Card[] response. Zod schema validates incoming hook payload and outgoing Card. No npm library needed; spec is simple enough (one POST endpoint + Card schema) to implement in ~150 LOC.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: NestJS CDS Hooks handler (first-party). BYO-3rd-party: point NestJS at cqf-ruler /cds-services as the upstream resolver.
register.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (CdsHookRequest + Card schema in healthstack-cds.tsp); codegen emitter generates typed NestJS controller stub per hook type; @curaos/cds-sdk exposes Card builder helpers.
register.person_centric_fit: CDS Hooks Cards surface at point-of-care (prescribing, order sign). Suggestions in Cards can trigger patient-facing tasks/nudges via automation-core event emission after clinician action.

register.name: nools (noolsjs/nools)
register.what: Rete-based forward-chaining rules engine in JavaScript (Node.js). Drools analog in JS. Fills healthcare.care.rules-engine with inference chain support.
register.taxonomy: Rete-based forward-chaining rules engine in JavaScript (Node.js). Drools analog in JS. Fills healthcare.care.rules-engine with inference chain support.
register.stack_fit: No TypeScript types. No recent releases. No active maintainer.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (embedded). Abandoned.
register.generator_first_target: N/A
register.person_centric_fit: N/A - abandoned project introduces technical debt risk.

register.name: google/cql (experimental Go engine)
register.what: Experimental Go CQL execution engine from Google with Beam job for large-population eCQM runs. Fills healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards at massive scale (BigQuery-backed). Not a Node/TS library.
register.taxonomy: Experimental Go CQL execution engine from Google with Beam job for large-population eCQM runs. Fills healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards at massive scale (BigQuery-backed). Not a Node/TS library.
register.stack_fit: Go binary, not embeddable in NestJS. Would require sidecar + HTTP bridge. Experimental - not production-ready per Google's own labeling.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (Go binary/container).
register.generator_first_target: N/A - reference only for future high-scale population health analytics lane.
register.person_centric_fit: Population-scale batch runs (Beam/BigQuery), not real-time person-facing CDS.

register.name: Novu (@novu/framework + @novu/react)
register.what: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
register.taxonomy: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
register.license: MIT (core) + commercial Enterprise Edition (SSO, audit logs)
register.stack_fit: @novu/framework/nest ships NovuModule.register() - zero wrapper needed for NestJS. @novu/react drops directly into CuraOS ui-kit. backendUrl+socketUrl overrides point React component at self-hosted API. Bun runtime works (bun compile is separate issue, not relevant for service usage). TypeSpec event contract can emit Novu workflow trigger shapes.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose + community Helm chart (not official). BYO-3rd-party: Novu Cloud ($30/mo per 30k runs). Both paths use identical SDK + workflow code - full [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] compliance.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@novu/framework): workflow definitions go in notify-core-service, emitted by codegen mold as .workflow.ts files alongside TypeSpec contracts. @novu/react plugs into ui-kit as InboxWidget.
register.person_centric_fit: Preference center per subscriber (person controls which channels/types they receive). In-app Inbox is person-facing real-time feed. Notification history queryable by subscriber. Strong dual-surface fit: person sees their feed; management surface triggers and monitors workflows.

register.name: react-email (@react-email/render + @react-email/components)
register.what: JSX-to-HTML email rendering library: write email templates as React/TSX components, render to HTML string for any SMTP/HTTP provider. Covers email template authoring layer for notify-core-service and healthstack notification surfaces (appointment reminders, care plan updates, clinical alerts). Not a delivery service - pure rendering.
register.taxonomy: JSX-to-HTML email rendering library: write email templates as React/TSX components, render to HTML string for any SMTP/HTTP provider. Covers email template authoring layer for notify-core-service and healthstack notification surfaces (appointment reminders, care plan updates, clinical alerts). Not a delivery service - pure rendering.
register.stack_fit: Pure npm import, zero framework coupling. Works with NestJS + nodemailer. Known issue: does not work with `bun compile` binary (issue #2585 open) - irrelevant for service mode (Bun-as-runtime works fine). Turborepo/monorepo compatible. Templates live in @curaos/email-templates package, shared across services.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Provider-agnostic by design: render to HTML, send via Postal (self-host), Resend, SES, or any SMTP. Covers both local and 3rd-party delivery.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target: the ui-app-emit or a new email-emit step generates .email.tsx template stubs from TypeSpec entity contracts, the same way it generates page stubs.
register.person_centric_fit: Templates can be authored with person-centric language and dynamic data (patient name, appointment detail, care instructions). Components reusable across patient-app emails and business-side alerts with the same design system.

register.name: Postal (self-hosted SMTP relay + HTTP API)
register.what: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
register.taxonomy: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
register.stack_fit: No Node SDK needed - Postal exposes HTTP API; wrap in a thin NestJS provider (PostalProvider implements EmailProvider interface) using `fetch` or axios. Zarf-packageable as a container. Works alongside CuraOS's Postgres CNPG (Postal uses MySQL internally - no conflict, separate service).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Local: Postal self-hosted container. BYO-3rd-party: swap PostalProvider for ResendProvider/SES/Mailgun - same NestJS interface. Full dual compliance.
register.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service EmailProvider port. Codegen emits PostalProvider class from a provider config schema in TypeSpec.
register.person_centric_fit: Delivery infrastructure only - person-facing value is reliable, tracked email delivery. Bounce/complaint suppression protects patient email from accidental spam-folder landing.

register.name: listmonk (self-hosted newsletter + transactional email)
register.what: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
register.taxonomy: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
register.stack_fit: AGPL service boundary: CuraOS calls listmonk's REST API from notify-core-service, never imports code. Postgres-native (shares CNPG PostgreSQL cluster or separate instance). Zarf-packageable. No npm SDK but HTTP client wrapper is trivial - 50 lines in a NestJS ListmonkProvider.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only for listmonk itself; but it relays via any SMTP/provider (Postal, SES, Mailgun). Covers local self-host path; BYO-3rd-party is the SMTP relay config.
register.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service ListmonkProvider and list-seeding scripts. TypeSpec contract emits campaign trigger events consumed by listmonk webhook handler.
register.person_centric_fit: Patient/customer opt-in/out management per list per person. Double opt-in flows protect patient autonomy. Subscription preference portal can be surfaced in patient-app as communication preferences. Unsubscribe is first-class, not an afterthought.

register.name: ntfy (self-hosted push notification server)
register.what: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
register.taxonomy: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
register.stack_fit: Trivial HTTP integration from NestJS: POST to ntfy topic = push sent. No SDK required; if desired, a 20-line NestJS NtfyProvider wraps it. Works as Novu provider adapter so Novu orchestrates the channel while ntfy handles delivery. Zarf-packageable (single Go binary container).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: ntfy container. BYO-3rd-party: configure Novu's FCM/APNs provider instead - same NestJS interface. iOS relay mode in ntfy itself bridges to Apple APNs without requiring a self-hosted APNs cert.
register.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service PushProvider port. Can also be a Novu custom provider definition.
register.person_centric_fit: Person installs ntfy app and subscribes to their patient-specific topic (scoped by tenant+patient ID with ACL). Push reaches them without CuraOS holding their FCM token. Topic-level unsubscribe is person-controlled.

register.name: Element/Matrix (Synapse + matrix-js-sdk)
register.what: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
register.taxonomy: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
register.stack_fit: matrix-js-sdk imports into NestJS for bot/bridge operations (programmatic message send from clinical workflows). Element Web embeds in CuraOS React frontend via iframe or as a standalone app. Significant ops footprint (Synapse + Postgres + media server). Not Zarf-trivial but Helm chart exists.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Synapse on CuraOS K8s. BYO-3rd-party: Matrix.org public homeserver or Element Cloud for teams that don't need on-prem. Federation means both options interoperate.
register.generator_first_target: service: a new secure-messaging-service wraps matrix-js-sdk for server-side operations (room creation, bot messages for system notifications). UI: Element Web or a matrix-react-sdk embedding in patient-app.
register.person_centric_fit: Patient owns their Matrix account (portable across homeservers). E2E encryption means even CuraOS cannot read messages at rest. Person-controlled key verification. Care team visible to patient as participants, not hidden from them.

register.name: Dittofeed (open-source lifecycle messaging)
register.what: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
register.taxonomy: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
register.stack_fit: TypeScript-native, Node.js SDK drops into NestJS as a service client. However, the self-host stack adds ClickHouse + Temporal on top of Postgres - significant infra expansion. For CuraOS v1 scope this is likely deferred. REST API usable without SDK.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose / Helm (lite mode: Postgres only, no ClickHouse). BYO-3rd-party: Dittofeed Cloud. Both paths use same SDK.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@dittofeed/sdk-node): notify-core-service tracks events to Dittofeed for journey triggers. TypeSpec events map to Dittofeed track() calls in service-seed emit.
register.person_centric_fit: Journey builder models patient/user lifecycle (onboarding, re-engagement, care adherence). Person-level opt-out and preferences. Segment by clinical attributes (diagnosis group, care plan status) for targeted communications.

register.name: jsx-email (Bun-native react-email alternative)
register.what: React-compatible JSX email rendering library forked from react-email with explicit Bun and edge runtime compatibility, plugin architecture, first-class Tailwind-to-inline-styles, and built-in Markdown rendering. Drop-in replacement for @react-email/render where bun compile compatibility matters. Same use-case as react-email but resolves the bun compile binary issue.
register.taxonomy: React-compatible JSX email rendering library forked from react-email with explicit Bun and edge runtime compatibility, plugin architecture, first-class Tailwind-to-inline-styles, and built-in Markdown rendering. Drop-in replacement for @react-email/render where bun compile compatibility matters. Same use-case as react-email but resolves the bun compile binary issue.
register.stack_fit: Drop-in for @react-email/render. Works with Bun compile (the blocker react-email has). NestJS integration identical. Tailwind-to-inline solves email client compatibility without extra build step.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: N/A - rendering library, provider-agnostic.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target: email-emit step in codegen mold generates .email.tsx stubs using jsx-email component primitives if Bun compile path is needed.
register.person_centric_fit: Same as react-email: JSX components authored with person-centric content, shared with ui-kit design tokens.

register.name: pocket-id
register.what: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
register.taxonomy: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
register.stack_fit: NestJS consumes JWKS + standard OIDC tokens via @nestjs/passport + passport-jwt (no wrapper needed). Pocket-id REST API for user/group management. LDAP sync available. No NestJS-specific SDK but standard OIDC protocol = zero wrapper friction.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (no BYO-3rd-party option -- no cloud variant). Meets the local-first charter; BYO-3rd-party would use Keycloak or Zitadel path instead.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen emitter should emit JWKS-aware JWT guard decorator + SMART scope decorators from TypeSpec contract rather than per-service hand-coded guards
register.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first auth is the best person-centric auth UX (no password, phishing-resistant). Persons authenticate once via Face ID/Touch ID; identity flows downstream to patient-app, business apps, clinician-app via OIDC. Aligns with person-owns-their-identity directive.

register.name: Keycloak
register.what: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
register.taxonomy: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
register.stack_fit: nest-keycloak-connect npm adapter (guards, decorators, resource/scope protection). Multi-tenant via realm-per-tenant or per-org realm resolver. JWKS endpoint for standard JWT validation. Wrapper needed for multi-tenant realm routing but documented pattern exists.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm chart, K8s operator) AND can be used as BYO-3rd-party (managed Keycloak from Red Hat, cloud IAM providers). Satisfies [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] dual requirement.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emitter should generate Keycloak-compatible resource+scope declarations from TypeSpec contract ops, not hand-code resource_access claims per service
register.person_centric_fit: Passkey/FIDO2 native (26.4+), social login, self-service account console for persons to manage their credentials, MFA. WebAuthn conditional UI now in default login forms. Directly supports person-owns-their-auth-devices.

register.name: Zitadel
register.what: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
register.taxonomy: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
register.stack_fit: OIDC standard tokens consumed by NestJS via passport-jwt/JWKS. gRPC management API has Go/TypeScript SDKs. TypeScript Actions for lifecycle hooks. No native NestJS module but standard OIDC = minimal wrapper. Multi-org maps cleanly to CuraOS tenant model.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm, K8s) + managed Zitadel Cloud. Satisfies dual requirement -- but AGPL means if CuraOS modifies Zitadel source and exposes as SaaS, disclosure required.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emit Zitadel resource registration API calls from TypeSpec contract during codegen scaffold
register.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first, self-service account management, GDPR-ready event log. Multi-org means each patient/customer org can have their own IdP branding/settings.

register.name: @simplewebauthn/server
register.what: TypeScript-first WebAuthn/FIDO2/passkey library for Node.js. Handles registration ceremony (attestation parsing, CBOR decoding) and authentication ceremony (assertion verification, signature check) entirely in-process. Fills: passkey WebAuthn in identity-service for non-pocket-id deployments or local dev. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authn primitive).
register.taxonomy: TypeScript-first WebAuthn/FIDO2/passkey library for Node.js. Handles registration ceremony (attestation parsing, CBOR decoding) and authentication ceremony (assertion verification, signature check) entirely in-process. Fills: passkey WebAuthn in identity-service for non-pocket-id deployments or local dev. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authn primitive).
register.stack_fit: npm-import into identity-service. Drizzle schema for passkey credential table documented. NestJS + PostgreSQL demo repo exists. Zero wrapper needed -- pure TypeScript, Bun-compatible. Plug into existing identity-service NestJS guards.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host always (in-process library, no external service). Complements pocket-id (which also uses FIDO2 but as a standalone IdP).
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen should emit passkey registration/authentication endpoint stubs + Drizzle credential table schema as part of identity-service scaffold
register.person_centric_fit: Enables passkey-first authentication (Touch ID, Face ID, hardware keys) as the primary person login method -- no password, phishing-resistant, best-in-class UX.

register.name: Cerbos
register.what: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
register.taxonomy: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
register.stack_fit: cerbos npm SDK + nestjs-cerbos interceptor pattern. NestJS guard calls Cerbos PDP over gRPC with principal attributes + resource attributes; PDP evaluates YAML policy. ABAC lets policies use tenant context, clinical role, HIPAA consent flags. No wrapper beyond the SDK guard pattern (already documented).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host PDP (open-source, Docker/Helm). Hub (policy management UI) is SaaS-optional; air-gap deployments use local bundle endpoint. Meets dual requirement: self-host PDP always available, Hub as optional BYO-cloud control plane.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec contract resource ops should generate Cerbos resource definitions + policy scaffolds (derived resource.yaml per service), injected into service scaffold by ui-app-emit / emitServiceLive
register.person_centric_fit: Centralizes who-can-see-what logic, enabling privacy-by-policy (clinician only sees consented patient records, person only sees their own data, HIPAA field-level rules as policy YAML). Simplifies adding consent-aware access rules without touching every service.

register.name: OpenFGA
register.what: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
register.taxonomy: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
register.stack_fit: @openfga/sdk npm + @rxap/nest-openfga module. NestJS guard delegates to OpenFGA check API. Tuples stored in Postgres (which CNPG already manages). Fits well for document/care-plan/consent record ownership. ABAC support is newer and less mature than ReBAC.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Docker/Helm) + FGA.dev managed cloud. Satisfies dual requirement.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec resource types should emit OpenFGA type definitions; SDK emit should generate NestJS FGA guard + tuple write helpers per resource type
register.person_centric_fit: ReBAC naturally encodes person-owns-their-record, patient-can-read-their-own-clinical-document, org-member-can-access-shared-resource. Consent as a relationship tuple is a clean model.

register.name: SpiceDB
register.what: Most Zanzibar-faithful open-source permissions database. Zed schema language, caveated relationships (ABAC+ReBAC hybrid), Watch API for cache invalidation, strong consistency. Fills: fine-grained permissions with ABAC caveats if Zanzibar fidelity is required. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (Zanzibar tier).
register.taxonomy: Most Zanzibar-faithful open-source permissions database. Zed schema language, caveated relationships (ABAC+ReBAC hybrid), Watch API for cache invalidation, strong consistency. Fills: fine-grained permissions with ABAC caveats if Zanzibar fidelity is required. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (Zanzibar tier).
register.stack_fit: authzed-node npm (gRPC client). No native NestJS module -- requires manual guard wiring. Zed schema language has TypeScript type generator tools (community). More operational complexity than Cerbos (schema migrations, consistency tokens).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Docker/Helm) + Authzed cloud managed. Satisfies dual requirement.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec types could emit Zed schema fragments; SDK emitter generates NestJS SpiceDB guard + WriteRelationships helpers
register.person_centric_fit: Caveated relationships can encode consent state as an attribute directly in the authorization tuple -- patient consent revocation immediately invalidates access without policy rebuild.

register.name: better-auth
register.what: TypeScript-first in-process auth framework. Plugin-based: passkey, OIDC provider (now OAuth 2.1 provider in v1.5+), multi-tenant organizations, Drizzle adapter, NestJS integration documented. Could replace or complement identity-service session/token layer. Fills: lightweight embedded auth for personal-* services or dev environment. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (lightweight embedded tier).
register.taxonomy: TypeScript-first in-process auth framework. Plugin-based: passkey, OIDC provider (now OAuth 2.1 provider in v1.5+), multi-tenant organizations, Drizzle adapter, NestJS integration documented. Could replace or complement identity-service session/token layer. Fills: lightweight embedded auth for personal-* services or dev environment. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (lightweight embedded tier).
register.stack_fit: Native Drizzle adapter (import from 'better-auth/adapters/drizzle'). NestJS integration: disable NestJS body parser, mount better-auth handler. Passkey plugin + OAuth 2.1 provider plugin cover authn + IdP needs in one npm package. Bun-compatible (framework-agnostic design).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host always (in-process). No cloud variant -- all data stays in your Postgres via Drizzle. Perfect for air-gap.
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen scaffold for identity-service should emit better-auth initialization + Drizzle schema migration + NestJS auth module wiring as optional embedded-auth mode
register.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first (pre-auth registration in 1.6), magic-link, social login all available. OAuth 2.1 provider means CuraOS can issue its own tokens to patient-app and business apps without a separate IdP service for simpler deployments.

register.name: Casbin (node-casbin + nest-authz + casbin-drizzle-adapter)
register.what: In-process policy enforcement library (ACL/RBAC/ABAC via policy model + adapter pattern). nest-authz provides NestJS guard/decorator. casbin-drizzle-adapter loads/saves policies from Drizzle-managed tables. Fills: lightweight in-process authz without a sidecar if Cerbos is overkill. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (in-process authz).
register.taxonomy: In-process policy enforcement library (ACL/RBAC/ABAC via policy model + adapter pattern). nest-authz provides NestJS guard/decorator. casbin-drizzle-adapter loads/saves policies from Drizzle-managed tables. Fills: lightweight in-process authz without a sidecar if Cerbos is overkill. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (in-process authz).
register.stack_fit: npm-import into any NestJS service. casbin-drizzle-adapter lets policy table live in same Postgres schema. No sidecar. However, no centralized policy management across services -- each service loads its own policy (vs Cerbos single PDP).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host always (in-process). Air-gap friendly.
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- could emit Casbin policy model + adapter init per service, but centralized Cerbos PDP is a better generator target
register.person_centric_fit: Neutral -- policies can encode person-owns-their-data rules but no built-in consent-awareness or clinical-role semantics; those must be hand-coded in policy model.

register.name: rrule-temporal
register.what: RFC-5545 + RFC-7529 recurrence rule expansion returning Temporal.ZonedDateTime; backend recurrence engine for CuraOS calendar_event.rrule storage and slot expansion. Fills gap: recurring event expansion in calendar-core-service and scheduling-service (taxonomy: calendar.recurring-events). Direct successor to unmaintained rrule.js, Temporal-native, tz-correct by construction.
register.taxonomy: RFC-5545 + RFC-7529 recurrence rule expansion returning Temporal.ZonedDateTime; backend recurrence engine for CuraOS calendar_event.rrule storage and slot expansion. Fills gap: recurring event expansion in calendar-core-service and scheduling-service (taxonomy: calendar.recurring-events). Direct successor to unmaintained rrule.js, Temporal-native, tz-correct by construction.
register.stack_fit: Drop-in NestJS import, no wrapper needed. Works with Bun runtime. Requires @js-temporal/polyfill on Node < Temporal-native; Node 22 may have partial native support. Pair with Luxon (already in calendar-core) for display formatting. Store RRULE strings in Drizzle text column, expand at read time.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg - @curaos/calendar-sdk (expand rrule strings in SDK layer so all consumers get tz-correct occurrences without re-implementing expansion)
register.person_centric_fit: Powers patient-facing 'my appointments' recurring view, clinician recurring schedule blocks, and hr leave accrual recurrence. Invisible infrastructure; surfaces as correct dates on person-app calendar.

register.name: rrule (jkbrzt/rrule)
register.what: De-facto JS recurrence rule library, RFC-5545 RRULE parsing and date expansion. Still widely used (1.7M weekly downloads) but last published 3 years ago (v2.8.1). Open issues accumulating since 2025 with no maintainer responses. Reference for migration path - existing codebases using it should plan migration to rrule-temporal.
register.taxonomy: De-facto JS recurrence rule library, RFC-5545 RRULE parsing and date expansion. Still widely used (1.7M weekly downloads) but last published 3 years ago (v2.8.1). Open issues accumulating since 2025 with no maintainer responses. Reference for migration path - existing codebases using it should plan migration to rrule-temporal.
register.stack_fit: Works with Bun/NestJS. No resource changes needed. But timezone handling has known bugs and Date-based (not Temporal). The CuraOS calendar-core schema already uses Luxon for IANA tz - rrule.js Date objects create an impedance mismatch.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg
register.person_centric_fit: Would serve same use cases as rrule-temporal but with tz bugs that affect cross-timezone patient scheduling.

register.name: react-big-calendar
register.what: GCal/Outlook-style React calendar component: month/week/day/agenda views, drag-drop, multi-date-library adapters, events management. Fills the CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard UI archetype gap in @curaos/ui (taxonomy: ui-kit-archetypes). Person-facing calendar views (patient-app my-appointments, clinician daily schedule) and management calendar (scheduling admin, CRM activity calendar).
register.taxonomy: GCal/Outlook-style React calendar component: month/week/day/agenda views, drag-drop, multi-date-library adapters, events management. Fills the CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard UI archetype gap in @curaos/ui (taxonomy: ui-kit-archetypes). Person-facing calendar views (patient-app my-appointments, clinician daily schedule) and management calendar (scheduling admin, CRM activity calendar).
register.stack_fit: React-native, no wrapper needed. TypeScript types via @types/react-big-calendar (v1.16.3). Adapts to date-fns or Luxon (already installed). Drop into @curaos/ui as the CalendarBoard primitive - generators can emit the CalendarBoard archetype wired to calendar-sdk hooks. No resource view (multi-provider/room) natively.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: ui-kit (@curaos/ui CalendarBoard component wrapping react-big-calendar, wired to calendar-sdk event feed; ui-app-emit emits CalendarBoard archetype for calendar-connected entities)
register.person_centric_fit: Patient-app: my-appointments calendar, clinician-app: daily/weekly schedule. CRM: activity timeline calendar view. Personal-calendar app: full calendar view. Strong person-centric fit for all journey surfaces.

register.name: FullCalendar (standard, MIT plugins)
register.what: Mature multi-framework calendar: month/week/day/agenda/timegrid views, drag-drop, recurring events via rrule plugin, TS support. Standard plugins are MIT. Premium plugins (Timeline/Resource) are commercial ($480/dev). For CuraOS: standard tier covers CalendarBoard archetype and most scheduling views. Resource timeline (multi-provider, multi-room) needs premium.
register.taxonomy: Mature multi-framework calendar: month/week/day/agenda/timegrid views, drag-drop, recurring events via rrule plugin, TS support. Standard plugins are MIT. Premium plugins (Timeline/Resource) are commercial ($480/dev). For CuraOS: standard tier covers CalendarBoard archetype and most scheduling views. Resource timeline (multi-provider, multi-room) needs premium.
register.stack_fit: @fullcalendar/react wraps core cleanly. TS types built-in. Heavier bundle than react-big-calendar or schedule-x. Premium resource views require per-developer license + OEM license if distributed to tenants. Standard tier is the safer adoption scope.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: ui-kit (@curaos/ui SchedulingBoard component for the management multi-resource view; CalendarBoard reuses react-big-calendar for person surface)
register.person_centric_fit: Strong for clinician-app schedule views (timegrid week is the standard clinical scheduling view). Standard tier covers person-facing appointment list/calendar. Resource views (multi-room booking for management surface) need premium.

register.name: schedule-x
register.what: Modern lightweight event calendar: day/week/month views, drag-drop, dark mode, RTL, i18n, React/Vue/Svelte. MIT for base calendar. Resource scheduler is a premium (paid per-project) feature. ~12k weekly downloads, v4.1.0. Alternative to react-big-calendar with better i18n/RTL baseline and lighter bundle.
register.taxonomy: Modern lightweight event calendar: day/week/month views, drag-drop, dark mode, RTL, i18n, React/Vue/Svelte. MIT for base calendar. Resource scheduler is a premium (paid per-project) feature. ~12k weekly downloads, v4.1.0. Alternative to react-big-calendar with better i18n/RTL baseline and lighter bundle.
register.stack_fit: React package @schedule-x/react. TypeScript first. Smaller bundle than FullCalendar. Temporal-polyfill used internally. Resource scheduler requires separate premium license per project.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: ui-kit (alternative CalendarBoard, especially for RTL/i18n-heavy deployments)
register.person_centric_fit: Person-facing calendar views with RTL and i18n are a fit for CuraOS's localization requirements. Base tier adequate for personal-calendar-service UI.

register.name: Cal.com (AGPL v4.9.3 self-hosted)
register.what: Full scheduling platform: booking pages, availability rules, calendar sync (Google/Outlook), payment, webhooks, multi-user teams. Could serve as the BYO-3rd-party scheduling provider for tenants who want a managed booking page. Fills: external booking link for CuraOS patient-facing appointment self-scheduling (taxonomy: scheduling.patient-self-schedule). AGPL v4.9.3 is the last open-source version; future releases closed-source.
register.taxonomy: Full scheduling platform: booking pages, availability rules, calendar sync (Google/Outlook), payment, webhooks, multi-user teams. Could serve as the BYO-3rd-party scheduling provider for tenants who want a managed booking page. Fills: external booking link for CuraOS patient-facing appointment self-scheduling (taxonomy: scheduling.patient-self-schedule). AGPL v4.9.3 is the last open-source version; future releases closed-source.
register.license: AGPL-3.0 (v4.9.3 last open version; future releases proprietary)
register.stack_fit: Cal.com is a Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL monolith - not NestJS-native. Integration path: deploy Cal.com v4.9.3 as a sidecar service (per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] BYO mode), integrate via webhooks into CuraOS Kafka/Redpanda event stream. @calcom/atoms and @calcom/embed-react npm packages call api.cal.com (NOT self-hosted) - not viable for self-hosted-first. Webhook-only integration is the clean path.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: BYO-3rd-party (tenant brings their Cal.com/Cal.diy instance; CuraOS holds event reference via webhook)
register.generator_first_target: api-adapter (webhook receiver in scheduling-service: cal.com booking.created -> scheduling.appointment.booked event on Kafka)
register.person_centric_fit: High for patient self-scheduling (person books their own appointment on a beautiful booking page). Management surface needs CuraOS scheduling-service to hold ground truth. Dual-surface: Cal.com handles the booking UX, CuraOS owns the appointment entity.

register.name: PostgreSQL tstzrange + EXCLUDE constraint (native platform feature)
register.what: DB-level double-booking prevention using PostgreSQL exclusion constraints on tstzrange. Eliminates the need for an application-level scheduling engine for conflict detection. Fills: resource conflict guard in scheduling-service and business-scheduling-service (any multi-resource booking). Example: EXCLUDE USING gist (resource_id WITH =, tstzrange(start_at, end_at) WITH &&) prevents overlapping bookings at the DB layer.
register.taxonomy: DB-level double-booking prevention using PostgreSQL exclusion constraints on tstzrange. Eliminates the need for an application-level scheduling engine for conflict detection. Fills: resource conflict guard in scheduling-service and business-scheduling-service (any multi-resource booking). Example: EXCLUDE USING gist (resource_id WITH =, tstzrange(start_at, end_at) WITH &&) prevents overlapping bookings at the DB layer.
register.stack_fit: Drizzle ORM supports custom SQL for exclusion constraints via sql`` template. CNPG runs standard PostgreSQL with btree_gist extension (required for tstzrange exclusion). Zero new npm deps. Emit the exclusion constraint from the Drizzle schema generator.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (drizzle schema emitter adds tstzrange exclusion constraint for any entity with resource_id + time range fields)
register.person_centric_fit: Invisible to person - prevents the bad experience of double-booked appointments. Management surface: booking conflict errors surface as clear 409 responses.

register.name: Luxon (already installed)
register.what: Timezone-aware date manipulation library, already in use in calendar-core-service (defaultTzid IANA resolution). No new dep. For backend scheduling logic: slot generation, duration arithmetic, IANA timezone conversion, DST-safe period calculations. Fills: tz-correct slot computation in scheduling-service.
register.taxonomy: Timezone-aware date manipulation library, already in use in calendar-core-service (defaultTzid IANA resolution). No new dep. For backend scheduling logic: slot generation, duration arithmetic, IANA timezone conversion, DST-safe period calculations. Fills: tz-correct slot computation in scheduling-service.
register.stack_fit: Already installed in calendar-core. Immutable, IANA tz via Intl API, Bun-compatible. Use for slot generation arithmetic in scheduling-service. Pair with rrule-temporal (which returns Temporal.ZonedDateTime) via a thin adapter or migrate slot logic to Temporal directly.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (already wired into calendar-core; extend to scheduling-sdk slot generation)
register.person_centric_fit: Powers correct appointment times in the person's local timezone on patient-app.

register.name: Cal.diy (MIT)
register.what: Personal booking page, stripped-down MIT fork of Cal.com. Single-user booking page concept: no team features, no app store, no multi-tenant. MIT licensed. Officially 'personal and non-production use' per Cal.com team. Could provide reference patterns for booking page UI and availability logic.
register.taxonomy: Personal booking page, stripped-down MIT fork of Cal.com. Single-user booking page concept: no team features, no app store, no multi-tenant. MIT licensed. Officially 'personal and non-production use' per Cal.com team. Could provide reference patterns for booking page UI and availability logic.
register.stack_fit: Not integrable as a library. Next.js app. Patterns only. The availability engine and booking form patterns are reference-grade for CuraOS's scheduling-service slot API design.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: BYO-3rd-party
register.generator_first_target: reference-only
register.person_centric_fit: Reference for simple patient self-booking UX patterns.

register.name: Ottehr scheduling engine
register.what: Open source, production EHR (MIT-style) with TypeScript appointment scheduling, multi-location, provider/group availability, telemedicine vs in-person. Reference for healthcare-specific scheduling data model: appointment types, provider availability windows, location constraints, patient self-booking flow.
register.taxonomy: Open source, production EHR (MIT-style) with TypeScript appointment scheduling, multi-location, provider/group availability, telemedicine vs in-person. Reference for healthcare-specific scheduling data model: appointment types, provider availability windows, location constraints, patient self-booking flow.
register.stack_fit: Tightly coupled to Oystehr FHIR platform (Zambdas serverless functions, Fhir client). Not extractable as a standalone npm SDK. Port-adapt the data model and availability algorithm patterns into CuraOS scheduling-service.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
register.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (scheduling-service.tsp: appointment-type, availability-window, slot, booking-request, confirmation - port-adapt Ottehr's data model)
register.person_centric_fit: Excellent reference for the patient self-scheduling journey (person picks type, location, provider, time slot). Management surface: provider availability config, multi-location scheduling admin.

register.name: next-runtime-env
register.what: Runtime injection of NEXT_PUBLIC_* and next.config-derived values without rebuild. Adds a PublicEnvScript component to root layout that reads process.env per-request (SSR), populates window.__ENV on client. Directly fixes the #840/ADR-0220 blocker: allowedOrigins, CSP nonces, and any NEXT_PUBLIC_ value become runtime-injectable without touching the build. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator gap - runtime config pattern.
register.taxonomy: Runtime injection of NEXT_PUBLIC_* and next.config-derived values without rebuild. Adds a PublicEnvScript component to root layout that reads process.env per-request (SSR), populates window.__ENV on client. Directly fixes the #840/ADR-0220 blocker: allowedOrigins, CSP nonces, and any NEXT_PUBLIC_ value become runtime-injectable without touching the build. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator gap - runtime config pattern.
register.stack_fit: Drop-in for Next.js App Router. One PublicEnvScript in root layout.tsx, env() helper replaces process.env on client. Generator (ui-app-emit) emits it once in the layout template - zero per-route changes. No Bun/NestJS/Drizzle wrapper needed.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (reads from container env); no 3rd-party dependency
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (root layout.tsx template)
register.person_centric_fit: Invisible to end users; enables correct per-tenant config injection (tenant-specific API base URLs, CSP) which directly supports multi-tenant person-centric surfaces

register.name: TanStack Start (full fleet migration)
register.what: Replace Next.js with TanStack Start across all 24 web apps. Addresses: runtime env (VITE_ vars still bake, but createServerFn escape hatch is cleaner); self-host/air-gap (bun build --compile = 132 MB alpine image vs 2-3 GB Next.js standalone); SSR/streaming (loaders + Suspense, 5.5x throughput benchmark vs Next.js); generator fit (file-based routes + routeTree.gen.ts is more emitter-friendly than App Router page.tsx conventions). Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator target replacement.
register.taxonomy: Replace Next.js with TanStack Start across all 24 web apps. Addresses: runtime env (VITE_ vars still bake, but createServerFn escape hatch is cleaner); self-host/air-gap (bun build --compile = 132 MB alpine image vs 2-3 GB Next.js standalone); SSR/streaming (loaders + Suspense, 5.5x throughput benchmark vs Next.js); generator fit (file-based routes + routeTree.gen.ts is more emitter-friendly than App Router page.tsx conventions). Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator target replacement.
register.stack_fit: Native Bun support (bun.sh official guide). Vite-based build fits Turborepo. No Nx official generator yet (GitHub discussion #34273 open as of March 2026 - community templates only). TanStack Query + Drizzle patterns well-documented. shadcn/ui official support. Auth cookie + NestJS JWT bridge documented. No wrapper needed for Bun runtime.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host first - Nitro or custom Bun server; no Vercel dependency. Bun compile to single binary for air-gap/Zarf. BYO hosting fully supported.
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit emitter - would emit src/routes/*.tsx files; Vite plugin auto-generates routeTree.gen.ts. More emitter-friendly than Next.js App Router because route discovery is explicit and generated file is stable.
register.person_centric_fit: File-based routing with beforeLoad auth guards and loaders maps cleanly to person-centric journey flows (patient app, personal-* apps). RSC support (April 2026) enables server-rendered patient data without client-side waterfall.

register.name: TanStack Start (selective - new apps only)
register.what: Use TanStack Start for net-new apps (not migrating the existing 24) while keeping the current Next.js fleet on next-runtime-env fix. Allows generator (ui-app-emit) to emit two targets: Next.js for existing apps, TanStack Start for greenfield. Lets the ecosystem/Nx generator situation mature before committing the fleet. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit codegen-emitter-target.
register.taxonomy: Use TanStack Start for net-new apps (not migrating the existing 24) while keeping the current Next.js fleet on next-runtime-env fix. Allows generator (ui-app-emit) to emit two targets: Next.js for existing apps, TanStack Start for greenfield. Lets the ecosystem/Nx generator situation mature before committing the fleet. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit codegen-emitter-target.
register.stack_fit: Same Bun + Turborepo fit. ui-app-emit would need a --framework flag (nextjs | tanstack-start). Two emitter templates to maintain in tools/codegen, but no migration burden on existing apps.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host first, same as full migration
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (add tanstack-start emitter target alongside nextjs target)
register.person_centric_fit: Same as full migration. Allows person-centric new surfaces (e.g. patient-app v2, personal-health-app) to be built on TanStack Start with cleaner data loading patterns.

register.name: TanStack Router (SPA mode, no Start)
register.what: Use TanStack Router standalone (no TanStack Start SSR layer) for apps that do not need SSR - pure client-side SPAs served as static files. Runtime env is trivially solved: inject window.__ENV from a server-side config endpoint at container startup, or use a sidecar nginx that rewrites a config.js file. No build-time baking problem because there is no build-time SSR. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit for non-SSR apps.
register.taxonomy: Use TanStack Router standalone (no TanStack Start SSR layer) for apps that do not need SSR - pure client-side SPAs served as static files. Runtime env is trivially solved: inject window.__ENV from a server-side config endpoint at container startup, or use a sidecar nginx that rewrites a config.js file. No build-time baking problem because there is no build-time SSR. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit for non-SSR apps.
register.stack_fit: Excellent. Vite + Bun build. routeTree.gen.ts codegen. Turborepo-friendly. No SSR layer = simpler Helm/Zarf packaging (just a static asset bundle behind nginx). NestJS JWT auth via API calls, no cookie proxy complexity.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Static assets self-hosted (nginx/CDN); no managed dependency
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (SPA variant emitter - simpler than SSR, no server entry)
register.person_centric_fit: Suitable for dashboard/admin apps where SEO is not a concern and interactivity is high. Not suitable for public-facing patient-facing pages where SSR matters for perceived performance.

register.name: Next.js with #840 fix (stay-nextjs path)
register.what: Keep all 24 apps on Next.js standalone; fix the NEXT_PUBLIC bake problem via next-runtime-env + generator-native PublicEnvScript in ui-app-emit root layout template; fix allowedOrigins/CSP by reading from process.env in a custom Next.js server wrapper (instrumentation.ts or server.js) that patches next.config at startup. This is the direct fix for the blocker that triggered the decision. No framework migration risk. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator template fix.
register.taxonomy: Keep all 24 apps on Next.js standalone; fix the NEXT_PUBLIC bake problem via next-runtime-env + generator-native PublicEnvScript in ui-app-emit root layout template; fix allowedOrigins/CSP by reading from process.env in a custom Next.js server wrapper (instrumentation.ts or server.js) that patches next.config at startup. This is the direct fix for the blocker that triggered the decision. No framework migration risk. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator template fix.
register.stack_fit: Zero friction - existing 24 apps unchanged except generator template update. ui-app-emit adds PublicEnvScript to root layout.tsx template. allowedOrigins fix: move to runtime-read in a custom server.js that next() wraps. Bun standalone: Next.js standalone is NOT a single binary (requires node_modules dir); image size remains 2-3 GB unless manually slimmed.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (standalone output); no Vercel required. Existing Helm/Zarf packaging unchanged.
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (root layout.tsx template + custom server.js template)
register.person_centric_fit: No change to person-centric surfaces. Existing App Router RSC pattern already in use for patient/personal apps.

register.name: Vite (standalone, framework-agnostic)
register.what: Use Vite directly (not via TanStack Start wrapper) as the build tool for custom SSR or SPA apps. TanStack Router plugs in via @tanstack/router-vite-plugin. Vite handles HMR, code-splitting, and asset bundling. Runtime env: no NEXT_PUBLIC analog - server reads process.env, passes to client however the app chooses. Reference point for understanding what TanStack Start adds on top. Taxonomy: build tooling for ui-app-emit.
register.taxonomy: Use Vite directly (not via TanStack Start wrapper) as the build tool for custom SSR or SPA apps. TanStack Router plugs in via @tanstack/router-vite-plugin. Vite handles HMR, code-splitting, and asset bundling. Runtime env: no NEXT_PUBLIC analog - server reads process.env, passes to client however the app chooses. Reference point for understanding what TanStack Start adds on top. Taxonomy: build tooling for ui-app-emit.
register.stack_fit: Excellent Bun compatibility. Turborepo pipeline integration well-documented. No Nx-specific plugin needed. Custom SSR entry point gives full control over env injection.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted build; no managed service
register.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (vite.config.ts template)
register.person_centric_fit: Neutral build tool; app architecture determines person-centric fit

adopt_now.name: shadcn/ui + Radix UI primitives
adopt_now.what: Copy-paste component distribution built on Radix headless primitives + Tailwind. Already partially adopted in @curaos/ui (10+ Radix packages installed). Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes`: provides battle-tested accessible primitives (Dialog, Select, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Command/cmdk, Combobox, DatePicker, etc.) that become the foundation for all 16 missing archetypes. Components are copied into the repo - no external runtime dependency.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Copy-paste component distribution built on Radix headless primitives + Tailwind. Already partially adopted in @curaos/ui (10+ Radix packages installed). Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes`: provides battle-tested accessible primitives (Dialog, Select, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Command/cmdk, Combobox, DatePicker, etc.) that become the foundation for all 16 missing archetypes. Components are copied into the repo - no external runtime dependency.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Native fit: Tailwind already in stack, Radix already installed, React 19, TypeScript 5. ui-app-emit already emits shadcn-style components. Zero wrapper needed.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (code copied into repo = zero external runtime). No 3rd-party SaaS. Fully air-gap safe.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-kit package (@curaos/ui) - already the integration point; shadcn CLI pulls components into src/components/
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Command palette (cmdk), accessible dialogs, drawers, and date pickers are person-facing UX primitives used in patient-app, personal-* apps, and clinician surfaces equally. RTL-capable via Radix internals.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Three gap packages to add for missing archetypes: cmdk (Command/command-palette), vaul (Drawer), react-day-picker + date-fns (DatePicker). All MIT. Effort S as claimed. Recommend adding Base UI as the primitive layer for any NET-NEW compone
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Already adopted - ponytail ladder rung 2 applies. The ui-kit package at curaos/frontend/packages/ui-kit already has 11 @radix-ui/* packages installed and wraps them in shadcn-style CVA+Radix components (button.tsx comment explicitly says "shadcn-style cva + Radix Slot"). cmdk, vaul (drawer), react-day-picker are NOT yet in package.json - those are the gap packages covering the missing archetypes (Command palette, Drawer, DatePicker). Adding them is extending the already-adopted pattern, not a new dependency class.

adopt_now.name: TanStack Table v9 + TanStack Virtual
adopt_now.what: Headless table/grid engine (sorting, filtering, grouping, pagination, column pinning, row selection, server-side) + row virtualization. Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` DataTable and `platform-reporting-builder` grids. v9 (beta as of 2026) uses prototype-chain row/column APIs - 90% less memory vs v8, handles 10-16M rows before hitting 4 GB. TanStack Virtual pairs for virtualized rendering of those rows.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Headless table/grid engine (sorting, filtering, grouping, pagination, column pinning, row selection, server-side) + row virtualization. Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` DataTable and `platform-reporting-builder` grids. v9 (beta as of 2026) uses prototype-chain row/column APIs - 90% less memory vs v8, handles 10-16M rows before hitting 4 GB. TanStack Virtual pairs for virtualized rendering of those rows.
adopt_now.stack_fit: React 19 compatible. No wrapper needed. Already aligns with existing data-table.tsx in ui-kit. TanStack Query v5 (server state) pairs naturally with Table for server-side fetch patterns used across NestJS APIs.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (npm package, no SaaS). Air-gap safe.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (codegen emitter) - archetype hint 'data-grid' maps to TanStack Table wrapper in @curaos/ui
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Person-facing apps (patient-app, personal-finance) need paginated/virtualized lists of clinical results, invoices, and transactions. Same table primitives serve both management grid and person timeline list.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Verdict is confirmed-trial not confirmed-adopt for three reasons: (1) v8 has a confirmed React 19 Compiler incompatibility with no clean fix - 'use no memo' is acceptable short-term but is a smell for a stack that will adopt the Compiler wi
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Yes, and it already exists. The codebase has a hand-rolled DataTable at curaos/frontend/packages/ui-kit/src/components/data-table.tsx (147 lines, zero deps). It covers: sortable columns, row selection (controlled + uncontrolled), aria-sort, numeric alignment, generic over row type. It does NOT cover: pagination, server-side fetch, column pinning, grouping, virtualization of large row counts. For the 24 web apps in current scope the existing component likely covers 80% of tables. The gap cases are: (a) platform-reporting-builder grids needing 1M+ row virtualization, (b) management grids needing column pinning + grouping. Those two use cases justify TanStack Table - but NOT across all 24 apps immediately. Ponytail ladder: rung 2 applies for most tables (existing DataTable). TanStack Table is rung 4/7 only for the virtualization + advanced grid subset.

adopt_now.verify_notes: SECURITY FLAG (resolved): May 11 2026 supply chain attack (CVE-2026-45321, GHSA-g7cv-rxg3-hmpx) hit 42 @tanstack/* packages via GitHub Actions cache poisoning. @tanstack/query* and @tanstack/form* are confirmed clean per multiple independen
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: For Query: no simpler alternative - already the standard, no change needed. For Form v1 specifically: RHF v7 (already installed) covers multi-step wizards via useFormContext + FormProvider. 2026 independent consensus from Formisch, splitforms, LogRocket, MakersDen: default to RHF; reach for TanStack Form only when nested dynamic field arrays + per-field async validation state are the core requirement. Bundle sizes nearly identical (RHF ~10.7 KB gzipped, TanStack Form ~9.6 KB gzipped). Ponytail ladder: Form v1 does not clear rung 5 for general use - RHF covers it. It clears rung 7 only for the specific multi-step wizard archetype where RHF's form-wide validation timing is a real constraint.

adopt_now.verify_notes: DOWNGRADE from adopt to trial. Three reasons: (1) Candidate description is stale - v2 is deprecated, not stable; adoption must be v3-pinned from day one. (2) WCAG 2.1 AA gap: HealthStack patient-app and clinician-app face legal healthcare a
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No chart utility exists in stdlib, Radix, Tailwind, or any already-installed dep (@curaos/ui has none). Scratch SVG is non-trivial for the required types (RadialBar, ComposedChart, Radar). visx (@visx/* packages, ~15KB modular) is the lower-level alternative but requires D3 knowledge and 2-3x implementation time per chart - not appropriate for generator-first emission. Nivo has better out-of-box WCAG 2.1 AA (all SVG charts include ARIA + keyboard nav) but heavier bundle and less ergonomic wrapper pattern. Recharts v3 accessibilityLayer is now default-on, closes most of the gap, though ARIA role choices (img vs graphics-symbol) remain imperfect. For CuraOS HealthStack (patient-app vitals, clinical dashboards) WCAG AA is a legal compliance requirement (ADA Title II healthcare deadline active as of 2026). This is the one real risk: Recharts accessibility is opt-in-improved, not guaranteed AA, requiring per-component augmentation.

adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables plain-language AI responses on person-facing surfaces; streaming to React frontend via NestJS SSE; tool calls drive automation steps.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Trial (not adopt) because of two real gates that must be resolved first: (1) ESM migration of automation-core-service (or scoping ai SDK to a new ESM-only package within the monorepo) must be planned as explicit work, not assumed free. (2)
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Ponytail check: the codebase has no existing LLM call layer (automation-core-service has zero ai/mastra deps). The actual use case is generateText/streamText calls inside AiAgentStep handlers. If Mastra is already planned as the agent runtime, Mastra wraps ai SDK internally - direct ai SDK import in automation-core-service may be redundant for the agentic path. For non-agentic LLM steps (single generateText), fetch() against LiteLLM's OpenAI-compatible endpoint with a Zod-validated response is a zero-dep option that avoids the ESM migration cost entirely. That is the ponytail ladder rung to test first before committing to ai SDK in CJS services. For the full streaming + tool-call + structured-output surface (the full value prop), fetch() is not a substitute - ai SDK earns its keep there.

adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables AI agents (internal and external) to invoke CuraOS workflows on behalf of a person; exposes person-consented tools to agent ecosystem.
adopt_now.verify_notes: THREE active security concerns that must be mitigated before production: (1) CVE-2026-25536 (CVSS High, cross-client data leak via StreamableHTTPServerTransport instance reuse; fixed in >=1.26.0 - pin to >=1.26.0, currently satisfied by 1.2
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Not applicable for the core use case (protocol interop with the MCP ecosystem is the point; you cannot substitute it). However, ponytail challenge: if the actual need is internal workflow automation addressability, a simpler REST/OpenAPI endpoint from NestJS already handles it with zero new deps and works today. MCP adoption is justified ONLY if external AI agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) need to invoke CuraOS workflows - i.e., the protocol interop value is real. If the near-term use is internal-only, defer. If Mastra is adopted as the automation orchestrator, @mastra/mcp already wraps this SDK - direct adoption of the raw SDK is then redundant (YAGNI). Resolve the Mastra decision first.

adopt_now.name: node-x12
adopt_now.what: ASC X12 parser, generator, query engine, and mapper with stream support. Covers the entire @curaos/x12-sdk gap (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle): ISA/GS envelope, 837P/837I generation, 835 ERA parsing, 270/271 eligibility round-trip. The query language (dot-path selectors) can extract segment values without writing loop code. JS-EDI-Notation provides a JSON-friendly interchange format that maps cleanly to TypeSpec contracts.
adopt_now.taxonomy: ASC X12 parser, generator, query engine, and mapper with stream support. Covers the entire @curaos/x12-sdk gap (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle): ISA/GS envelope, 837P/837I generation, 835 ERA parsing, 270/271 eligibility round-trip. The query language (dot-path selectors) can extract segment values without writing loop code. JS-EDI-Notation provides a JSON-friendly interchange format that maps cleanly to TypeSpec contracts.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure TypeScript, ESM-compatible; works under Bun. No NestJS adapter needed - import into @curaos/x12-sdk package directly. Drizzle schema sits upstream of it. Zod can validate the parsed JS objects. Generator-first: wrap in sdk-emit output so service code never calls node-x12 directly.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (library). No payer connectivity. Must pair with a clearinghouse adapter (Stedi/Claim.MD/Availity) for the 3rd-party leg.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk) - node-x12 is a vendored dep inside the SDK; sdk-emit exposes typed TS facades, never raw X12 API.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Underlying transport only. Person surfaces (claim-status timeline, up-front copay estimate, EOB plain-language statement) are built above it in claims-service. The library enables real 270 eligibility checks that power patient cost estimates before service.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Verdict rationale: downgrade from "adopt" to "confirmed-trial". The candidate is correct that node-x12 fills the gap and the generator-first wrapping isolates risk. But three adversarial findings push this to trial-gated: (1) The successor
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No stdlib or already-installed dep covers ASC X12 wire format parsing - this is a domain-specific binary-adjacent format with loop/segment/element grammar that requires a real parser. The ponytail ladder dead-ends at rung 5: no already-installed dep covers it. Scratch implementation of a compliant 5010 X12 parser + generator is non-trivial (weeks, not days) and would itself need the same maintenance. The real alternative comparison is: (A) node-x12 v1.7.1 locked + tightly wrapped, (B) EDIFlow @ediflow/x12 + @ediflow/hipaa-x12-005010 (April 2026, v0.3.0, actively maintained but ~3 months old, higher API churn risk), (C) tastypackets/x12-parser (parser only, no generator, no 837 emit), (D) buy clearinghouse SDK (Stedi/Claim.MD) that abstracts X12 entirely. Option D is worth flagging: if the clearinghouse adapter layer (already required for payer connectivity per the candidate's own note) can handle envelope assembly, node-x12 scope shrinks to internal canonical representation only. EDIFlow is the strongest emerging alternative but v0.3.0 at 3 months old is not adoption-ready for a healthcare billing lane.

adopt_now.name: x12-parser (tastypackets)
adopt_now.what: Streaming X12 parser built on Node Transform streams - no generation, query, or mapping. Tested against 1M+ 835 records in production. Pure stream-in / JSON-out: ideal for high-volume 835 ERA ingestion pipelines (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle, 835 parse leg only). Zero non-dev dependencies.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Streaming X12 parser built on Node Transform streams - no generation, query, or mapping. Tested against 1M+ 835 records in production. Pure stream-in / JSON-out: ideal for high-volume 835 ERA ingestion pipelines (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle, 835 parse leg only). Zero non-dev dependencies.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Works under Bun (Node streams compatible). No native TS types - need a thin .d.ts wrapper. Does not generate X12, so must pair with node-x12 or scratch-written generator for 837 emission. Best as the 835 stream ingestion path inside @curaos/x12-sdk.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (library). Parse leg only.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk, 835 stream parser path).
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables ERA auto-posting speed - faster 835 processing directly benefits patient EOB delivery and balance reconciliation on the person-app.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Downgrade from "adopt" to "trial" on two real grounds: (1) 4+ year stale codebase on a Node.js streaming library - Bun stream compat gaps could surface at scale and there is no upstream to fix them; (2) no TS types requires SDK-level wrappe
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No stdlib or already-installed dep covers X12 835 parsing. X12 is a proprietary delimiter-variable binary-ish EDI format with HIPAA-mandated loop structure (CLP/SVC implied context, ISA-declared delimiters, companion-guide variance per payer). Scratch implementation is non-trivial: delimiter sniffing, loop-context state machine, segment schema, and 1M+ record throughput. Not a one-liner. The ponytail ladder stops at rung 5 (already-installed dep) - but nothing is installed yet. This is a legitimate external dep slot. Simpler alternative: node-x12 has TS types and parse+generate, but is also in maintenance mode since 2021 and has lower weekly downloads than x12-parser. No clearly superior maintained option exists in the JS/TS ecosystem as of 2026.

Pairs with NLM Clinical Tables free API (clinicaltables.nlm.nih.gov) for CPT/HCPCS/SNOMED when online - the self-host vs BYO split for code lookup.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Zero-dependency npm package bundling the full ICD-10-CM code set (74,000+ codes) for offline local lookup with TypeScript types. adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct import into terminology-service or claims-service for claim validation. Normalizes dots/casing automatically. For CPT (AMA-licensed, not freely redistributable) use NLM API or AutoICD API. Zod validates the code + description shape. Works under Bun.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both paths: @lowlysre/icd-10-cm is the local/air-gap self-host path. NLM Clinical Tables is the BYO-3rd-party free public API path. CPT codes require AMA license - no free local option; AutoICD API ($49/mo Reference tier) is the BYO path for CPT/HCPCS.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk, diagnosis-pointer validation) + terminology-service (code lookup endpoint).
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Diagnosis code lookup enables the person-facing claim detail view to show readable diagnosis descriptions (not raw ICD codes). Clinician coding surface uses same lookup for code suggestion during charge capture.

adopt_now.name: dinero.js v2
adopt_now.what: Immutable, tree-shakeable, bigint-native money arithmetic library with 168 ISO 4217 currencies and compile-time currency mismatch detection. Directly fills @curaos/currency package gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.multi-currency) and replaces hand-rolled SafeMinorNonNeg/int64 patterns throughout accounting-core, sales-core, commerce-core, and healthstack-billing.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Immutable, tree-shakeable, bigint-native money arithmetic library with 168 ISO 4217 currencies and compile-time currency mismatch detection. Directly fills @curaos/currency package gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.multi-currency) and replaces hand-rolled SafeMinorNonNeg/int64 patterns throughout accounting-core, sales-core, commerce-core, and healthstack-billing.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Zero-dep npm import, full TypeScript generics, tree-shakeable. Bun-native. Drizzle stores int64 minor units (unchanged); Dinero wraps at service boundary for arithmetic and formatting. FX gain/loss computed as Dinero difference before posting to accounting-core GL. No wrapper needed - import directly into @curaos/currency package.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (offline, no external API). FX rate feed is BYO (ECB/Frankfurter free API or Open Exchange Rates). Satisfies both lanes.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: @curaos/currency package (new); accounting.tsp FX gain/loss entry generation; all money-path services import via @curaos/currency.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables accurate multi-currency display in 'my invoices', 'my balance', 'my copay estimate' across all person-facing apps with locale-aware formatting built in.
adopt_now.verify_notes: VERDICT RATIONALE - trial not full adopt: The value proposition is real (compile-time currency safety, allocation math, FX scaffolding) and the integration is clean. But three friction points prevent immediate 'adopt': (1) bus factor 1 with
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: REAL ALTERNATIVE EXISTS and partially covers the use case. The codebase already stores amounts as bigint minor units in Drizzle (amount_minor BIGINT + text currency_code). For arithmetic: native BigInt operators cover add/subtract/compare at service boundaries with zero deps. For locale formatting: Intl.NumberFormat (stdlib, Bun-native) covers all 168 ISO 4217 currencies with locale-aware display - no library needed. What dinero adds on top: (1) currency-mismatch type safety at compile time, (2) allocation/splitting (banker's rounding), (3) FX conversion scaffolding. Those three are real gaps stdlib cannot close. So the honest ladder answer: stdlib + native BigInt covers ~70% of the claimed value; adopt dinero only if compile-time currency safety and allocation math are explicit requirements for the @curaos/currency package.

adopt_now.name: node-sales-tax (sales-tax npm)
adopt_now.what: Offline international tax rate calculator covering EU VAT (MOSS), Canada GST, 50+ countries with optional online VAT number fraud check. Partial fill for @curaos/tax-engine gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.tax). Covers simple flat-rate VAT/GST look-up; compound/repartition/withholding from Odoo account_tax model still needs port-adapt on top.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Offline international tax rate calculator covering EU VAT (MOSS), Canada GST, 50+ countries with optional online VAT number fraud check. Partial fill for @curaos/tax-engine gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.tax). Covers simple flat-rate VAT/GST look-up; compound/repartition/withholding from Odoo account_tax model still needs port-adapt on top.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct npm import, TypeScript types built-in, zero deps, Bun-compatible. Plugs into @curaos/tax-engine as the rate-lookup primitive; compound/repartition/withholding logic wraps on top. No Drizzle interaction needed at rate-lookup layer.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (offline JSON rates, no API key). BYO lane: swap for TaxJar/Avalara SDK when tenant needs real-time nexus calculation. Satisfies both lanes via pluggable rate-provider pattern.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: @curaos/tax-engine package; accounting.tsp tax computation step; sales-core invoice line tax resolution.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables accurate tax display on 'my invoice' before payment; correct copay tax on patient billing estimate.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Adopt as trial with three hard conditions: (1) Wrap inside @curaos/tax-engine so no service ever calls sales-tax directly - the pluggable rate-provider pattern the candidate describes is mandatory, not optional, both for BYO-TaxJar/Avalara
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Partially. For EU-only coverage: ibericode/vat-rates or benbucksch/eu-vat-rates are pure static JSON with no runtime dep and no code surface.
