They are narrower (EU only, no Canada GST, no 50+ country coverage) and have no computation logic - callers must implement rate resolution themselves. For CuraOS scope (ERP + HealthStack patient billing across multiple jurisdictions), sales-tax's rate coverage and the minimal computation API are worth the marginal weight over a raw JSON file, provided cross-fetch is quarantined. The ponytail ladder stops here: no already-installed dep covers 50-country tax rate lookup + Canadian GST; stdlib does not; the package is justified but must be adopted with the cross-fetch caveat addressed.

adopt_now.name: xyflow / @xyflow/react
adopt_now.what: MIT node-based canvas library for React (React Flow). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (WorkflowCanvas archetype) and `ui-kit-archetypes` (WorkflowCanvas component). Used by Windmill, n8n, Retool for their flow editors. Custom node types map to CuraOS BPM step types (human-approval, connector, code-step, AI-agent). Generator-first target: ui-kit WorkflowCanvas component + workflow-designer surface in builder-core.
adopt_now.taxonomy: MIT node-based canvas library for React (React Flow). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (WorkflowCanvas archetype) and `ui-kit-archetypes` (WorkflowCanvas component). Used by Windmill, n8n, Retool for their flow editors. Custom node types map to CuraOS BPM step types (human-approval, connector, code-step, AI-agent). Generator-first target: ui-kit WorkflowCanvas component + workflow-designer surface in builder-core.
adopt_now.stack_fit: React component; TypeScript-native with exported Node/Edge/OnConnect types. Zero backend coupling - graph JSON stored in Drizzle. MiniMap, Background, Controls ship out of box. Custom node renders any React component, so BPM step palette maps directly to @curaos/ui components.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only; no cloud dependency. First-party self-hosted. Pro examples subscription is optional and does not affect runtime.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-kit WorkflowCanvas component (wraps xyflow) + workflow-core-service flow definition JSON schema stored via Drizzle
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: WorkflowCanvas is a management surface (admins design BPM flows). Person sees the workflow as a journey timeline card, not the canvas. Management retains full canvas control; person gets 'waiting on you' inbox item derived from the same flow definition.

adopt_now.name: dnd-kit (@dnd-kit/core + @dnd-kit/sortable)
adopt_now.what: MIT drag-and-drop primitive toolkit for React. Not a full builder - the low-level foundation that Puck and other builders sit on. Covers `ui-kit-archetypes` gap for KanbanBoard and any sortable list archetype needing custom drag behavior beyond what Puck's DropZone provides. Generator-first target: @curaos/ui KanbanBoard, ApprovalInbox sortable columns.
adopt_now.taxonomy: MIT drag-and-drop primitive toolkit for React. Not a full builder - the low-level foundation that Puck and other builders sit on. Covers `ui-kit-archetypes` gap for KanbanBoard and any sortable list archetype needing custom drag behavior beyond what Puck's DropZone provides. Generator-first target: @curaos/ui KanbanBoard, ApprovalInbox sortable columns.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure React hooks; 6KB core; no CSS opinion; TypeScript-native. Fits directly into @curaos/ui component library. Puck already uses it internally so no version conflict if Puck is also adopted.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host; no cloud.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: @curaos/ui KanbanBoard + ApprovalInbox components (archetype vocabulary in ui-app-emit)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: KanbanBoard/ApprovalInbox used on both management (admin pipeline, task board) and person (my-tasks board, my-requests queue) surfaces from the same component with different data.

adopt_now.name: MapLibre GL JS + react-map-gl
adopt_now.what: MapLibre GL JS (BSD-3, npm: maplibre-gl) is the open-source WebGL map renderer; react-map-gl v8 (MIT, npm: react-map-gl) wraps it with idiomatic React/TypeScript components. Together they cover taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.maps for the ui-kit MapView archetype and all 24 web + 2 mobile apps that need interactive maps.
adopt_now.taxonomy: MapLibre GL JS (BSD-3, npm: maplibre-gl) is the open-source WebGL map renderer; react-map-gl v8 (MIT, npm: react-map-gl) wraps it with idiomatic React/TypeScript components. Together they cover taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.maps for the ui-kit MapView archetype and all 24 web + 2 mobile apps that need interactive maps.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct npm-import into @curaos/ui. react-map-gl/maplibre peer requires maplibre-gl>=4. Full TypeScript, no @types needed. Bun-compatible. Map component wraps in ui-kit MapView archetype; ui-app-emit generates it from contract archetype hint 'map'.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: serves both: self-hosted tiles from Martin/PMTiles/SeaweedFS OR BYO 3rd-party tile provider (MapTiler, Esri, HERE) by swapping tile URL - zero code change
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-kit component (MapView, MapMarker, GeofenceOverlay) + ui-app-emit archetype 'map' -> emits MapView usage with tile URL + source from contract
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Powers patient/customer-facing location UX (find nearby facility, live delivery tracking on personal-shop, SOS location pin on personal-tracking app) AND management fleet map dashboard. Same component, two surfaces.
adopt_now.verify_notes: ADOPT web layer (MapLibre GL JS v5.24 + react-map-gl v8.1.1 from react-map-gl/maplibre) as confirmed-trial, not full adopt, because: (1) react-map-gl v8 is relatively young (v8.0 released within the last ~6 months) and the MapLibre v5 API s
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No. Ponytail ladder check: no map library exists in the codebase (grep confirms zero references to maplibre, leaflet, openlayers, mapbox, deck.gl). Leaflet is the only lighter alternative - it IS simpler but disqualified for fleet/health use cases requiring 10k+ real-time markers; Leaflet DOM rendering degrades sharply above ~1k markers per published benchmarks. Native platform (<iframe> embed, static image tiles) does not cover interactive vector maps with geofence overlays, live tracking pins, and zoom/filter interaction. MapLibre GL is the minimum viable choice for the stated use cases; no installed dep covers it.

adopt_now.name: Valhalla routing engine
adopt_now.what: Open-source OSM routing engine with multi-modal routing, isochrones, time-aware turn restrictions, map matching, matrix queries, and optimized routes. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location (routing leg of location service) and fleet-core dispatch (ETA, route optimization). `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (MIT, npm) is a typed HTTP client.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Open-source OSM routing engine with multi-modal routing, isochrones, time-aware turn restrictions, map matching, matrix queries, and optimized routes. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location (routing leg of location service) and fleet-core dispatch (ETA, route optimization). `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (MIT, npm) is a typed HTTP client.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Deploy as Docker sidecar. NestJS geospatial-core-service calls Valhalla HTTP API via `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (v1.0.29, MIT, 71 weekly downloads - thin but typed). Wrap into @curaos/routing-sdk to abstract self-host vs BYO provider. fleet-core-service emits dispatch.route.calculated events from routing results.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: self-host via Docker; BYO via HERE Routing, Google Routes, Mapbox Directions API - same @curaos/routing-sdk interface
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/routing-sdk) + service geospatial-core-service adds /routing endpoint wired to Valhalla; fleet-core-service consumes routing-sdk for ETA
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Powers patient/customer journey: 'get directions to clinic', 'track my delivery ETA', 'how long until driver arrives'. Management surface: fleet dispatcher sees optimal routes, driver app gets turn-by-turn. Isochrones enable 'show facilities within 30 min' person surface.
adopt_now.verify_notes:
**Research value: high** - Multiple independent sources confirm prior art and real deployment patterns.

ADVERSARIAL FINDINGS (net verdict: confirm-trial, not full adopt yet):

1. LICENSE - PASS. MIT confirmed, compatible with self-host + a
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Two alternatives worth flagging: (1) @routingjs/valhalla (from nilsnolde/routingjs, gis-ops maintained) is a better-maintained multi-engine TypeScript client with a unified interface across Valhalla, OSRM, GraphHopper, and ORS - it directly enables the BYO-provider duality the curaos-local-vs-3rdparty rule requires, making it a stronger pick than @iwpnd/valhalla-ts. (2) OSRM is faster for pure A-to-B ETA queries with no dynamic costing, but lacks isochrones and multi-modal - insufficient for 'show facilities within 30 min' requirement. No already-installed CuraOS dependency or stdlib covers geospatial routing. Valhalla is the correct OSS engine; the client library choice should be upgraded.

adopt_now.name: Traccar
adopt_now.what: Open-source GPS tracking server supporting 200+ protocols and 2000+ GPS hardware device models. REST API + WebSocket for live position events. PostgreSQL backend. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.fleet-tracking: the hardware-ingestion layer that fleet-core-service cannot replicate without enormous protocol effort.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Open-source GPS tracking server supporting 200+ protocols and 2000+ GPS hardware device models. REST API + WebSocket for live position events. PostgreSQL backend. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.fleet-tracking: the hardware-ingestion layer that fleet-core-service cannot replicate without enormous protocol effort.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Deploy as Docker sidecar with its own PG DB (separate from CuraOS CNPG tenants). NestJS fleet-core-service consumes Traccar REST API (GET /api/positions, GET /api/events, WebSocket /api/socket) via a thin @curaos/traccar-adapter. Position events forwarded as fleet.position.updated Kafka events. fleet-core geofence logic in PostGIS owns geofence evaluation (Traccar geofences are duplicates - skip). No Bun/NestJS changes needed beyond the adapter.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: self-host (Docker, PostgreSQL backend); BYO-3rd-party: Samsara, Here Fleet, Teltonika Cloud, Google Maps Fleet - same adapter contract
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/traccar-adapter) adapter between Traccar REST+WebSocket and fleet-core-service position ingestion contract
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Driver app (personal-tracking-service) sends positions to Traccar via existing protocol OR directly to fleet-core API. Traccar handles hardware GPS devices (ambulance, delivery van, field worker device) that personal-tracking cannot handle. Person (patient/customer) sees derived delivery ETA and SOS response location, not raw Traccar data.

adopt_now.name: Photon (komoot)
adopt_now.what: OpenStreetMap geocoder built on OpenSearch (search-as-you-type, typo-tolerant, forward + reverse geocode). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location: the geocoding leg of geospatial-core-service (convert address <-> coordinates). GraphHopper publishes weekly updated Photon database dumps, drastically reducing import burden.
adopt_now.taxonomy: OpenStreetMap geocoder built on OpenSearch (search-as-you-type, typo-tolerant, forward + reverse geocode). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location: the geocoding leg of geospatial-core-service (convert address <-> coordinates). GraphHopper publishes weekly updated Photon database dumps, drastically reducing import burden.
adopt_now.stack_fit: CuraOS already runs Elasticsearch/OpenSearch for search-service. Photon uses the same OpenSearch index. NestJS geospatial-core-service calls Photon HTTP REST via thin @curaos/geocoder-sdk wrapper. Zarf-bundleable. No npm package needed - plain HTTP.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: self-host via Docker + OpenSearch; BYO: Google Geocoding API, HERE Geocoder, Mapbox Geocoding - same @curaos/geocoder-sdk interface
adopt_now.generator_first_target: service geospatial-core-service: generator emits /geocode and /reverse endpoints wired to @curaos/geocoder-sdk; geocoder-sdk has self-hosted Photon + BYO provider implementations
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Powers 'find my nearest clinic', facility search, delivery address autocomplete, SOS address resolution in person apps. Management surface: driver address lookup, geofence definition by address.

adopt_now.name: PMTiles (protomaps)
adopt_now.what: Single-file archive format for vector/raster tiles served via HTTP range requests from any S3-compatible object store (including SeaweedFS). Zero tile server needed for bounded/static datasets. Covers the same curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles taxonomy_id as Martin but as a simpler deployment path for pre-built tile archives (country basemaps, regional overlays).
adopt_now.taxonomy: Single-file archive format for vector/raster tiles served via HTTP range requests from any S3-compatible object store (including SeaweedFS). Zero tile server needed for bounded/static datasets. Covers the same curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles taxonomy_id as Martin but as a simpler deployment path for pre-built tile archives (country basemaps, regional overlays).
adopt_now.stack_fit: npm package `pmtiles` for MapLibre integration (no import on NestJS needed). SeaweedFS already deployed - upload .pmtiles basemap file, set CORS headers, done. Martin handles dynamic PostGIS tiles; PMTiles handles static basemap archives. Complementary, not competing.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: first-party self-host on SeaweedFS; BYO-3rd-party: Protomaps CDN, MapTiler CDN - same PMTiles URL in MapLibre style
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-kit MapView archetype: tile source switches between Martin (dynamic) and PMTiles (static) via env config; no code change
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables offline-capable basemap for mobile apps (personal-tracking, personal-shop delivery view). Air-gap deployments get a bundled basemap Zarf artifact with zero runtime tile server.

adopt_now.name: Drizzle ORM PostGIS geometry type (built-in + wkx)
adopt_now.what: Drizzle ORM v0.31+ ships native `geometry` column type for PostGIS points (SRID 4326, mode: xy). Polygon/multipolygon/linestring requires a `customType` wrapper with `wkx` npm package for WKB-to-GeoJSON conversion. Together this covers the data-layer contract gap in geospatial-core-service: no external library needed beyond what is already in the stack.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Drizzle ORM v0.31+ ships native `geometry` column type for PostGIS points (SRID 4326, mode: xy). Polygon/multipolygon/linestring requires a `customType` wrapper with `wkx` npm package for WKB-to-GeoJSON conversion. Together this covers the data-layer contract gap in geospatial-core-service: no external library needed beyond what is already in the stack.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct drop-in for geospatial-core-service Drizzle schema. geometry('location', { type: 'point', srid: 4326 }) for vehicle/facility positions; customType + wkx for geofence polygons. GiST index via sql template in drizzle migration. Generator folds geometry column + GiST index into codegen mold for any entity with 'location' field hint.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: first-party only (DB layer)
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter: add geometry column + GiST index emission when contract entity has @geo decorator; wkx added to SDK package template
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables proper spatial storage and queries (ST_DWithin for 'nearby', ST_Contains for geofence entry) backing both person (find nearby clinic) and management (geofence alert) surfaces.

adopt_now.name: @medplum/fhirtypes + @medplum/core + @medplum/fhirpath
adopt_now.what: Decomposable TypeScript npm packages from the Medplum monorepo: @medplum/fhirtypes = generated R4 type definitions (compile-time FHIR schema enforcement); @medplum/core = MedplumClient (FHIR CRUD/search/subscription), resource validators, FHIRPath evaluator client; @medplum/fhirpath = standalone FHIRPath engine. Together they fill gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 client + type-safe contract base for @curaos/fhir-client package) and [clinical-fhir-resource-set] (R4 type safety for Observation/Condition/MedicationRequest/AllergyIntolerance etc. contracts). These packages can be npm-imported WITHOUT running the Medplum server.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Decomposable TypeScript npm packages from the Medplum monorepo: @medplum/fhirtypes = generated R4 type definitions (compile-time FHIR schema enforcement); @medplum/core = MedplumClient (FHIR CRUD/search/subscription), resource validators, FHIRPath evaluator client; @medplum/fhirpath = standalone FHIRPath engine. Together they fill gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 client + type-safe contract base for @curaos/fhir-client package) and [clinical-fhir-resource-set] (R4 type safety for Observation/Condition/MedicationRequest/AllergyIntolerance etc. contracts). These packages can be npm-imported WITHOUT running the Medplum server.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Native TS, Bun-compatible, no wrapper needed. @medplum/core client talks to any FHIR R4 server (Medplum, HAPI, or CuraOS own server). @medplum/fhirtypes provides the type layer for TypeSpec-generated FHIR contracts. Zod schemas can wrap fhirtypes for runtime validation. NestJS service can inject MedplumClient as a provider.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: first-party self-host (point client at CuraOS interop-service FHIR endpoint) AND BYO third-party (point at external HAPI or cloud FHIR server). Same client, different base URL.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/fhir-client): @medplum/fhirtypes as the type layer + @medplum/core client as the HTTP adapter. TypeSpec emitter for healthstack services imports fhirtypes as the canonical R4 shape.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: R4 types enforce correct FHIR resource shape for patient-facing APIs (Patient compartment, Observation, CarePlan); FHIRPath enables declarative 'my record' queries across resources without extra service code.
adopt_now.verify_notes:
Verdict is confirmed-trial (not confirmed-adopt) because:
1. ESM-in-NestJS-11 integration path is unproven in the CuraOS repo; needs a one-off spike to validate tsconfig + Bun + NestJS 11 ESM interop before generator templates are written a
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: @types/fhir (DefinitelyTyped) for type-only use: active, zero-dep, no version lock to client. REJECTED: known open defects (circular FhirResource reference, missing constraint profiles), not co-evolved with a FHIR client, would require a separate FHIR HTTP client anyway. Savings do not outweigh the completeness gap in a HIPAA-adjacent codebase. @medplum/fhirtypes wins on correctness. For the HTTP client layer: hand-rolling a thin fetch wrapper against a FHIR R4 OpenAPI spec is feasible but produces a maintenance surface that @medplum/core already solved with weekly patches and validator parity. Ponytail verdict: adopt the library, not the scratch implementation.

adopt_now.name: fhir-validator-js (Outburn) + fhir-validator-wrapper (FHIR/HL7)
adopt_now.what: Node.js wrappers around the canonical HL7 Java FHIR Validator CLI. fhir-validator-js (Apache-2.0, v1.4.0, Mar 2025) manages JVM sessions, supports multiple concurrent IG contexts, downloads JAR automatically. Fills gap [clinical-fhir-resource-set] test requirement: 'FHIR-R4 validation per resource (HAPI/fhir.js in CI)' and [interop-fhir-server] 'FHIR-validate every resource in CI'. Runs against US Core, SNOMED-backed IGs, and custom CuraOS profiles.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Node.js wrappers around the canonical HL7 Java FHIR Validator CLI. fhir-validator-js (Apache-2.0, v1.4.0, Mar 2025) manages JVM sessions, supports multiple concurrent IG contexts, downloads JAR automatically. Fills gap [clinical-fhir-resource-set] test requirement: 'FHIR-R4 validation per resource (HAPI/fhir.js in CI)' and [interop-fhir-server] 'FHIR-validate every resource in CI'. Runs against US Core, SNOMED-backed IGs, and custom CuraOS profiles.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Used in CI (just ci gate) and optionally as a sidecar in healthstack-interop-service for inbound FHIR resource validation. Requires JRE in the pod/CI runner - add openjdk:21-jre to Dockerfile. Bun-compatible for the JS wrapper layer. NestJS service calls it async before persisting inbound resources.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: JVM runs locally in CI or K8s pod. No external call; IGs loaded locally. Supports air-gap with pre-downloaded JAR + IG packages.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: Codegen emitter: every healthstack service's CI test suite gets a FHIR validation step scaffolded by the service-core template.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Indirect: validated FHIR resources are trustworthy inputs to patient-facing read models; validation failures caught in CI not at patient query time.
adopt_now.verify_notes: SECURITY FLAG - three active CVEs in the underlying HL7 Java validator (org.hl7.fhir.validation / org.hl7.fhir.core): CVE-2026-34361 (CVSS 9.3, Critical) and CVE-2026-34360 (blind SSRF) both affect the /loadIG HTTP endpoint - fixed in 6.9.4
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Yes, with a critical scope distinction. @medplum/core validateResource / indexStructureDefinitionBundle provides JVM-free FHIR R4 schema + profile validation in pure TypeScript. It supports US Core and custom StructureDefinitions loaded locally. It is already a plausible dependency given CuraOS HealthStack's FHIR surface. For structural correctness checks and base-spec conformance it is sufficient and has zero JVM cost. The gap: @medplum/core does NOT replicate the HL7 reference validator's terminology-binding checks (SNOMED, LOINC value set expansion), FHIRPath invariant evaluation depth, or gold-standard IG conformance scoring. For interoperability certification-grade CI validation against US Core + CuraOS custom profiles, the HL7 Java validator is the only authoritative tool. The two are complementary, not interchangeable - use @medplum/core for fast unit-level checks and fhir-validator-js for conformance-gate CI runs.

adopt_now.name: fhirclient (SMART on FHIR JS/TS client)
adopt_now.what: Official SMART on FHIR JS/TS client library (Apache-2.0). Handles EHR-launch and standalone-launch OAuth2 + PKCE flows, token refresh, FHIR resource fetching with scoping. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] SMART patient compartment scoping and the 'my record' patient-portal export path. Required for any external EHR integration (Epic, Cerner, Athena) and for CuraOS's patient-app acting as a SMART app against external EHRs.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Official SMART on FHIR JS/TS client library (Apache-2.0). Handles EHR-launch and standalone-launch OAuth2 + PKCE flows, token refresh, FHIR resource fetching with scoping. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] SMART patient compartment scoping and the 'my record' patient-portal export path. Required for any external EHR integration (Epic, Cerner, Athena) and for CuraOS's patient-app acting as a SMART app against external EHRs.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Node 18+ / Bun compatible. Used in NestJS backend for server-side SMART launch; in React patient-app for browser-side launch. No wrapper needed. Pairs with @medplum/fhirtypes for typed resource access.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: first-party (CuraOS SMART server via identity-service + healthstack-interop-service) AND BYO-3rd-party (Epic/Cerner SMART launch for external EHR connection).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/fhir-client): SMART launch adapter wrapping fhirclient, exposed as a NestJS provider.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: The primary mechanism for 'my record' patient access: person authenticates via SMART, client fetches their compartment resources from any FHIR R4 server. Directly enables [healthcare.registration.patient-portal-phr] gap.

adopt_now.name: Snowstorm (IHTSDO) - already in use as terminology-service backing
adopt_now.what: Apache-2.0 FHIR terminology server with deep SNOMED CT support plus LOINC, ICD-10, ICD-10-CM. CuraOS terminology-service is already backed by Snowstorm (verified: 'present-strong', taxonomy_id healthcare.interop.snomed-loinc-terminology). Gap is NOT a new server but value-set API adapter: healthstack-quality-service needs $expand + $validate-code calls for eCQM measure code binding, and healthstack-meds-service needs RxNorm $lookup. Fills [ecqm-quality-engine] value-set binding step and coded-field binding for all FHIR resource services.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Apache-2.0 FHIR terminology server with deep SNOMED CT support plus LOINC, ICD-10, ICD-10-CM. CuraOS terminology-service is already backed by Snowstorm (verified: 'present-strong', taxonomy_id healthcare.interop.snomed-loinc-terminology). Gap is NOT a new server but value-set API adapter: healthstack-quality-service needs $expand + $validate-code calls for eCQM measure code binding, and healthstack-meds-service needs RxNorm $lookup. Fills [ecqm-quality-engine] value-set binding step and coded-field binding for all FHIR resource services.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Already deployed. NestJS services call FHIR $expand/$lookup via HTTP. A thin @curaos/terminology-sdk client (wrapping fetch) is the only new code needed. No new infrastructure.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-hosted (Snowstorm on-cluster). BYO-3rd-party path: VSAC API (NLM) or Ontoserver for hosted value sets.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/terminology-sdk): thin codegen-emitted client for $expand/$lookup/$translate, consumed by healthstack service TypeSpec-generated code.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables plain-language display names for coded clinical values shown to patients (SNOMED preferred term, LOINC display). Required for correct AVS and patient-app clinical summaries.

adopt_now.name: OHIF Viewer v3 + Orthanc DICOM server
adopt_now.what: OHIF (MIT, v3.11, React/Cornerstone.js): zero-footprint browser DICOM viewer supporting DICOMweb (QIDO-RS/WADO-RS/STOW-RS), self-hosted as static bundle or Docker. Orthanc (GPL-3, C++): lightweight PACS server with DICOMweb plugin, REST API, widely used as self-hosted DICOM store. Together they fill gap [healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs) for healthstack-imaging-service DICOM/PACS integration. OHIF iframe-embedded into CuraOS imaging UI; Orthanc runs as K8s sidecar service.
adopt_now.taxonomy: OHIF (MIT, v3.11, React/Cornerstone.js): zero-footprint browser DICOM viewer supporting DICOMweb (QIDO-RS/WADO-RS/STOW-RS), self-hosted as static bundle or Docker. Orthanc (GPL-3, C++): lightweight PACS server with DICOMweb plugin, REST API, widely used as self-hosted DICOM store. Together they fill gap [healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs) for healthstack-imaging-service DICOM/PACS integration. OHIF iframe-embedded into CuraOS imaging UI; Orthanc runs as K8s sidecar service.
adopt_now.stack_fit: OHIF: iframe embed or separate static deployment - no import into NestJS. Orthanc: HTTP REST + WADO-RS from healthstack-imaging-service. DICOMweb client calls from NestJS (simple fetch wrapper). No deep integration needed. GPL-3 on Orthanc = service boundary safe (CuraOS code never inside Orthanc).
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-hosted Orthanc (air-gap compatible) AND BYO-3rd-party PACS (Sectra, Change Healthcare, AWS HealthImaging - any DICOMweb-compliant store).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-imaging-service): DICOMweb adapter stub emitted by codegen; OHIF viewer URL rendered in imaging-app React component.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Patient 'my imaging' view: OHIF renders study thumbnails + viewer for person to see their own scans. Clinician worklist is the management surface. Same DICOMweb backend serves both.

adopt_now.name: CDS Hooks spec + sandbox-cds-services (HL7 open)
adopt_now.what: CDS Hooks is the HL7-standard REST protocol for embedding clinical decision support into EHR workflows (hook -> prefetch context -> CDS service returns cards/suggestions). cds-hooks/sandbox-cds-services provides Node/Express reference implementations. Fills gap [cds-rules-engine] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support) integration protocol: CuraOS healthstack-quality-service exposes CDS Hooks endpoints so external EHRs and its own clinician-app can call it. Enables drug-allergy checks, reminders, and risk-score cards to surface in any SMART-enabled EHR.
adopt_now.taxonomy: CDS Hooks is the HL7-standard REST protocol for embedding clinical decision support into EHR workflows (hook -> prefetch context -> CDS service returns cards/suggestions). cds-hooks/sandbox-cds-services provides Node/Express reference implementations. Fills gap [cds-rules-engine] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support) integration protocol: CuraOS healthstack-quality-service exposes CDS Hooks endpoints so external EHRs and its own clinician-app can call it. Enables drug-allergy checks, reminders, and risk-score cards to surface in any SMART-enabled EHR.
adopt_now.stack_fit: CDS Hooks = HTTP contract, not a library. healthstack-quality-service (NestJS) adds CDS Hooks REST endpoints (GET /cds-services, POST /cds-services/{id}). No npm package to import; implement against spec. @curaos/cds-hooks-sdk can be a thin NestJS decorator that maps CDS Hook request/response to TypeSpec contract types.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-hosted (CuraOS serves hooks to its own clinician-app) AND BYO-3rd-party (Epic/Cerner EHR calls CuraOS CDS service via CDS Hooks standard).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: Contract-typespec (CDS rule + hook contract in healthstack-quality.tsp) + codegen-emitter for CDS Hooks endpoint scaffold.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Indirect: CDS alerts from hooks become patient-facing nudges in patient-app (notify + tasks surfaces). Clinician gets inline decision support at point of care.

adopt_now.name: OpenTelemetry JS/TS SDK + @opentelemetry/instrumentation-nestjs-core
adopt_now.what: Distributed tracing, metrics, and log correlation for every NestJS service. Fills taxonomy gap workflow-automation.ops.observability (partial) and the scaffold-only backend/packages/observability package. The official OTel NestJS plugin auto-instruments controllers, guards, interceptors, BullMQ workers, and HTTP layers.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Distributed tracing, metrics, and log correlation for every NestJS service. Fills taxonomy gap workflow-automation.ops.observability (partial) and the scaffold-only backend/packages/observability package. The official OTel NestJS plugin auto-instruments controllers, guards, interceptors, BullMQ workers, and HTTP layers.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct npm-import into NestJS services. Bun requires a --preload otel-init.ts file (no --require flag) - documented 2026 pattern from oneuptime.com. Needs a thin wrapper module for NestJS DI (see nestjs-otel below). Generator-first: live-emit Handlebars main.ts template should import otel-init first.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: route OTLP spans to self-hosted Grafana LGTM stack (Tempo + Mimir + Loki). BYO-3rd-party: swap OTLP endpoint to Datadog, Honeycomb, Grafana Cloud, etc. Zero code change.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (live-emit Handlebars main.ts + otel-init.ts template) + SDK pkg (@curaos/observability package fills scaffold-only gap)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Backend infra - enables SLO alerting that protects patient and user journey reliability (scheduling, billing, consent latency budgets from ops/slo).
adopt_now.verify_notes: Downgraded from adopt to confirmed-trial because the Bun HTTP instrumentation gap (issue #26536) is unresolved and directly affects CuraOS's Bun-primary NestJS runtime. Before baking into the generator as default-on: (1) Run a smoke test: d
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No. The observability package is scaffold-only (just a README). Zero OTel deps exist in any curaos service package. Hand-rolling distributed tracing, metrics, and log correlation across 40+ NestJS services from scratch is far worse than OTel. No stdlib or already-installed dep covers this. Ponytail ladder stops at rung 5: OTel is the minimum viable solution for the stated problem.

adopt_now.name: nestjs-otel (pragmaticivan/nestjs-otel)
adopt_now.what: NestJS DI-aware wrapper around the OTel SDK: OpenTelemetryModule, metric histogram decorators, async context propagation. Fills the same workflow-automation.ops.observability gap - provides the NestJS-idiomatic layer on top of raw OTel so services get metrics/traces without boilerplate per-service.
adopt_now.taxonomy: NestJS DI-aware wrapper around the OTel SDK: OpenTelemetryModule, metric histogram decorators, async context propagation. Fills the same workflow-automation.ops.observability gap - provides the NestJS-idiomatic layer on top of raw OTel so services get metrics/traces without boilerplate per-service.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct import into NestJS app module. Wraps @opentelemetry/sdk-node; works alongside instrumentation-nestjs-core. No Bun-specific issues beyond the preload pattern. Generator-first: the module import goes into the codegen app-module template.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both - OTLP exporter target is configurable at runtime via env vars.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (app-module.ts.hbs template injection)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Backend infra; reduces per-service instrumentation boilerplate so the team focuses on domain features not observability plumbing.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Trial grade, not full adopt, for two reasons: (1) Bun preload integration has not been externally validated against CuraOS's exact bun compile + k3d deploy pipeline - needs one service spike before fleet-wide codegen emission. (2) Single-ma
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Partially yes. Raw @opentelemetry/instrumentation-nestjs-core (CNCF-backed, actively published - 0.65.0 released ~11 days ago) covers automatic trace collection from controller handlers with zero wrapper. For traces-only across services, the ponytail ladder says prefer the official package. nestjs-otel earns its keep ONLY for the additive features the official package cannot do: (1) per-request WideEvent attribute bag (structured enrichment from any DI provider in the call chain without request-scoped injection), (2) @Span / @Histogram / @Counter method decorators for domain-level metrics. If CuraOS services need only HTTP-span traces, drop to instrumentation-nestjs-core. If they need metric decorators and structured span enrichment in service methods (the observability-quality lane implies they do), nestjs-otel adds real value with ~5 lines of app module wiring vs. 50+ lines of manual SDK + interceptor boilerplate per service.

adopt_now.name: GlitchTip
adopt_now.what: Self-hosted error tracking with 100% Sentry SDK wire protocol compatibility. Fills error tracking gap (curaos_error_tracking_rule names GlitchTip prod + Sentry SaaS dev). MIT licensed, 4 containers, 256-512MB RAM. GlitchTip 6 (2026) added improved stacktraces.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Self-hosted error tracking with 100% Sentry SDK wire protocol compatibility. Fills error tracking gap (curaos_error_tracking_rule names GlitchTip prod + Sentry SaaS dev). MIT licensed, 4 containers, 256-512MB RAM. GlitchTip 6 (2026) added improved stacktraces.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Client side: @sentry/nestjs SDK (unchanged) pointed at GlitchTip DSN. Zero NestJS code change. OTel-mode Sentry v8 SDK also works: same OTel pipeline feeding GlitchTip as error sink and LGTM as trace/metrics backend. Helm chart for K8s deploy.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host primary (GlitchTip self-hosted on homelab/k3d). BYO-3rd-party: swap DSN to Sentry SaaS or Bugsink - no client code change.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/observability fills scaffold-only; includes Sentry/GlitchTip init) + codegen-emitter (otel-init.ts Handlebars includes error reporting init)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Catches patient-facing errors (scheduling failures, auth loops, billing crashes) before users report them; error rate feeds SLO burn-rate alerting.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Three real risks worth tracking: (1) Postgres write saturation above ~20M events/month - acceptable for CuraOS homelab scale; upgrade path is self-hosted Sentry (same DSN swap, zero client change). (2) No pre-built Zarf bundle - must author
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: LGTM stack (already in CuraOS observability) does NOT cover structured error grouping, issue deduplication, release tracking, or user-impact correlation the way GlitchTip does. The ponytail ladder check: (1) YAGNI? No - error_tracking_rule already names GlitchTip explicitly. (2) Already in codebase? No error sink exists. (3) Stdlib? Not applicable. (4) Native platform? OTel exceptions exist but no grouping/dedup UI. (5) Already-installed dep? LGTM handles traces/metrics/logs but has no Sentry-protocol error grouping. GlitchTip fills a genuine gap LGTM does not close. Verdict: not replaceable by what is already present.

adopt_now.name: Langfuse
adopt_now.what: LLM observability, evals, prompt management, and cost tracking for AI/automation-core and CDS steps. Fills workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (absent) observability sub-gap and the LLM eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule. MIT core; EE features (SCIM, audit log, data retention) need license key.
adopt_now.taxonomy: LLM observability, evals, prompt management, and cost tracking for AI/automation-core and CDS steps. Fills workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (absent) observability sub-gap and the LLM eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule. MIT core; EE features (SCIM, audit log, data retention) need license key.
adopt_now.license: MIT (core) + commercial EE for advanced features
adopt_now.stack_fit: JS SDK: npm install langfuse. OTel-mode: point OTel OTLP exporter to Langfuse ingest endpoint - zero extra instrumentation code if OTel is already set up. NestJS: inject LangfuseService as provider. Self-host requires platform >= 3.95.0. Helm chart for K8s.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Helm on homelab (2 containers + Postgres + Redis). BYO-3rd-party: Langfuse Cloud (GDPR-compliant EU region available).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg + codegen-emitter (ai-llm step template in automation-core and workflow-core services)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: LLM steps in CDS, automation-core, and AI workflow nodes affect patient safety and user journeys; Langfuse's eval metrics (faithfulness, hallucination) catch regressions before they reach users.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Verdict is trial, not adopt, for three reasons: (1) EE paywall on audit logs is a hard constraint for HealthStack regulated deployments - this must be contractually resolved before production rollout, not deferred; (2) air-gap image-registr
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Ponytail check: OTel already in CuraOS stack. If the only need is LLM span tracing routed into the existing Grafana/Tempo pipeline, zero new service is needed - emit gen_ai.* semantic conventions from LLM call sites and they flow through the existing OTel collector. That covers the observability sub-gap with no new infrastructure. What Langfuse adds beyond that: structured evals (faithfulness, hallucination scoring), prompt management UI, cost tracking per trace, and dataset/experiment management. These are not covered by stdlib OTel + Grafana. So the simpler path handles tracing only; Langfuse is justified only if eval scoring and prompt management are real near-term requirements. Arize Phoenix (Apache 2.0, fully open, no EE paywall, OTel-native) is a cleaner air-gap alternative if audit-log EE requirement is a concern, though it lacks prompt management.

adopt_now.name: Pyrra
adopt_now.what: SLO management for Prometheus: reads OpenSLO/CRD definitions, generates multi-window multi-burn-rate Prometheus recording rules and alerts, provides a UI. Fills ops/slo gap - CuraOS already has OpenSLO YAML templates; Pyrra is the K8s operator that activates them as live alerts.
adopt_now.taxonomy: SLO management for Prometheus: reads OpenSLO/CRD definitions, generates multi-window multi-burn-rate Prometheus recording rules and alerts, provides a UI. Fills ops/slo gap - CuraOS already has OpenSLO YAML templates; Pyrra is the K8s operator that activates them as live alerts.
adopt_now.stack_fit: K8s operator mode reads ServiceLevelObjective CRDs; filesystem mode reads YAML (matches ops/slo OpenSLO files). Helm chart available. No NestJS/Bun integration needed - pure ops plane. Generator: service-catalog.json already generates OpenSLO; Pyrra consumes them.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (K8s operator or Docker). No managed cloud SaaS option.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts already emits OpenSLO; wire Pyrra CRD schema as the target format)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: SLO burn-rate alerts fire before SLO breach, protecting patient scheduling/billing journey P95 latency budgets defined in ops/slo.

adopt_now.name: Grafana LGTM (Loki + Grafana + Tempo + Mimir) via lgtm-distributed Helm chart
adopt_now.what: Full self-hosted observability backend: Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Grafana (dashboards). Single Helm umbrella chart lgtm-distributed v3.0.1. Fills the backend sink for OTel spans/metrics/logs that CuraOS services emit. CuraOS ops/observability already generates Grafana dashboards - this is the deployment target.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Full self-hosted observability backend: Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Grafana (dashboards). Single Helm umbrella chart lgtm-distributed v3.0.1. Fills the backend sink for OTel spans/metrics/logs that CuraOS services emit. CuraOS ops/observability already generates Grafana dashboards - this is the deployment target.
adopt_now.license: Apache-2.0 (Grafana OSS) + AGPL for Grafana Enterprise features
adopt_now.stack_fit: NestJS services send OTLP to Alloy (Grafana Agent successor) which forwards to Mimir/Loki/Tempo. CuraOS ops/observability generate.ts already emits Grafana dashboard JSON - those dashboards import directly. Helm chart for homelab K8s.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: lgtm-distributed on homelab/k3d (monolithic mode for small cluster). BYO-3rd-party: Grafana Cloud (same OTLP endpoint, same dashboards).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts target format alignment with Mimir remote_write + Tempo OTLP endpoint)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Operational reliability for all person-centric journeys; Tempo distributed traces show which service in the patient scheduling or billing chain is slow.

adopt_now.name: Pact + Pact Broker (pact-js + pact-broker-docker)
adopt_now.what: Consumer-driven contract testing for HTTP APIs and async messaging between CuraOS microservices. pact-js v13 MIT; self-hosted Pact Broker via pactfoundation/pact-broker Docker image. AsyncAPI plugin (pact-asyncapi-comparator) covers Kafka/Redpanda event contracts from CuraOS AsyncAPI specs. Bi-directional contract mode (BDCT) accepts OpenAPI from TypeSpec output directly.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Consumer-driven contract testing for HTTP APIs and async messaging between CuraOS microservices. pact-js v13 MIT; self-hosted Pact Broker via pactfoundation/pact-broker Docker image. AsyncAPI plugin (pact-asyncapi-comparator) covers Kafka/Redpanda event contracts from CuraOS AsyncAPI specs. Bi-directional contract mode (BDCT) accepts OpenAPI from TypeSpec output directly.
adopt_now.stack_fit: pact-js npm-import in test files; pact-nestjs DI wrapper for provider verification. BDCT mode: TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> feed to Pact Broker as provider contract, no need to write consumer test boilerplate. AsyncAPI plugin covers Redpanda event contracts. Self-hosted broker: 1 Docker container + Postgres.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: pact-broker-docker + Postgres (homelab). BYO-3rd-party: Pactflow SaaS (same CLI tooling, just different broker URL).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (generate provider contract verification test stub per service from TypeSpec + AsyncAPI contracts)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Contract tests catch breaking API changes between patient-app/clinician-app consumers and backend services before they reach users; can-i-deploy gate blocks deploy of incompatible service versions.

adopt_now.name: Microsoft Presidio (Python service) + pii-vault (TS alternative)
adopt_now.what: PII/PHI detection and anonymization before LLM calls, log redaction, and audit scrubbing. Presidio = Python REST API (run as sidecar); pii-vault = pure TypeScript Presidio-compatible npm package (MIT, multi-language). Fills the PHI scrubbing requirement in curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule and the ConsentGuard/data-boundary rule before any LLM trace lands in Langfuse.
adopt_now.taxonomy: PII/PHI detection and anonymization before LLM calls, log redaction, and audit scrubbing. Presidio = Python REST API (run as sidecar); pii-vault = pure TypeScript Presidio-compatible npm package (MIT, multi-language). Fills the PHI scrubbing requirement in curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule and the ConsentGuard/data-boundary rule before any LLM trace lands in Langfuse.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Presidio: run as K8s sidecar (2 containers: analyzer + anonymizer); call via HTTP from NestJS services before emitting LLM traces to Langfuse. pii-vault: npm-import directly into NestJS if pure-TS is preferred; no Python sidecar needed. Wrapper needed: inject as NestJS provider via presidio-anonymizer-nestjs or custom service.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Presidio containers or pii-vault in-process. BYO-3rd-party: Azure AI Language PII detection service (same interface).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/observability or @curaos/phi-boundary package - extend existing healthstack-phi-boundary package with redaction pipeline)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Protects patient PHI from leaking into LLM telemetry/traces; critical for HIPAA compliance on HealthStack AI features and CDS steps.

adopt_now.name: Grafana k6 (v2.0)
adopt_now.what: Load/performance testing with SLO threshold codification. Already adopted in ops/perf with constant-arrival-rate baselines and SLO-mapped thresholds. k6 v2.0 (2026) adds AI-assisted test generation, Playwright browser module parity, and JSON summary output for CI consumption.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Load/performance testing with SLO threshold codification. Already adopted in ops/perf with constant-arrival-rate baselines and SLO-mapped thresholds. k6 v2.0 (2026) adds AI-assisted test generation, Playwright browser module parity, and JSON summary output for CI consumption.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Already real-working in ops/perf. Tests run as standalone binary (not imported into services); AGPL service boundary does not affect CuraOS application code. Results stream to Prometheus/Mimir via k6 Prometheus remote_write output.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: k6 binary in CI + ops/perf scripts. BYO-3rd-party: Grafana Cloud k6 for distributed load generation.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts already emits k6 baselines per service-catalog.json; extend to emit Playwright browser scenario stubs)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Validates that patient scheduling, billing, and appointment booking endpoints stay within SLO latency budgets under load.

adopt_now.name: Temporal TypeScript SDK + nestjs-temporal-core
adopt_now.what: Durable execution engine: deterministic workflow replay, signals, queries, SLA timers, human-task wait, exactly-once activity retry. Fills: workflow-core-service primary substrate (already adopted, M5 complete). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.sla
adopt_now.taxonomy: Durable execution engine: deterministic workflow replay, signals, queries, SLA timers, human-task wait, exactly-once activity retry. Fills: workflow-core-service primary substrate (already adopted, M5 complete). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.sla
adopt_now.license: MIT (SDK) + MIT (nestjs-temporal-core) + Server: MIT (self-host open source)
adopt_now.stack_fit: Already wired: @temporalio/* v1.17.2 + nestjs-temporal-core 3.2.8 in workflow-core-service package.json. CRITICAL gap: Temporal worker requires authentic Node.js - cannot run inside Bun process. Workaround in prod: run workers as a separate Node subprocess (not Bun) via bun:spawn or a sidecar container. Client/NestJS HTTP layer stays in Bun. No wrapper needed for client path; worker sidecar is the integration seam.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Temporal server via official Helm chart + PostgreSQL (CNPG already in stack). BYO-3rd-party: Temporal Cloud (managed). Both viable, both documented.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@temporalio/workflow + nestjs-temporal-core): codegen emitter in tools/codegen can scaffold workflow definition files + activity stubs from .tsp contract definitions
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Signals/queries enable real-time human-task inbox; SLA timer emits breach events that drive patient/customer notifications. Enables journey-centric approval flows (admit, prescribe, prior-auth) without polling hacks.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Downgrade from 'adopt' to 'confirmed-trial' because the Bun worker gap is a BLOCKING runtime defect in the current integration - the service as wired cannot start under Bun. Conditions for promotion to 'confirmed-adopt': (A) worker extracte
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: NO simpler rung covers the full requirement. BullMQ handles queues but not deterministic replay, saga versioning, SLA breach timers with resume, or human-in-loop await without polling. No stdlib or already-installed dep reaches durable execution. Temporal is the correct tool class. Ponytail ladder stops here: build the Node sidecar, not a from-scratch alternative.

adopt_now.name: pg-boss (timgit/pg-boss)
adopt_now.what: PostgreSQL-native job queue with SKIP LOCKED exactly-once delivery, dead-letter queues, scheduled/delayed jobs, ACID guarantees. No Redis. Fills: background task processing (claim submission batch, SLA check jobs, billing cycle runs, notification dispatch) without adding Redis to the stack. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.background-jobs, platform.workflow.scheduled-jobs
adopt_now.taxonomy: PostgreSQL-native job queue with SKIP LOCKED exactly-once delivery, dead-letter queues, scheduled/delayed jobs, ACID guarantees. No Redis. Fills: background task processing (claim submission batch, SLA check jobs, billing cycle runs, notification dispatch) without adding Redis to the stack. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.background-jobs, platform.workflow.scheduled-jobs
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure PostgreSQL - uses existing CNPG cluster, no extra infra. Drizzle-compatible (both use pg driver). NestJS adapters available. Bun-compatible (pure JS/TS, no native modules). 100-200 jobs/sec throughput ceiling vs BullMQ (thousands/sec) - fine for healthcare workflow volumes. Human-task pattern: pg-workflows extension adds step.waitFor() semantics on top of pg-boss.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (runs inside your Postgres). No external provider option - this IS the queue.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter can scaffold pg-boss queue handler registrations from TypeSpec async operation definitions
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Scheduled jobs drive proactive patient outreach (appointment reminders, medication refill nudges, care-gap alerts). No Redis ops burden simplifies self-hosted deployments.

adopt_now.name: XState v5 (statelyai/xstate)
adopt_now.what: Actor-model state machine / statechart library. Models per-entity finite state with guards, actions, parallel states, invoked services. Fills: per-encounter / per-claim / per-order state machine inside individual services (not a workflow orchestrator - complements Temporal). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.state-machine, platform.workflow.per-entity-state
adopt_now.taxonomy: Actor-model state machine / statechart library. Models per-entity finite state with guards, actions, parallel states, invoked services. Fills: per-encounter / per-claim / per-order state machine inside individual services (not a workflow orchestrator - complements Temporal). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.state-machine, platform.workflow.per-entity-state
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure TypeScript, Bun-compatible, no native dependencies. NestJS: inject actor as a provider, persist snapshot to Drizzle/Postgres on each transition. Deep-persistence snapshot format changed in v5 (migration concern for existing actors). Stately visual editor produces XState machine definitions (visual authoring path for simple state machines).
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party only (library in-process). No managed cloud requirement.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter can generate XState machine definitions from TypeSpec state enum + transition decorators
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Models patient/encounter lifecycle states (admitted, in-treatment, discharged) + order states (pending, approved, dispensed) as explicit finite automata - makes person journey state visible and auditable. Guards prevent invalid state transitions (safety).

adopt_now.name: Documenso (self-hosted e-sign service) + @documenso/sdk-typescript
adopt_now.what: Full-featured self-hosted DocuSign alternative with PKCS#12 digital signatures, multi-signer envelopes, embedded signing, webhooks, and eIDAS/UETA compliance. Fills the gap where esign-core-service already owns the contract layer but needs a BYO-3rd-party provider option (local_vs_3rdparty dual requirement). The sdk-typescript (MIT) is the api-adapter target. Covers taxonomy: erp.recurring.contracts esign surface + contract.signed_status projection.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Full-featured self-hosted DocuSign alternative with PKCS#12 digital signatures, multi-signer envelopes, embedded signing, webhooks, and eIDAS/UETA compliance. Fills the gap where esign-core-service already owns the contract layer but needs a BYO-3rd-party provider option (local_vs_3rdparty dual requirement). The sdk-typescript (MIT) is the api-adapter target. Covers taxonomy: erp.recurring.contracts esign surface + contract.signed_status projection.
adopt_now.license: Service: AGPL-3.0 (Community) / Commercial $250/mo Platform tier for AGPL escape. SDK (@documenso/sdk-typescript): MIT.
adopt_now.stack_fit: sdk-typescript is a plain fetch-based TS SDK - direct npm import in NestJS esign-core-service with no wrapper needed. Service deploy = Helm chart + PostgreSQL (CNPG already present). Zod validation wraps SDK response types at the service boundary.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: run Documenso container (AGPL CE, PKCS#12 signing passphrase + PostgreSQL required). BYO-3rd-party: point same sdk-typescript at Documenso Cloud or DocuSign/HelloSign via api-adapter shim in esign-core. Dual requirement satisfied.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @documenso/sdk-typescript injected via esign-core-service NestJS module as the 'remote provider' branch of the PdfRendererProvider port pattern already established in reports-service. Contract: esign.tsp already owns the SigningRequest/Signature shapes; the sdk bridges to the external service without changing the TypeSpec.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: In-app 'Review & sign' step exposed via esign-core ops for the My Agreements / contract-renewal person journey. Shareable self-serve signing link for non-account counterparties. Clinician/admin envelope console for multi-signer status, void, chain-of-custody audit.

adopt_now.name: Gotenberg (self-hosted PDF engine)
adopt_now.what: Docker-based stateless API wrapping Chromium + LibreOffice for HTML/CSS/Markdown -> PDF, DOCX -> PDF, PDF merge/split. Already the chosen PDF engine per ADR-0113 (InMemoryPdfRendererProvider stub confirms the real Gotenberg adapter is injected at modulith level). MIT license, 12k+ GitHub stars, version 8.32+ active. Fills: reports-service PDF rendering (erp.platform.documents 'present-strong'), AVS PDF render, CMS-1500/UB-04 paper claim forms, invoice PDF.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Docker-based stateless API wrapping Chromium + LibreOffice for HTML/CSS/Markdown -> PDF, DOCX -> PDF, PDF merge/split. Already the chosen PDF engine per ADR-0113 (InMemoryPdfRendererProvider stub confirms the real Gotenberg adapter is injected at modulith level). MIT license, 12k+ GitHub stars, version 8.32+ active. Fills: reports-service PDF rendering (erp.platform.documents 'present-strong'), AVS PDF render, CMS-1500/UB-04 paper claim forms, invoice PDF.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Already integrated via the PdfRendererProvider interface in reports-service. gotenberg-js-client npm package exists but is Gotenberg v6-era; direct fetch() calls to /forms/chromium/convert/html are simpler and already the pattern. No wrapper needed - the provider interface is the wrapper.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host primary: Helm chart + Docker image, air-gap safe (Zarf-packaged). BYO-3rd-party: swap the PdfRendererProvider NestJS port to point at any hosted HTML-to-PDF service (PDFBolt, WeasyPrint cloud) - the interface already exists in reports-service.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: Codegen-emitter-target: the reports-service GotenbergHttpAdapterProvider (the real adapter injected at modulith) should be emitted as part of service scaffold rather than hand-written each time. Port interface already in codegen mold shape.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Invisible infrastructure. Enables: patient AVS PDF download, invoice PDF email, CMS-1500 download in patient-app (transparency over own billing), clinical document export.

adopt_now.name: @pdfme/generator (JSON-template PDF generation)
adopt_now.what: MIT-licensed TypeScript-native PDF generator from JSON templates. Works in Node.js and browser. WYSIWYG template designer UI (@pdfme/ui) for business users to edit templates. Actively maintained: v6.1.6 released ~Jun 2026. Covers: form-fillable PDF templates for invoices, discharge summaries, consent forms, quotes - the 'template engine' slot separate from Gotenberg's HTML-render-to-PDF path. Taxonomy: erp.platform.documents template authoring + esign document prep.
adopt_now.taxonomy: MIT-licensed TypeScript-native PDF generator from JSON templates. Works in Node.js and browser. WYSIWYG template designer UI (@pdfme/ui) for business users to edit templates. Actively maintained: v6.1.6 released ~Jun 2026. Covers: form-fillable PDF templates for invoices, discharge summaries, consent forms, quotes - the 'template engine' slot separate from Gotenberg's HTML-render-to-PDF path. Taxonomy: erp.platform.documents template authoring + esign document prep.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct npm import in NestJS reports-service or a dedicated template-generator-service. `generate(template, inputs)` returns Uint8Array PDF bytes - pipe to SeaweedFS storage via storage-sdk. @pdfme/ui ships as a React component for the admin template designer - plugs directly into the ui-kit / admin web apps.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (npm import - no service dependency). No BYO-3rd-party equivalent needed; the template JSON stays in CuraOS storage.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @pdfme/generator imported in reports-service behind a TemplateRendererProvider port (complement to PdfRendererProvider). Template JSON stored per-tenant in storage-core. @pdfme/ui React component emitted by ui-app-emit for document-template admin screen.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables: tenant-branded invoice PDFs, discharge summary templates, consent form templates with fillable fields. Template designer in admin surface; person gets correctly branded, pre-populated document.
adopt_now.verify_notes:
CONDITIONAL TRIAL, not full adopt. Conditions before landing in generator-first codegen:

1. Wrap behind a PdfTemplateProvider interface in reports-service (or template-generator-service). Local impl = pdfme. 3rd-party slot = explicit stub
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: Gotenberg (already in CuraOS stack per candidate taxonomy) covers HTML-to-PDF with full CSS pixel-perfect fidelity. If the use case is templated documents, a simpler path is: Handlebars/Mustache HTML templates stored in storage-core, rendered to PDF by the existing Gotenberg service. No new dependency, no expression-eval attack surface, no React designer constraint. Tradeoff: no WYSIWYG admin designer (business users get a code template editor, not a drag-drop canvas). If the WYSIWYG designer is a real user requirement (clinical admins authoring consent form layouts without developer involvement), pdfme fills a genuine gap Gotenberg cannot. If templates are developer-authored and rarely changed, Gotenberg + HTML templates is simpler and already present.

adopt_now.name: Unstructured (self-hosted OCR/document ETL API)
adopt_now.what: Open-source Python/FastAPI service for partitioning 26+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, images, HTML, PPTX) into structured JSON elements with layout analysis, table detection, and multi-strategy OCR (hi_res/fast/ocr_only). Official unstructured-js-client (TypeScript) on npm. Self-hosted via Docker. Fills: document-core-service (clean slate) ingestion pipeline, OCR extraction for uploaded patient documents, consent form parsing, lab result PDF ingestion.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Open-source Python/FastAPI service for partitioning 26+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, images, HTML, PPTX) into structured JSON elements with layout analysis, table detection, and multi-strategy OCR (hi_res/fast/ocr_only). Official unstructured-js-client (TypeScript) on npm. Self-hosted via Docker. Fills: document-core-service (clean slate) ingestion pipeline, OCR extraction for uploaded patient documents, consent form parsing, lab result PDF ingestion.
adopt_now.license: Apache-2.0 (open source core). Unstructured Platform (enterprise) is commercial SaaS.
adopt_now.stack_fit: unstructured-js-client is a plain TypeScript HTTP client - direct import in NestJS document-core-service. Service URL is env-configurable (local or Platform API). Partition results are JSON arrays of typed elements (Title/NarrativeText/Table/Image) - Zod schemas wrap them at ingestion boundary.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: unstructured-api Docker container on K8s. BYO-3rd-party: point js-client at Unstructured Platform API (SaaS). Dual satisfied.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: Service: document-core-service (currently clean slate) becomes the owner of the Unstructured provider port. SDK pkg: @unstructured-io/unstructured-client (or js-client) imported as the ingestion provider. Emits document.partitioned events to Kafka for downstream consumers.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables: ingest scanned patient records into structured data (problems, meds, allergies from faxed/uploaded documents), parse insurance cards, extract lab values from PDF results for the patient-experience read model.

adopt_now.name: Meilisearch (Community Edition)
adopt_now.what: Rust-based full-text + hybrid (BM25 + vector/semantic) search engine with typo tolerance, facets, tenant tokens for multi-tenancy. Fills taxonomy gaps: platform.search (search-service FHIR-param mapping enrichment from weaker-than-source-enrichments), patient/record search UX (patient-search), and the FilterBar/SearchShell ui-kit archetype contract lint gate (ui-kit-archetypes). Covers the `search-service` local module.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Rust-based full-text + hybrid (BM25 + vector/semantic) search engine with typo tolerance, facets, tenant tokens for multi-tenancy. Fills taxonomy gaps: platform.search (search-service FHIR-param mapping enrichment from weaker-than-source-enrichments), patient/record search UX (patient-search), and the FilterBar/SearchShell ui-kit archetype contract lint gate (ui-kit-archetypes). Covers the `search-service` local module.
adopt_now.license: MIT (Community Edition, v1.28+ binary). Enterprise Edition (sharding only) = BUSL-1.1, production-requires commercial. CE is safe-permissive for self-host.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Official `meilisearch` npm package (TypeScript). `nestjs-meilisearch` v3.0.0 NestJS module available. Self-hosted single binary or Docker. Multi-tenant via signed tenant tokens (JWT, scoped filter_by rules). Hybrid search (BM25 + vector via OpenAI/HuggingFace embeddings) is stable since v1.9+; semanticRatio tunable. Integrates directly with search-service NestJS module via HTTP SDK. Drizzle/Postgres stays source of truth; Meilisearch is the index layer.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted CE (first-party, Zarf/Helm/K8s compatible). BYO: Meilisearch Cloud SaaS at meilisearch.com. Both supported.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/search-sdk wrapping meilisearch-js) + codegen-emitter (search-emit: index definition per .tsp entity, FHIR search-param mapping table, FilterBar contract lint gate)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Patient/user-facing instant search (sub-50ms) with plain-language typo tolerance. Tenant tokens scope each person to their own data. FHIR search-param semantics mappable on top of the index layer.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Verdict rationale - confirmed-trial, not confirmed-adopt. Adopt is blocked by: (1) CVE-2026-57823 directly undermines the tenant token isolation claim for patient data - must be confirmed resolved at the pinned version before patient-facing
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is already in the stack. PostgreSQL (CNPG, already deployed) with pg_search (ParadeDB BM25 extension) + pgvector delivers hybrid search (BM25 + vector) without adding a new stateful service. Benchmarks show PG GIN/BM25 at P50 4ms latency for boolean queries. ParadeDB pg_search is production-stable as of 2025, runs inside CNPG, and CNPG already has Citus for multi-tenancy. This eliminates: a new binary to air-gap, a new Helm chart, a sync layer, tenant token JWT management, an embeddings sidecar, and the CE/EE binary tracking burden. The ponytail ladder stops here (rung 4: native platform feature / already-installed dependency). The case for Meilisearch must justify why pg_search + pgvector inside existing CNPG is insufficient - specifically typo tolerance and sub-50ms instant-search UX. pg_search does not provide typo tolerance (fuzzy match via trigrams is possible but inferior). For patient-facing instant search with typo tolerance, Meilisearch's UX delta is real. For internal FHIR parameter search and FilterBar contract linting, pg_search likely covers it.

adopt_now.name: pgvector (PostgreSQL extension)
adopt_now.what: First-class vector search (HNSW + IVFFlat indexes) inside existing CNPG/PostgreSQL. Fills: RAG/semantic search on clinical records, FHIR-param hybrid ranking, terminology similarity, CDS cohort embedding queries. Gaps covered: vector dimension of search-service (weaker-than-source-enrichments FHIR search-param) and future CDS embeddings (cds-rules-engine analog). Taxonomy: platform.search, healthstack.quality (CDS embedding).
adopt_now.taxonomy: First-class vector search (HNSW + IVFFlat indexes) inside existing CNPG/PostgreSQL. Fills: RAG/semantic search on clinical records, FHIR-param hybrid ranking, terminology similarity, CDS cohort embedding queries. Gaps covered: vector dimension of search-service (weaker-than-source-enrichments FHIR search-param) and future CDS embeddings (cds-rules-engine analog). Taxonomy: platform.search, healthstack.quality (CDS embedding).
adopt_now.license: PostgreSQL License (permissive, equivalent to MIT/BSD). Extension hosted under pgvector MIT.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Zero new service: enabled as a CNPG PostgreSQL extension. Drizzle ORM supports vector column via drizzle-orm/pg-core vector() type. NestJS service calls Drizzle as normal. HNSW index: p95 ~2-5ms at 100K vectors, ~8-15ms at 1M vectors on 4vCPU/8GB. Sparse vector support (v0.9) enables BM25+dense hybrid within Postgres. No new operator required.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: CNPG extension (first-party). No BYO-3rd-party option needed - it IS Postgres. RDS/Neon/Supabase also offer it if cloud option is wanted.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (add vector() column to .tsp entities that opt in via @vectorEmbedded decorator; drizzle schema emit adds HNSW index migration)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables semantic 'find similar records', 'did you mean', personalized ranking on patient/user data without leaving Postgres boundary. PHI stays inside CNPG isolation.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Three concrete corrections to the candidate submission: (1) v0.9.0 does not exist as of 2026-06-29; latest is v0.8.3 - "sparse vector support in v0.9" is an inaccurate claim; sparse vector (halfvec/sparsevec) was added in 0.7.x-0.8.x range,
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: PostgreSQL full-text search (tsvector/tsquery + pg_trgm) already in CNPG covers keyword and fuzzy search without a new extension. For the CDS embedding and FHIR semantic ranking use case described, there is no simpler already-installed alternative - tsvector does not do dense vector similarity. pgvector is the minimum viable addition. Ponytail ladder stops here: no stdlib covers 768-dim cosine similarity at scale.

adopt_now.name: Apache Superset (embedded analytics)
adopt_now.what: Self-hosted BI/dashboard platform with embedded SDK (iframe + guest token + RLS). Fills: reports-service Superset-embed path (already referenced locally but gaps exist in multi-tenant RLS wiring + NestJS token minting). Covers taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (Superset-embed slice of reports-service), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards, crm-insurance-broker.platform.reporting. Does NOT replace the ReportDefinition/field-catalog generator gap - that stays in reports-sdk.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Self-hosted BI/dashboard platform with embedded SDK (iframe + guest token + RLS). Fills: reports-service Superset-embed path (already referenced locally but gaps exist in multi-tenant RLS wiring + NestJS token minting). Covers taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (Superset-embed slice of reports-service), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards, crm-insurance-broker.platform.reporting. Does NOT replace the ReportDefinition/field-catalog generator gap - that stays in reports-sdk.
adopt_now.stack_fit: NestJS backend calls POST /api/v1/security/guest_token/ with service-account credentials, returns short-lived JWT scoped to tenant dashboards + RLS clause (e.g. tenant_id='X'). Frontend embeds via @superset-ui/embedded-sdk iframe. Wrapper needed: NestJS SupersetService that mints tokens per request. Does NOT plug into Drizzle/TypeSpec directly - it reads from its own DB (Postgres) that mirrors or connects to the data warehouse layer.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: Docker/Helm/K8s (Zarf-packageable). BYO: Preset.io managed Superset. Both viable.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: service/ui-kit (SupersetService in reports-service + ui-app-emit ReportViewer archetype wired to embedded-sdk)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Person-facing 'my analytics' views (patient outcome trends, claim history charts, invoice aging) served as scoped guest-token dashboards. Management surface: full BI explorer for admin/clinical users. Same Superset instance, different guest token scopes.
adopt_now.verify_notes:
DOWNGRADE FROM ADOPT TO TRIAL. Three issues make "adopt" premature:

1. RLS security track record is non-trivial in a HIPAA-adjacent system. OX Security found an RLS bypass via subquery injection in v4.1.3 (users can reference tables outsid
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No drop-in simpler alternative exists for this specific use case. Grafana targets ops/metrics, not SQL-explorer BI; its multi-tenant embedded guest story is weaker. Metabase is simpler to operate but carries LGPL (commercial use requires paid Enterprise tier) and its embedded multi-tenant support is less mature. Scratch-build is infeasible at comparable feature depth. Superset is the correct pick on the build-vs-buy axis. The ponytail ladder stops here: no already-installed dep, no stdlib, no native platform feature covers embedded multi-tenant BI dashboards.

adopt_now.name: cql-execution (CQF/HL7 JS CQL engine)
adopt_now.what: JavaScript/TypeScript library for executing Clinical Quality Language (CQL) expressions against ELM JSON artifacts. Direct fill for ecqm-quality-engine gap (taxonomy: healthcare.ancillary.public-health-reporting, healthstack-quality-service). Covers eCQM measure execution, population-criteria eval (IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/etc), and FHIR-based CQL logic via cql-exec-fhir PatientSource. Identified in gap-analysis as the TS port target for the pophealth/HDS Ruby engine.
adopt_now.taxonomy: JavaScript/TypeScript library for executing Clinical Quality Language (CQL) expressions against ELM JSON artifacts. Direct fill for ecqm-quality-engine gap (taxonomy: healthcare.ancillary.public-health-reporting, healthstack-quality-service). Covers eCQM measure execution, population-criteria eval (IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/etc), and FHIR-based CQL logic via cql-exec-fhir PatientSource. Identified in gap-analysis as the TS port target for the pophealth/HDS Ruby engine.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Direct npm import into healthstack-quality-service. `cql-execution` + `cql-exec-fhir` (FHIR PatientSource) compose to evaluate any eCQM measure against FHIR R4 patient data. No NestJS wrapper needed beyond a QualityMeasureService class. Pairs with cqf-ruler (Java, run-as-background-service) for HQMF import and QRDA export - exactly the C-mode pattern the ecqm gap-analysis prescribes.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: npm-import (runs inside NestJS process). No self-hosted service needed for evaluation. CQF-Ruler (Java sidecar, Docker) handles HQMF/QRDA conversion per gap plan.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (encapsulate cql-execution + cql-exec-fhir in @curaos/quality-sdk; codegen-emitter adds measure contract stubs per .tsp quality entity)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Person-facing: 'my care gaps' card (consent-gated, derived from same quality engine). Management: population dashboard with IPP/DENOM/NUMER breakdowns, QRDA III submission, provider/practice quality scores.

adopt_now.name: cql-execution + cql-exec-fhir
adopt_now.what: HL7 CQL (Clinical Quality Language) execution engine in TypeScript/JS. Executes CQL ELM artifacts against FHIR R4 patient data. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS logic evaluation), healthcare.care.rules-engine (CQL rule expressions), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (eCQM population criteria IPP/DENOM/NUMER). cql-exec-fhir adds PatientSource from FHIR R4 bundles. Used by fqm-execution (MITRE), DEQM Test Server, and CQF Ruler underneath.
adopt_now.taxonomy: HL7 CQL (Clinical Quality Language) execution engine in TypeScript/JS. Executes CQL ELM artifacts against FHIR R4 patient data. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS logic evaluation), healthcare.care.rules-engine (CQL rule expressions), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (eCQM population criteria IPP/DENOM/NUMER). cql-exec-fhir adds PatientSource from FHIR R4 bundles. Used by fqm-execution (MITRE), DEQM Test Server, and CQF Ruler underneath.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure TS, no native deps. Drop into NestJS service as a module-scoped singleton. Input: compiled ELM JSON + FHIR R4 Bundle per patient. No Zod wrapper needed for invocation; Zod can validate ELM payload at API boundary. Bun-compatible (pure JS). Wrapper needed: thin NestJS provider that loads ELM from DB/FS and calls Library.execute().
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: embed in healthstack-quality-service, store ELM in Postgres. BYO-3rd-party: point PatientSource at external FHIR server (cqf-ruler or HAPI). Both paths use same npm package.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/cds-sdk wraps cql-execution + cql-exec-fhir); contract-typespec (healthstack-quality.tsp CQLLibrary + Measure resources); codegen emitter scaffolds NestJS CQL provider per measure.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: CQL authoring produces the logic that powers person-facing care-gap nudges and clinician CDS alerts from one engine. ELM artifacts are portable so a rule authored once surfaces on both patient-app (plain-language gap card) and clinician surface (QRDA population result).

adopt_now.name: fqm-execution (MITRE projecttacoma)
adopt_now.what: TypeScript eCQM calculation library wrapping cql-execution + cql-exec-fhir with FHIR-Measure-aware output: per-patient MeasureReport, QRDA Category I/III, care-gaps Bundle with DetectedIssue + GuidanceResponse, data-requirements analysis. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (eCQM engine), healthcare.interop.hl7 (QRDA Cat I/III), healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (care-gap surface). fqm-execution-service adds a Docker REST wrapper (POST /Measure/$evaluate-measure, /Measure/$care-gaps, /Measure/$data-requirements).
adopt_now.taxonomy: TypeScript eCQM calculation library wrapping cql-execution + cql-exec-fhir with FHIR-Measure-aware output: per-patient MeasureReport, QRDA Category I/III, care-gaps Bundle with DetectedIssue + GuidanceResponse, data-requirements analysis. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (eCQM engine), healthcare.interop.hl7 (QRDA Cat I/III), healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (care-gap surface). fqm-execution-service adds a Docker REST wrapper (POST /Measure/$evaluate-measure, /Measure/$care-gaps, /Measure/$data-requirements).
adopt_now.stack_fit: Full TypeScript, npm-import into healthstack-quality-service NestJS module. No native deps. Accepts FHIR Bundle (Measure + Libraries + ValueSets) + patient Bundles. Returns typed MeasureReport. Bun-compatible. For air-gap deployments run fqm-execution-service Docker alongside (port 3000) and call via HTTP from conversion-core.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: embed fqm-execution as npm dep in healthstack-quality-service. BYO-3rd-party: run fqm-execution-service Docker as a sidecar and point conversion-core at it via internal Kubernetes service.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/cds-sdk, wraps fqm-execution); contract-typespec (Measure + MeasureReport in healthstack-quality.tsp); codegen emitter generates the NestJS provider boilerplate and wires PatientSource from FHIR bundles.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: care-gaps endpoint returns DetectedIssue per gap + GuidanceResponse with data to close the gap - maps directly to patient-app 'my care gaps' card (consent-gated). Same engine drives the clinician population dashboard and QRDA submission.
adopt_now.verify_notes: Downgrade from adopt to confirmed-trial on three concrete concerns: (1) Bun runtime is unverified - the CuraOS rule [[curaos-bun-primary-rule]] makes this a blocker for direct npm-import mode until a smoke test proves Bun executes cql-execu
adopt_now.simpler_alternative: No simpler option exists. This is a narrow regulator-graded output domain (QRDA Cat I/III, FHIR MeasureReport, care-gaps Bundle with DetectedIssue + GuidanceResponse). The only other TS option is cqm-execution (QDM-based, older, less FHIR-native). Building from scratch against the CQL spec would dwarf the adoption cost by 10x+. Ponytail ladder stops here: no stdlib, no installed dep, no one-liner covers eCQM calculation + QRDA generation.

adopt_now.name: @gorules/zen-engine
