adopt_now.what: Rust-core business rules engine with Node.js NAPI bindings. Evaluates decision tables, expression graphs (ZEN Expression Language), and rule graphs stored as portable JSON (JDM - JSON Decision Model). Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.rules-engine (table-driven cohort-scoped clinical rules, no-code authoring surface), healthcare.care.alerts (rule output -> alert events). Replaces the port-adapt of vista-dashboard-rules C9C engine with a maintained npm package. Healthcare page on gorules.io lists eligibility, formulary, clinical workflow automation as explicit use cases. GoRules BRMS adds a visual authoring UI (self-hosted or cloud at EU50/month).
adopt_now.taxonomy: Rust-core business rules engine with Node.js NAPI bindings. Evaluates decision tables, expression graphs (ZEN Expression Language), and rule graphs stored as portable JSON (JDM - JSON Decision Model). Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.rules-engine (table-driven cohort-scoped clinical rules, no-code authoring surface), healthcare.care.alerts (rule output -> alert events). Replaces the port-adapt of vista-dashboard-rules C9C engine with a maintained npm package. Healthcare page on gorules.io lists eligibility, formulary, clinical workflow automation as explicit use cases. GoRules BRMS adds a visual authoring UI (self-hosted or cloud at EU50/month).
adopt_now.stack_fit: NAPI binary ships per-platform (linux-x64-gnu, linux-x64-musl, darwin-x64/arm64, win32). Bun uses Node.js NAPI compatibility layer - confirmed working for most NAPI-rs packages. Install: npm i @gorules/zen-engine. NestJS: inject ZenEngine as a provider, load JDM JSON from Postgres/S3 at startup, call engine.evaluate(decisionId, context) per patient context. No Zod wrapper needed for eval; Zod validates incoming context shape. JDM files are portable JSON - store in DB, edit via GoRules BRMS UI, version-control in repo.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: embed engine in-process in NestJS (zero network latency). BYO-3rd-party: optionally run GoRules BRMS self-hosted Docker for visual rule authoring UI + rule storage. Cloud BRMS (gorules.io) is opt-in, rule eval always stays in-process.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/cds-sdk registers ZenEngine as NestJS provider, emits typed evaluate() wrappers per rule category); contract-typespec (RuleDefinition + AlertEvent in healthstack-quality.tsp); codegen emitter generates JDM template stubs per domain.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Clinical rules stored as JDM tables (e.g., drug contraindication lookup, immunization reminder schedule, risk-score coefficient table) can be evaluated per patient context and produce structured alert/recommendation output. Person-facing nudge (patient-app) and clinician alert both consume the same rule output event. No-code authoring via GoRules BRMS lets clinical staff edit rules without code deploys.

adopt_now.name: CDS Hooks specification (protocol reference, no mature npm library)
adopt_now.what: HL7 CDS Hooks v2.0 is the wire protocol for synchronous, workflow-triggered CDS. Defines hook types (patient-view, order-select, order-sign, encounter-discharge) and JSON Card response format (info, suggestion, app-link). Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (hook dispatch protocol). No maintained npm library exists - implement as a NestJS controller handling POST /cds-services/{hookId}. Existing npm packages (@asymmetrik/node-cds-hooks, cds-hooks) are stagnant.
adopt_now.taxonomy: HL7 CDS Hooks v2.0 is the wire protocol for synchronous, workflow-triggered CDS. Defines hook types (patient-view, order-select, order-sign, encounter-discharge) and JSON Card response format (info, suggestion, app-link). Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (hook dispatch protocol). No maintained npm library exists - implement as a NestJS controller handling POST /cds-services/{hookId}. Existing npm packages (@asymmetrik/node-cds-hooks, cds-hooks) are stagnant.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Implement CDS Hooks responder as a NestJS controller: validate JWT (JWKS from EHR), parse hook context (FHIR R4 prefetch bundle), call internal rule engines (ZEN / cql-execution / fqm-execution), return Card[] response. Zod schema validates incoming hook payload and outgoing Card. No npm library needed; spec is simple enough (one POST endpoint + Card schema) to implement in ~150 LOC.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: NestJS CDS Hooks handler (first-party). BYO-3rd-party: point NestJS at cqf-ruler /cds-services as the upstream resolver.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (CdsHookRequest + Card schema in healthstack-cds.tsp); codegen emitter generates typed NestJS controller stub per hook type; @curaos/cds-sdk exposes Card builder helpers.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: CDS Hooks Cards surface at point-of-care (prescribing, order sign). Suggestions in Cards can trigger patient-facing tasks/nudges via automation-core event emission after clinician action.

adopt_now.name: Novu (@novu/framework + @novu/react)
adopt_now.what: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
adopt_now.license: MIT (core) + commercial Enterprise Edition (SSO, audit logs)
adopt_now.stack_fit: @novu/framework/nest ships NovuModule.register() - zero wrapper needed for NestJS. @novu/react drops directly into CuraOS ui-kit. backendUrl+socketUrl overrides point React component at self-hosted API. Bun runtime works (bun compile is separate issue, not relevant for service usage). TypeSpec event contract can emit Novu workflow trigger shapes.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose + community Helm chart (not official). BYO-3rd-party: Novu Cloud ($30/mo per 30k runs). Both paths use identical SDK + workflow code - full [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] compliance.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@novu/framework): workflow definitions go in notify-core-service, emitted by codegen mold as .workflow.ts files alongside TypeSpec contracts. @novu/react plugs into ui-kit as InboxWidget.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Preference center per subscriber (person controls which channels/types they receive). In-app Inbox is person-facing real-time feed. Notification history queryable by subscriber. Strong dual-surface fit: person sees their feed; management surface triggers and monitors workflows.

adopt_now.name: react-email (@react-email/render + @react-email/components)
adopt_now.what: JSX-to-HTML email rendering library: write email templates as React/TSX components, render to HTML string for any SMTP/HTTP provider. Covers email template authoring layer for notify-core-service and healthstack notification surfaces (appointment reminders, care plan updates, clinical alerts). Not a delivery service - pure rendering.
adopt_now.taxonomy: JSX-to-HTML email rendering library: write email templates as React/TSX components, render to HTML string for any SMTP/HTTP provider. Covers email template authoring layer for notify-core-service and healthstack notification surfaces (appointment reminders, care plan updates, clinical alerts). Not a delivery service - pure rendering.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Pure npm import, zero framework coupling. Works with NestJS + nodemailer. Known issue: does not work with `bun compile` binary (issue #2585 open) - irrelevant for service mode (Bun-as-runtime works fine). Turborepo/monorepo compatible. Templates live in @curaos/email-templates package, shared across services.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Provider-agnostic by design: render to HTML, send via Postal (self-host), Resend, SES, or any SMTP. Covers both local and 3rd-party delivery.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target: the ui-app-emit or a new email-emit step generates .email.tsx template stubs from TypeSpec entity contracts, the same way it generates page stubs.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Templates can be authored with person-centric language and dynamic data (patient name, appointment detail, care instructions). Components reusable across patient-app emails and business-side alerts with the same design system.

adopt_now.name: Postal (self-hosted SMTP relay + HTTP API)
adopt_now.what: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
adopt_now.taxonomy: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
adopt_now.stack_fit: No Node SDK needed - Postal exposes HTTP API; wrap in a thin NestJS provider (PostalProvider implements EmailProvider interface) using `fetch` or axios. Zarf-packageable as a container. Works alongside CuraOS's Postgres CNPG (Postal uses MySQL internally - no conflict, separate service).
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Local: Postal self-hosted container. BYO-3rd-party: swap PostalProvider for ResendProvider/SES/Mailgun - same NestJS interface. Full dual compliance.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service EmailProvider port. Codegen emits PostalProvider class from a provider config schema in TypeSpec.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Delivery infrastructure only - person-facing value is reliable, tracked email delivery. Bounce/complaint suppression protects patient email from accidental spam-folder landing.

adopt_now.name: listmonk (self-hosted newsletter + transactional email)
adopt_now.what: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
adopt_now.stack_fit: AGPL service boundary: CuraOS calls listmonk's REST API from notify-core-service, never imports code. Postgres-native (shares CNPG PostgreSQL cluster or separate instance). Zarf-packageable. No npm SDK but HTTP client wrapper is trivial - 50 lines in a NestJS ListmonkProvider.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only for listmonk itself; but it relays via any SMTP/provider (Postal, SES, Mailgun). Covers local self-host path; BYO-3rd-party is the SMTP relay config.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service ListmonkProvider and list-seeding scripts. TypeSpec contract emits campaign trigger events consumed by listmonk webhook handler.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Patient/customer opt-in/out management per list per person. Double opt-in flows protect patient autonomy. Subscription preference portal can be surfaced in patient-app as communication preferences. Unsubscribe is first-class, not an afterthought.

adopt_now.name: ntfy (self-hosted push notification server)
adopt_now.what: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Trivial HTTP integration from NestJS: POST to ntfy topic = push sent. No SDK required; if desired, a 20-line NestJS NtfyProvider wraps it. Works as Novu provider adapter so Novu orchestrates the channel while ntfy handles delivery. Zarf-packageable (single Go binary container).
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: ntfy container. BYO-3rd-party: configure Novu's FCM/APNs provider instead - same NestJS interface. iOS relay mode in ntfy itself bridges to Apple APNs without requiring a self-hosted APNs cert.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service PushProvider port. Can also be a Novu custom provider definition.
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Person installs ntfy app and subscribes to their patient-specific topic (scoped by tenant+patient ID with ACL). Push reaches them without CuraOS holding their FCM token. Topic-level unsubscribe is person-controlled.

adopt_now.name: pocket-id
adopt_now.what: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
adopt_now.stack_fit: NestJS consumes JWKS + standard OIDC tokens via @nestjs/passport + passport-jwt (no wrapper needed). Pocket-id REST API for user/group management. LDAP sync available. No NestJS-specific SDK but standard OIDC protocol = zero wrapper friction.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (no BYO-3rd-party option -- no cloud variant). Meets the local-first charter; BYO-3rd-party would use Keycloak or Zitadel path instead.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen emitter should emit JWKS-aware JWT guard decorator + SMART scope decorators from TypeSpec contract rather than per-service hand-coded guards
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first auth is the best person-centric auth UX (no password, phishing-resistant). Persons authenticate once via Face ID/Touch ID; identity flows downstream to patient-app, business apps, clinician-app via OIDC. Aligns with person-owns-their-identity directive.

adopt_now.name: Keycloak
adopt_now.what: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
adopt_now.taxonomy: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
adopt_now.stack_fit: nest-keycloak-connect npm adapter (guards, decorators, resource/scope protection). Multi-tenant via realm-per-tenant or per-org realm resolver. JWKS endpoint for standard JWT validation. Wrapper needed for multi-tenant realm routing but documented pattern exists.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm chart, K8s operator) AND can be used as BYO-3rd-party (managed Keycloak from Red Hat, cloud IAM providers). Satisfies [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] dual requirement.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emitter should generate Keycloak-compatible resource+scope declarations from TypeSpec contract ops, not hand-code resource_access claims per service
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Passkey/FIDO2 native (26.4+), social login, self-service account console for persons to manage their credentials, MFA. WebAuthn conditional UI now in default login forms. Directly supports person-owns-their-auth-devices.

adopt_now.name: @simplewebauthn/server
adopt_now.what: TypeScript-first WebAuthn/FIDO2/passkey library for Node.js. Handles registration ceremony (attestation parsing, CBOR decoding) and authentication ceremony (assertion verification, signature check) entirely in-process. Fills: passkey WebAuthn in identity-service for non-pocket-id deployments or local dev. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authn primitive).
adopt_now.taxonomy: TypeScript-first WebAuthn/FIDO2/passkey library for Node.js. Handles registration ceremony (attestation parsing, CBOR decoding) and authentication ceremony (assertion verification, signature check) entirely in-process. Fills: passkey WebAuthn in identity-service for non-pocket-id deployments or local dev. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authn primitive).
adopt_now.stack_fit: npm-import into identity-service. Drizzle schema for passkey credential table documented. NestJS + PostgreSQL demo repo exists. Zero wrapper needed -- pure TypeScript, Bun-compatible. Plug into existing identity-service NestJS guards.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host always (in-process library, no external service). Complements pocket-id (which also uses FIDO2 but as a standalone IdP).
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen should emit passkey registration/authentication endpoint stubs + Drizzle credential table schema as part of identity-service scaffold
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Enables passkey-first authentication (Touch ID, Face ID, hardware keys) as the primary person login method -- no password, phishing-resistant, best-in-class UX.

adopt_now.name: Cerbos
adopt_now.what: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
adopt_now.stack_fit: cerbos npm SDK + nestjs-cerbos interceptor pattern. NestJS guard calls Cerbos PDP over gRPC with principal attributes + resource attributes; PDP evaluates YAML policy. ABAC lets policies use tenant context, clinical role, HIPAA consent flags. No wrapper beyond the SDK guard pattern (already documented).
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host PDP (open-source, Docker/Helm). Hub (policy management UI) is SaaS-optional; air-gap deployments use local bundle endpoint. Meets dual requirement: self-host PDP always available, Hub as optional BYO-cloud control plane.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec contract resource ops should generate Cerbos resource definitions + policy scaffolds (derived resource.yaml per service), injected into service scaffold by ui-app-emit / emitServiceLive
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Centralizes who-can-see-what logic, enabling privacy-by-policy (clinician only sees consented patient records, person only sees their own data, HIPAA field-level rules as policy YAML). Simplifies adding consent-aware access rules without touching every service.

adopt_now.name: rrule-temporal
adopt_now.what: RFC-5545 + RFC-7529 recurrence rule expansion returning Temporal.ZonedDateTime; backend recurrence engine for CuraOS calendar_event.rrule storage and slot expansion. Fills gap: recurring event expansion in calendar-core-service and scheduling-service (taxonomy: calendar.recurring-events). Direct successor to unmaintained rrule.js, Temporal-native, tz-correct by construction.
adopt_now.taxonomy: RFC-5545 + RFC-7529 recurrence rule expansion returning Temporal.ZonedDateTime; backend recurrence engine for CuraOS calendar_event.rrule storage and slot expansion. Fills gap: recurring event expansion in calendar-core-service and scheduling-service (taxonomy: calendar.recurring-events). Direct successor to unmaintained rrule.js, Temporal-native, tz-correct by construction.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Drop-in NestJS import, no wrapper needed. Works with Bun runtime. Requires @js-temporal/polyfill on Node < Temporal-native; Node 22 may have partial native support. Pair with Luxon (already in calendar-core) for display formatting. Store RRULE strings in Drizzle text column, expand at read time.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg - @curaos/calendar-sdk (expand rrule strings in SDK layer so all consumers get tz-correct occurrences without re-implementing expansion)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Powers patient-facing 'my appointments' recurring view, clinician recurring schedule blocks, and hr leave accrual recurrence. Invisible infrastructure; surfaces as correct dates on person-app calendar.

adopt_now.name: react-big-calendar
adopt_now.what: GCal/Outlook-style React calendar component: month/week/day/agenda views, drag-drop, multi-date-library adapters, events management. Fills the CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard UI archetype gap in @curaos/ui (taxonomy: ui-kit-archetypes). Person-facing calendar views (patient-app my-appointments, clinician daily schedule) and management calendar (scheduling admin, CRM activity calendar).
adopt_now.taxonomy: GCal/Outlook-style React calendar component: month/week/day/agenda views, drag-drop, multi-date-library adapters, events management. Fills the CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard UI archetype gap in @curaos/ui (taxonomy: ui-kit-archetypes). Person-facing calendar views (patient-app my-appointments, clinician daily schedule) and management calendar (scheduling admin, CRM activity calendar).
adopt_now.stack_fit: React-native, no wrapper needed. TypeScript types via @types/react-big-calendar (v1.16.3). Adapts to date-fns or Luxon (already installed). Drop into @curaos/ui as the CalendarBoard primitive - generators can emit the CalendarBoard archetype wired to calendar-sdk hooks. No resource view (multi-provider/room) natively.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-kit (@curaos/ui CalendarBoard component wrapping react-big-calendar, wired to calendar-sdk event feed; ui-app-emit emits CalendarBoard archetype for calendar-connected entities)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Patient-app: my-appointments calendar, clinician-app: daily/weekly schedule. CRM: activity timeline calendar view. Personal-calendar app: full calendar view. Strong person-centric fit for all journey surfaces.

adopt_now.name: PostgreSQL tstzrange + EXCLUDE constraint (native platform feature)
adopt_now.what: DB-level double-booking prevention using PostgreSQL exclusion constraints on tstzrange. Eliminates the need for an application-level scheduling engine for conflict detection. Fills: resource conflict guard in scheduling-service and business-scheduling-service (any multi-resource booking). Example: EXCLUDE USING gist (resource_id WITH =, tstzrange(start_at, end_at) WITH &&) prevents overlapping bookings at the DB layer.
adopt_now.taxonomy: DB-level double-booking prevention using PostgreSQL exclusion constraints on tstzrange. Eliminates the need for an application-level scheduling engine for conflict detection. Fills: resource conflict guard in scheduling-service and business-scheduling-service (any multi-resource booking). Example: EXCLUDE USING gist (resource_id WITH =, tstzrange(start_at, end_at) WITH &&) prevents overlapping bookings at the DB layer.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Drizzle ORM supports custom SQL for exclusion constraints via sql`` template. CNPG runs standard PostgreSQL with btree_gist extension (required for tstzrange exclusion). Zero new npm deps. Emit the exclusion constraint from the Drizzle schema generator.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
adopt_now.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (drizzle schema emitter adds tstzrange exclusion constraint for any entity with resource_id + time range fields)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Invisible to person - prevents the bad experience of double-booked appointments. Management surface: booking conflict errors surface as clear 409 responses.

adopt_now.name: Luxon (already installed)
adopt_now.what: Timezone-aware date manipulation library, already in use in calendar-core-service (defaultTzid IANA resolution). No new dep. For backend scheduling logic: slot generation, duration arithmetic, IANA timezone conversion, DST-safe period calculations. Fills: tz-correct slot computation in scheduling-service.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Timezone-aware date manipulation library, already in use in calendar-core-service (defaultTzid IANA resolution). No new dep. For backend scheduling logic: slot generation, duration arithmetic, IANA timezone conversion, DST-safe period calculations. Fills: tz-correct slot computation in scheduling-service.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Already installed in calendar-core. Immutable, IANA tz via Intl API, Bun-compatible. Use for slot generation arithmetic in scheduling-service. Pair with rrule-temporal (which returns Temporal.ZonedDateTime) via a thin adapter or migrate slot logic to Temporal directly.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
adopt_now.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (already wired into calendar-core; extend to scheduling-sdk slot generation)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Powers correct appointment times in the person's local timezone on patient-app.

adopt_now.name: next-runtime-env
adopt_now.what: Runtime injection of NEXT_PUBLIC_* and next.config-derived values without rebuild. Adds a PublicEnvScript component to root layout that reads process.env per-request (SSR), populates window.__ENV on client. Directly fixes the #840/ADR-0220 blocker: allowedOrigins, CSP nonces, and any NEXT_PUBLIC_ value become runtime-injectable without touching the build. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator gap - runtime config pattern.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Runtime injection of NEXT_PUBLIC_* and next.config-derived values without rebuild. Adds a PublicEnvScript component to root layout that reads process.env per-request (SSR), populates window.__ENV on client. Directly fixes the #840/ADR-0220 blocker: allowedOrigins, CSP nonces, and any NEXT_PUBLIC_ value become runtime-injectable without touching the build. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator gap - runtime config pattern.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Drop-in for Next.js App Router. One PublicEnvScript in root layout.tsx, env() helper replaces process.env on client. Generator (ui-app-emit) emits it once in the layout template - zero per-route changes. No Bun/NestJS/Drizzle wrapper needed.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (reads from container env); no 3rd-party dependency
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (root layout.tsx template)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: Invisible to end users; enables correct per-tenant config injection (tenant-specific API base URLs, CSP) which directly supports multi-tenant person-centric surfaces

adopt_now.name: Next.js with #840 fix (stay-nextjs path)
adopt_now.what: Keep all 24 apps on Next.js standalone; fix the NEXT_PUBLIC bake problem via next-runtime-env + generator-native PublicEnvScript in ui-app-emit root layout template; fix allowedOrigins/CSP by reading from process.env in a custom Next.js server wrapper (instrumentation.ts or server.js) that patches next.config at startup. This is the direct fix for the blocker that triggered the decision. No framework migration risk. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator template fix.
adopt_now.taxonomy: Keep all 24 apps on Next.js standalone; fix the NEXT_PUBLIC bake problem via next-runtime-env + generator-native PublicEnvScript in ui-app-emit root layout template; fix allowedOrigins/CSP by reading from process.env in a custom Next.js server wrapper (instrumentation.ts or server.js) that patches next.config at startup. This is the direct fix for the blocker that triggered the decision. No framework migration risk. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator template fix.
adopt_now.stack_fit: Zero friction - existing 24 apps unchanged except generator template update. ui-app-emit adds PublicEnvScript to root layout.tsx template. allowedOrigins fix: move to runtime-read in a custom server.js that next() wraps. Bun standalone: Next.js standalone is NOT a single binary (requires node_modules dir); image size remains 2-3 GB unless manually slimmed.
adopt_now.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (standalone output); no Vercel required. Existing Helm/Zarf packaging unchanged.
adopt_now.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (root layout.tsx template + custom server.js template)
adopt_now.person_centric_fit: No change to person-centric surfaces. Existing App Router RSC pattern already in use for patient/personal apps.

trial.name: FullCalendar (standard/MIT) + react-big-calendar
trial.what: Two MIT-licensed options for CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard archetype (gap `ui-kit-archetypes`). FullCalendar Standard: month/week/day/list views, event drag-resize, resource grouping (non-resource views are MIT; Timeline/Resource views are premium $480/dev/yr). react-big-calendar: simpler, no premium tier, 1.20.0 actively maintained (last publish 24 days ago). Needed for: appointment scheduling (HealthStack), staff/resource scheduling, calendar-based task views.
trial.taxonomy: Two MIT-licensed options for CalendarBoard/SchedulingBoard archetype (gap `ui-kit-archetypes`). FullCalendar Standard: month/week/day/list views, event drag-resize, resource grouping (non-resource views are MIT; Timeline/Resource views are premium $480/dev/yr). react-big-calendar: simpler, no premium tier, 1.20.0 actively maintained (last publish 24 days ago). Needed for: appointment scheduling (HealthStack), staff/resource scheduling, calendar-based task views.
trial.stack_fit: Both React 19 compatible. FullCalendar @fullcalendar/react is MIT wrapper. react-big-calendar uses moment.js or date-fns as localizer - use date-fns to avoid moment bundle weight. RTL support limited in both; needs wrapper.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted npm. Air-gap safe for MIT tier.
trial.generator_first_target: ui-kit package (@curaos/ui) - CalendarBoard component wraps one of these; ui-app-emit emits calendar archetype for contracts with dateTime + assignee fields
trial.person_centric_fit: Patient-facing: my appointments calendar, medication schedule. Provider-facing: scheduling board with resource columns (exam rooms, providers). Management: staff roster calendar.

trial.name: RJSF (react-jsonschema-form) v6
trial.what: JSON Schema + uiSchema -> React form renderer with validation (AJV8 default, swappable). Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` schema-driven form generation and `platform-reporting-builder` dynamic field configuration. Direct path to emitting forms from TypeSpec-derived JSON Schema contracts: TypeSpec -> OpenAPI schema -> JSON Schema subset -> RJSF renders the form automatically. Needed for: dynamic workflow form steps, admin config screens, report builder field selectors.
trial.taxonomy: JSON Schema + uiSchema -> React form renderer with validation (AJV8 default, swappable). Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` schema-driven form generation and `platform-reporting-builder` dynamic field configuration. Direct path to emitting forms from TypeSpec-derived JSON Schema contracts: TypeSpec -> OpenAPI schema -> JSON Schema subset -> RJSF renders the form automatically. Needed for: dynamic workflow form steps, admin config screens, report builder field selectors.
trial.stack_fit: TypeSpec contracts produce JSON Schema compatible output - direct pipeline to RJSF. Zod 4 schemas can be converted to JSON Schema via zod-to-json-schema. Needs a shadcn/ui theme adapter (build once in @curaos/ui). AJV8 validator swappable for lighter custom validator.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted npm + schema files in repo. Air-gap safe.
trial.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target: ui-app-emit can emit RJSF-based screen instead of hand-coded form when contract is purely data-capture (no complex UX logic)
trial.person_centric_fit: Person-facing: dynamic intake forms (patient registration, insurance enrollment, loan application) generated from service contracts without per-form UI code. Management: admin config forms for workflow steps, report definitions, connector settings.

trial.name: AG Grid Community
trial.what: Headless-optional data grid with enterprise-grade features in free tier: infinite scroll, server-side row model, pivot, column grouping, export (CSV), cell editing, row virtualization, master-detail rows. Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` high-density ledger/billing grid screens (claims list, AR aging, GL journal entries) where TanStack Table v8 custom virtualization would be significant work. Community = MIT/Apache-2; Enterprise = $999/dev/yr (Excel export, range select, advanced pivot, row grouping tree).
trial.taxonomy: Headless-optional data grid with enterprise-grade features in free tier: infinite scroll, server-side row model, pivot, column grouping, export (CSV), cell editing, row virtualization, master-detail rows. Covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` high-density ledger/billing grid screens (claims list, AR aging, GL journal entries) where TanStack Table v8 custom virtualization would be significant work. Community = MIT/Apache-2; Enterprise = $999/dev/yr (Excel export, range select, advanced pivot, row grouping tree).
trial.stack_fit: React 19 adapter available. Works with Tailwind for theming via CSS variables. No NestJS coupling needed. Heavy bundle (~500KB) - use dynamic import for ledger-only screens. Pairs with TanStack Query for server-side data fetching.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Community: self-hosted npm, air-gap safe. Enterprise: license key required, not air-gap friendly without prior arrangement.
trial.generator_first_target: ui-kit package (@curaos/ui) - LedgerGrid component wraps AG Grid Community; ui-app-emit emits ledger archetype for contracts with financial line-item fields
trial.person_centric_fit: Primarily management surface: billing ledger, claims list, GL journal, inventory grid. Person-facing uses lighter components (TanStack Table + Virtual). Recommendation: use AG Grid Community only for the densest management data grids where TanStack Table + Virtual is not sufficient out of the box.

trial.name: Trigger.dev v4 (@trigger.dev/sdk + @trigger.dev/nestjs)
trial.what: Apache 2.0 durable task execution platform: retried long-running jobs, schedules, event-triggered tasks, real-time observability dashboard, self-host via Docker/K8s. Fills the execution durability gap for automation-core-service scheduled triggers and plugin-runtime-service async jobs - both confirmed stubs. @trigger.dev/nestjs is the official NestJS module.
trial.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 durable task execution platform: retried long-running jobs, schedules, event-triggered tasks, real-time observability dashboard, self-host via Docker/K8s. Fills the execution durability gap for automation-core-service scheduled triggers and plugin-runtime-service async jobs - both confirmed stubs. @trigger.dev/nestjs is the official NestJS module.
trial.stack_fit: Bun runtime supported in v4. @trigger.dev/nestjs module for DI integration. Self-host on Postgres+Redis (same infra as CuraOS). Complements BullMQ already in use for recurrence cadence - Trigger.dev is the durability layer above BullMQ for multi-step workflows.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (no mandatory SaaS dependency when self-hosted). Cloud option available for BYO-infra teams.
trial.generator_first_target: service: automation-core-service trigger dispatcher wires to Trigger.dev task definitions; SDK pkg: @trigger.dev/nestjs injected into automation-core NestJS module
trial.person_centric_fit: Long-running automations (maternity risk eval, claim submission, insurance eligibility check) run durably without timing out; person sees real-time job status.

trial.name: Candid Health (candidhealth npm SDK)
trial.what: SaaS RCM clearinghouse with a generated TypeScript SDK (candid-node). Handles claims submission, eligibility checks (via Availity/Stedi passthrough), ERA auto-posting, and denial workflows through a REST API. Covers taxonomy x12-revenue-cycle as a BYO-3rd-party clearinghouse option. SDK is MIT; the service behind it is commercial SaaS.
trial.taxonomy: SaaS RCM clearinghouse with a generated TypeScript SDK (candid-node). Handles claims submission, eligibility checks (via Availity/Stedi passthrough), ERA auto-posting, and denial workflows through a REST API. Covers taxonomy x12-revenue-cycle as a BYO-3rd-party clearinghouse option. SDK is MIT; the service behind it is commercial SaaS.
trial.stack_fit: TypeScript SDK imports cleanly into NestJS. Wrap in a clearinghouse-port interface so the self-host path (direct X12 via node-x12) and the BYO-Candid path are swappable. Zod can validate the Candid response shapes. Drizzle stores the canonical claim records; Candid is the transport.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: 3rd-party only (SaaS). No self-host option. However, CuraOS [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] requires both paths: the Candid adapter IS the BYO-3rd-party leg; the @curaos/x12-sdk direct-clearinghouse path IS the self-host/local leg.
trial.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-claims-service payer-adapter port); config-driven clearinghouse registry in codegen.
trial.person_centric_fit: Candid's eligibility API directly enables real-time patient copay estimates. Their ERA auto-posting closes the loop on patient balance after adjudication - both feed the patient-app cost-transparency surface.
trial.verify_notes: Air-gap hard fail: Candid is cloud-only (staging + prod, vendor-hosted). Zero self-hosted or offline option. Air-gap tenants get nothing from this adapter. The local leg (node-x12 + direct clearinghouse) must be complete and production-capa
trial.simpler_alternative: Stedi: programmable clearinghouse TypeScript SDK, drop-in Change Healthcare replacement, $70M Series B (Sep 2025), no bundled RCM workflow lock-in. Better-scoped for CuraOS which owns its own claim workflow. node-x12 (already planned) covers the self-host/local/air-gap leg. Candid is superior only when customer already contracts Candid for full RCM services.

trial.name: Stedi (programmable clearinghouse + EDI Inspector)
trial.what: API-first commercial clearinghouse with free EDI Inspector tool and open-source awesome-edi resources. Stedi's production API provides X12 translate (EDI to JSON), eligibility (270/271), and claims (837/835) via REST - the BYO-clearinghouse option for CuraOS. EDI Inspector at edi.stedi.com is a free validation tool useful for @curaos/x12-sdk conformance testing during development.
trial.taxonomy: API-first commercial clearinghouse with free EDI Inspector tool and open-source awesome-edi resources. Stedi's production API provides X12 translate (EDI to JSON), eligibility (270/271), and claims (837/835) via REST - the BYO-clearinghouse option for CuraOS. EDI Inspector at edi.stedi.com is a free validation tool useful for @curaos/x12-sdk conformance testing during development.
trial.stack_fit: REST adapter in NestJS HttpModule or Axios. No official Node SDK - thin wrapper needed. JSON output from Stedi translate API maps cleanly to TypeSpec billing contracts. Useful as conformance validator for @curaos/x12-sdk test suite even if not used in prod.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: 3rd-party SaaS only for production clearinghouse. EDI Bootstrap is self-hostable as a format converter (no payer connectivity). Stedi is the strongest BYO-clearinghouse option: largest payer network, developer-first docs, REST not SFTP-only.
trial.generator_first_target: Service adapter (healthstack-claims-service, payer-port config); EDI Inspector for @curaos/x12-sdk snapshot test fixtures.
trial.person_centric_fit: REST-based 270/271 enables sub-second eligibility checks at point-of-scheduling - feeds patient copay estimate display on patient-app before appointment confirmation.

trial.name: Availity REST API (HIPAA transactions)
trial.what: Clearinghouse REST API with eligibility (270/271), claims status (276/277), and claim attachments - backed by the largest clearinghouse network in the US (8.8M daily transactions). Covers the BYO-3rd-party payer-connectivity leg of taxonomy x12-revenue-cycle. No official Node SDK but REST API is documented. Candid Health's eligibility passthrough uses Availity internally.
trial.taxonomy: Clearinghouse REST API with eligibility (270/271), claims status (276/277), and claim attachments - backed by the largest clearinghouse network in the US (8.8M daily transactions). Covers the BYO-3rd-party payer-connectivity leg of taxonomy x12-revenue-cycle. No official Node SDK but REST API is documented. Candid Health's eligibility passthrough uses Availity internally.
trial.stack_fit: NestJS HttpModule adapter. OAuth2 auth flow. No TS types provided - generate from their OpenAPI spec if available, else hand-write Zod schemas for response validation.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: 3rd-party only. No self-host. The strongest option for direct payer eligibility coverage in US. Air-gap incompatible.
trial.generator_first_target: Service adapter (healthstack-claims-service, payer-port config registry).
trial.person_centric_fit: Real-time eligibility is the prerequisite for showing a patient their estimated out-of-pocket cost before the visit. Widest payer coverage means more patients get accurate estimates.

trial.name: Lago (self-hosted billing engine)
trial.what: Open-source metering + usage-based billing + subscription management + invoicing platform. Fills erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions). Covers: event ingestion, usage metering, plan/charge definitions, subscription lifecycle, invoice generation, dunning, payment orchestration, revenue analytics.
trial.taxonomy: Open-source metering + usage-based billing + subscription management + invoicing platform. Fills erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions). Covers: event ingestion, usage metering, plan/charge definitions, subscription lifecycle, invoice generation, dunning, payment orchestration, revenue analytics.
trial.stack_fit: AGPL: never import Lago code into CuraOS build. Integration via REST SDK (@getlago/lago-node). NestJS accounting-core-service calls Lago REST API as a billing-state sidecar. Helm chart available for K8s air-gap deployment. Lago manages billing state; CuraOS maps Lago invoices back to accounting-core GL via event subscription. Zod validation on Lago response DTOs needed.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Lago pod in same K8s cluster (self-hosted lane) OR configure to call Lago Cloud (BYO-3rd-party lane). Satisfies dual requirement from [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
trial.generator_first_target: accounting.tsp (subscription/invoice contract wraps Lago events); @curaos/lago-adapter package (thin NestJS module wrapping Lago Node SDK with Zod validation); codegen emitter for subscription-core-service.
trial.person_centric_fit: Person sees clean invoice + subscription self-management ('my plan', 'my usage this month', 'cancel or upgrade'). Lago's invoice PDF + webhook feed CuraOS personal-app billing view.
trial.verify_notes: Corrections to candidate before trial: (1) SDK package name: lago-javascript-client (not @getlago/lago-node). Update codegen generator target. (2) ClickHouse and Kafka are scaling add-ons, not baseline requirements - trial starts with Postg
trial.simpler_alternative: No credible simpler alternative covers the same surface. Flexprice (Go backend, AGPL-3.0 backend / Apache 2.0 frontend, ~3.5k stars) is younger and thinner. Kill Bill is Java/Apache 2.0 but architecturally heavier and decade-old design. Building usage metering + subscription lifecycle + invoice generation + dunning from scratch in NestJS is a multi-month L effort with high correctness risk (billing math, proration, tax, idempotency). Lago at trial scope (REST sidecar + adapter module) is the minimum-viable path. Ponytail ladder stops at rung 5: already-available dependency that solves it, integrated via thin adapter. No custom billing engine needed.

trial.name: EspoCRM (BYO sidecar + REST client)
trial.what: Full-featured CRM with clean JSON entity definitions (Lead, Contact, Opportunity, Case, Meeting, InboundEmail) that map 1:1 to the gap-analysis crm-insurance-broker taxonomy. GPL-3.0 PHP backend. Fills crm-insurance-broker.sales.leads, .contacts, .opportunities, .service.cases-tickets via BYO-3rd-party path. Primary CuraOS value: (A) run as BYO-3rd-party CRM sidecar for operators who already use EspoCRM; (B) its entityDefs JSON are the cleanest reference data models for CuraOS crm.tsp contract design (directly cited in source-to-local-map.json as best_source for Lead, Opportunity, Case).
trial.taxonomy: Full-featured CRM with clean JSON entity definitions (Lead, Contact, Opportunity, Case, Meeting, InboundEmail) that map 1:1 to the gap-analysis crm-insurance-broker taxonomy. GPL-3.0 PHP backend. Fills crm-insurance-broker.sales.leads, .contacts, .opportunities, .service.cases-tickets via BYO-3rd-party path. Primary CuraOS value: (A) run as BYO-3rd-party CRM sidecar for operators who already use EspoCRM; (B) its entityDefs JSON are the cleanest reference data models for CuraOS crm.tsp contract design (directly cited in source-to-local-map.json as best_source for Lead, Opportunity, Case).
trial.stack_fit: PHP backend - not importable into NestJS. Integration is via documented REST API + the official Node.js client (espocrm/api-client-javascript). Thin NestJS adapter service wraps the client behind crm.tsp interface. No Bun issues for the HTTP client.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: BYO-3rd-party (self-hosted EspoCRM instance; CuraOS adapts via REST API)
trial.generator_first_target: api-adapter (crm-core-service BYO adapter module; entityDefs JSON as reference for contract-typespec crm.tsp design)
trial.person_centric_fit: EspoCRM is org-centric. Value is as a BYO operator sidecar and as a data-model reference. Person-centric surface entirely on CuraOS side.
trial.verify_notes: Three corrections required in source-to-local-map.json before this proceeds to implementation: (1) license field: GPL-3.0 -> AGPLv3. (2) js-client claim: "official Node.js client" -> "documentation-only code snippet, no npm package, write a
trial.simpler_alternative: Checked ponytail ladder. No already-installed CuraOS module covers Lead/Opportunity/Case BYO-CRM sidecar integration today. The crm-core-service is a gap in the current service inventory (crm-insurance lane). Rolling a generic REST adapter for ANY external CRM is ~100-150 lines of NestJS module + Zod response shapes. That is likely comparable effort to an EspoCRM-specific adapter given the JS client is not packaged. However, the entityDefs reference value (best_source for TypeSpec contract design) is real and hard to replicate without a reference implementation. Alternative: skip EspoCRM as a sidecar target entirely and design crm.tsp contracts from first principles using industry-standard CRM field taxonomies (Salesforce SOQL, HubSpot v3 API, or IATA standards). That path removes the AGPLv3 dependency on the reference entirely and produces a more portable contract. That is worth noting but is a design question above this candidate's scope.

trial.name: GrapesJS (@grapesjs/react)
trial.what: BSD-3-Clause web builder framework rewritten in TypeScript; official React wrapper @grapesjs/react. Covers `ui-kit-archetypes` gap for email/notification template authoring and page template builder surface inside builder-core. Plugin ecosystem for blocks/panels/commands. Generator-first target: notify-service NotificationTemplate authoring UI + builder-core template surface.
trial.taxonomy: BSD-3-Clause web builder framework rewritten in TypeScript; official React wrapper @grapesjs/react. Covers `ui-kit-archetypes` gap for email/notification template authoring and page template builder surface inside builder-core. Plugin ecosystem for blocks/panels/commands. Generator-first target: notify-service NotificationTemplate authoring UI + builder-core template surface.
trial.stack_fit: React 18/19 compatible via @grapesjs/react wrapper. Outputs HTML/CSS or JSON. Heavier than Puck (~200KB). Better for HTML email template editing (notify-service) than for app-page composition where Puck is cleaner. No Bun/NestJS/Drizzle coupling.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host; core is BSD, no cloud required. Studio SDK is an optional commercial upgrade for a richer no-code experience.
trial.generator_first_target: notify-service NotificationTemplate (drag-drop template editor) + builder-core HTML template surface type
trial.person_centric_fit: Template authoring is a management surface (admins author email/SMS/notification templates). Person receives the rendered output. Full template variable/locale management preserved on management surface; person gets correctly personalized comms.
trial.verify_notes: 1. SCOPE CORRECTION: Candidate names @grapesjs/react (official wrapper, ~3700 downloads/week, last release ~Oct 2024, React 19 NOT confirmed fixed in OSS wrapper). Codebase uses grapesjs-react (community wrapper, v4.0.3 in node_modules). Th
trial.simpler_alternative: PONYTAIL LADDER HIT: grapesjs (core) + grapesjs-react are ALREADY INSTALLED in the monorepo (curaos/frontend/apps/builder-studio, grapesjs@^0.22.13, grapesjs-react@^4.0.2) and already in production use for the SitesCanvas surface. This is rung 2 - already in codebase. No new dependency needed. The candidate's @grapesjs/react wrapper (official, ~3700 weekly downloads, lower maturity) should be evaluated against the already-in-use grapesjs-react@4.0.x before switching. EmailBuilder.js (MIT, usewaypoint, 468 stars) is a simpler JSON-first alternative specifically for email/notification templates - lighter, no interop bugs, clean JSON output schema that fits notify-service better than HTML/CSS blob. Worth evaluating for the notify-service use case specifically before extending GrapesJS there.

trial.name: Activepieces (self-hosted service)
trial.what: MIT workflow automation platform (Zapier alternative). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (connector registry, AI-agent step, MCP servers, pause-resume, test-step). 280+ pieces auto-become MCP servers. createPiece pattern is the direct prior art for @curaos/connector-sdk. NOT an npm-importable React SDK - embedding is via iframe or API. Generator-first target: @curaos/connector-sdk port of createPiece pattern; automation-core-service connector registry.
trial.taxonomy: MIT workflow automation platform (Zapier alternative). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (connector registry, AI-agent step, MCP servers, pause-resume, test-step). 280+ pieces auto-become MCP servers. createPiece pattern is the direct prior art for @curaos/connector-sdk. NOT an npm-importable React SDK - embedding is via iframe or API. Generator-first target: @curaos/connector-sdk port of createPiece pattern; automation-core-service connector registry.
trial.stack_fit: Can run as a self-hosted sidecar automation engine (BYO-3rd-party mode) OR its createPiece/pause-resume source is port-adapted into @curaos/connector-sdk (first-party self-hosted mode). Iframe embed for the visual builder. REST/webhook API for workflow trigger from NestJS. No native NestJS SDK but REST coupling is straightforward.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Activepieces as the automation engine (BYO-3rd-party option) OR port createPiece into @curaos/connector-sdk for a fully first-party connector registry. curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule satisfied.
trial.generator_first_target: @curaos/connector-sdk (port createPiece pattern) + automation-core-service AiAgentStep/McpServer contracts
trial.person_centric_fit: Person sees 'my connections' + 'my automations' + 'waiting on you' inbox derived from automation runs. Management sees full flow designer + connector catalog + run logs. Person-centric: pre-built journey templates; management: full no-code canvas.

trial.name: deck.gl
trial.what: WebGL data visualization layers (ScatterplotLayer, TripsLayer, HeatmapLayer, PathLayer) composited over MapLibre GL. Covers the dense-dataset visualization gap in curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.fleet-tracking: fleet live positions (hundreds of vehicles), delivery route paths, geofence heatmaps in the management dashboard.
trial.taxonomy: WebGL data visualization layers (ScatterplotLayer, TripsLayer, HeatmapLayer, PathLayer) composited over MapLibre GL. Covers the dense-dataset visualization gap in curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.fleet-tracking: fleet live positions (hundreds of vehicles), delivery route paths, geofence heatmaps in the management dashboard.
trial.stack_fit: npm-import into @curaos/ui alongside react-map-gl. DeckGL React component wraps into MapView archetype when 'fleet-density' or 'trip-viz' hint is set. No server-side changes. Bun-compatible.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: frontend rendering only - agnostic to tile source; works with self-hosted or BYO
trial.generator_first_target: ui-kit MapView archetype variant 'fleet-density' or 'trip-layer' -> emits DeckGL layers atop MapLibre base
trial.person_centric_fit: Primarily management surface (fleet dispatcher heatmap, route density analytics). Person surface limited to own trip history replay - marginal but non-zero.
trial.verify_notes: Staged adoption path: (1) Add MapLibre to ui-kit as the MapView base - this is the real first decision. (2) Implement fleet live positions and geofence heatmaps using MapLibre native layers. (3) If live dispatcher repaint rate jank is measu
trial.simpler_alternative: MapLibre GL JS native heatmap layer and native clustering cover the low-to-mid density scenario (hundreds of vehicles as markers, geofence heatmaps via style layers) without deck.gl. MapLibre is uninstalled too, so both options have the same zero-dep baseline today. The ponytail ladder says: install MapLibre first and use its native layers. Escalate to deck.gl only when live-update repaint rate causes measurable jank (MapLibre is documented as slow on frequent repaints) or when TripsLayer animated replay is a hard requirement. deck.gl earns its keep at the animated/high-density tier only.

trial.name: DeepEval
trial.what: LLM evaluation framework with 50+ metrics (hallucination, faithfulness, answer relevancy, toxicity, bias). TypeScript SDK released 2026. Fills eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule alongside Langfuse. Used for CI-gated LLM quality checks on CDS rules, automation-core AI steps, and any LLM-generated content.
trial.taxonomy: LLM evaluation framework with 50+ metrics (hallucination, faithfulness, answer relevancy, toxicity, bias). TypeScript SDK released 2026. Fills eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule alongside Langfuse. Used for CI-gated LLM quality checks on CDS rules, automation-core AI steps, and any LLM-generated content.
trial.stack_fit: Python SDK: run as CI step or NestJS subprocess call for eval. TS SDK: npm-import into test files (Vitest/Bun test). Integrates with OTel and LiteLLM. Langfuse + DeepEval combination is the canonical CuraOS agent-eval-obs stack per curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule. Wrapper needed: thin NestJS provider to invoke DeepEval eval endpoint in integration tests.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: run Python eval runner in CI (local). BYO-3rd-party: Confident AI cloud for centralized eval dashboard.
trial.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (generate LLM-eval test stub alongside service integration tests for ai-llm-step services)
trial.person_centric_fit: Eval guards prevent hallucinated CDS recommendations or incorrect medication information from reaching clinicians and patients.

trial.name: Trigger.dev v3
trial.what: Apache-2.0 durable background job + workflow platform with first-class human-in-the-loop (Waitpoints: pause task until human approves/rejects), NestJS SDK, self-host via Docker/K8s + PostgreSQL. Fills: BYO-3rd-party escape valve for teams that want managed workflow without Temporal Cloud; also fills long-running background task lane. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.background-jobs
trial.taxonomy: Apache-2.0 durable background job + workflow platform with first-class human-in-the-loop (Waitpoints: pause task until human approves/rejects), NestJS SDK, self-host via Docker/K8s + PostgreSQL. Fills: BYO-3rd-party escape valve for teams that want managed workflow without Temporal Cloud; also fills long-running background task lane. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.background-jobs
trial.stack_fit: @trigger.dev/sdk + @trigger.dev/nestjs. Tasks defined as TypeScript functions decorated with task(). Bun-compatible client path (task scheduling). Worker runs on dedicated isolated compute - same Bun/Node boundary issue as Temporal for worker runtime but managed by Trigger.dev's own runner. Self-host adds a Trigger.dev server pod + PostgreSQL + Redis. No Drizzle/Zod conflicts.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose / Helm + PostgreSQL + Redis, all open source under Apache-2.0. BYO-3rd-party: Trigger.dev Cloud. Both with identical feature set (no feature gate on self-host).
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter could scaffold task() definitions from TypeSpec operation decorators for approval/notification workflows
trial.person_centric_fit: Waitpoints are a natural fit for patient/customer journey approvals (prior auth, claim review, care plan sign-off) - task literally pauses and resumes on human signal. Observability dashboard shows person journey progress.

trial.name: paed01/bpmn-engine
trial.what: Pure JavaScript/Node BPMN 2.0 execution engine (no Java, no server). Runs BPMN XML process definitions, supports UserTask (human-in-the-loop), ServiceTask, Gateway, Timer events, boundary events, signal events. Fills: BPMN standard process definition execution for builder-core-service visual workflow authoring; healthstack-workflow-service clinical pathway execution. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.bpmn-execution, platform.workflow.visual-authoring
trial.taxonomy: Pure JavaScript/Node BPMN 2.0 execution engine (no Java, no server). Runs BPMN XML process definitions, supports UserTask (human-in-the-loop), ServiceTask, Gateway, Timer events, boundary events, signal events. Fills: BPMN standard process definition execution for builder-core-service visual workflow authoring; healthstack-workflow-service clinical pathway execution. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.bpmn-execution, platform.workflow.visual-authoring
trial.stack_fit: Pure JS, Bun-compatible. No native modules. Embeds directly in NestJS service as a module. State persistence is caller-managed (inject custom state store - Drizzle/Postgres fits naturally). TypeScript types available. Pairs with bpmn-js (frontend designer) for the builder-core-service no-code authoring surface. UserTask suspends execution and exposes signal() / resume() API matching Temporal signal pattern.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party only - library runs in-process. No managed cloud option. This covers the BPMN-standard definition language; Temporal handles durability of execution underneath.
trial.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target: codegen can generate BPMN XML skeletons from TypeSpec workflow definitions, enabling contract-first BPMN authoring
trial.person_centric_fit: BPMN UserTask maps naturally to patient consent flows, clinical approval steps, prior-auth sequences. Visual BPMN diagrams are auditable process evidence (HIPAA/compliance value). Builder-core authoring surface lets non-developers define care pathways.

trial.name: @react-pdf/renderer (React-component PDF generation)
trial.what: MIT-licensed React-component-based PDF generator. 15.9k stars, v4.5.1 (2025), React 19 support since v4.1.0. Enables building PDF documents with JSX + familiar CSS-subset styling. Server-side rendering via renderToStream/renderToBuffer. Fills: programmatic PDF generation where the template is code (e.g., AVS report, complex multi-section clinical document) rather than a JSON template. Complement to pdfme for code-driven documents.
trial.taxonomy: MIT-licensed React-component-based PDF generator. 15.9k stars, v4.5.1 (2025), React 19 support since v4.1.0. Enables building PDF documents with JSX + familiar CSS-subset styling. Server-side rendering via renderToStream/renderToBuffer. Fills: programmatic PDF generation where the template is code (e.g., AVS report, complex multi-section clinical document) rather than a JSON template. Complement to pdfme for code-driven documents.
trial.stack_fit: Imports in NestJS service or dedicated PDF worker. renderToBuffer() returns Buffer - pipe to storage-sdk. No DOM required - server-side safe. Known packaging quirks with Bun (not official support but community reports working). Wrap in a try/catch with fallback to Gotenberg for complex rendering.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (npm import).
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: imported in reports-service alongside Gotenberg. AVS Composition template renders via react-pdf; standard HTML invoice via Gotenberg. The PdfRendererProvider port can dispatch by document type.
trial.person_centric_fit: Best for: AVS (After-Visit Summary) PDF with dynamic clinical sections, patient discharge reports, branded certificates. Code-driven = easy to internationalize (RTL) and add conditional sections.

trial.name: Carbone (document template engine)
trial.what: npm-importable report/document generator: injects JSON into ODT/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF templates, outputs any of those formats. Core concept: template files with {{variable}} markers, rendered server-side. Fills: contract/invoice/report template generation from DOCX templates (non-developer business users manage templates in Word/LibreOffice). v3.8.2 on npm (Jun 2026), actively maintained.
trial.taxonomy: npm-importable report/document generator: injects JSON into ODT/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF templates, outputs any of those formats. Core concept: template files with {{variable}} markers, rendered server-side. Fills: contract/invoice/report template generation from DOCX templates (non-developer business users manage templates in Word/LibreOffice). v3.8.2 on npm (Jun 2026), actively maintained.
trial.license: Carbone Community License (CCL) - free for internal use and embedding in value-added products. FORBIDDEN as a hosted document-generator-as-a-service offering (i.e., selling Carbone generation as a service).
trial.stack_fit: Direct npm import in NestJS. `carbone.render(template, data, options, callback)` - wrappable as an async provider. DOCX/ODT templates stored in storage-core. LibreOffice required for DOCX-to-PDF conversion (already in Gotenberg container - can reuse via Gotenberg's LibreOffice route instead of bundling LibreOffice separately).
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: npm import into NestJS service (CCL allows embedding in product). BYO-3rd-party: Carbone Cloud API (commercial). CCL restriction: CuraOS as a SaaS must confirm it is not reselling document generation as the primary product offering - legal review needed.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: Carbone imported as a 'DocxTemplateRenderer' provider alongside pdfme (JSON templates) and Gotenberg (HTML-to-PDF). Template storage managed by documents-core-service.
trial.person_centric_fit: Business template rendering for: contracts, insurance policies, quotes. Business users manage templates in Word without developer involvement. Person receives a branded, personalized PDF document.

trial.name: Qdrant
trial.what: Rust vector DB with payload filtering, sparse+dense hybrid search, and tenant isolation via payload index. Fills the vector search gap when pgvector is insufficient (>5M vectors, higher recall requirements, dedicated vector service). Covers: taxonomy platform.search (semantic dimension), CDS embedding queries, terminology similarity beyond SNOMED tree. Upgrade path from pgvector, not the starting point.
trial.taxonomy: Rust vector DB with payload filtering, sparse+dense hybrid search, and tenant isolation via payload index. Fills the vector search gap when pgvector is insufficient (>5M vectors, higher recall requirements, dedicated vector service). Covers: taxonomy platform.search (semantic dimension), CDS embedding queries, terminology similarity beyond SNOMED tree. Upgrade path from pgvector, not the starting point.
trial.stack_fit: Official `@qdrant/js-client-rest` (Apache-2.0, TypeScript). NestJS wrapper as a thin service module. Payload filtering is part of the indexing pipeline (unlike Weaviate post-filter) = fast filtered vector search. Operates alongside Postgres: Postgres is source of truth, Qdrant is the vector index. Benchmark P99 at 10M vectors: ~12ms vs Weaviate ~16ms, Milvus ~18ms.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted single binary/Docker/Helm (Zarf-packageable). Qdrant Cloud as BYO cloud option.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/vector-sdk wrapping @qdrant/js-client-rest, switchable with pgvector adapter)
trial.person_centric_fit: Semantic 'find similar clinical notes', 'recommended care actions', 'similar patients' (PHI-gated). Person-facing: 'related health topics' search. Management: cohort similarity for quality measures.

trial.name: Cube (semantic layer / headless BI)
trial.what: Open-source TypeScript+Rust semantic layer: defines metrics, dimensions, joins once in code; exposes SQL, REST, GraphQL, and MCP APIs to downstream consumers (Superset, embedded charts, AI agents). Fills: the field-catalog-from-contracts generator gap in platform-reporting-builder (ReportDefinition field resolution) and the semantic consistency layer between reports-service and Superset. Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (semantic/field-catalog slice).
trial.taxonomy: Open-source TypeScript+Rust semantic layer: defines metrics, dimensions, joins once in code; exposes SQL, REST, GraphQL, and MCP APIs to downstream consumers (Superset, embedded charts, AI agents). Fills: the field-catalog-from-contracts generator gap in platform-reporting-builder (ReportDefinition field resolution) and the semantic consistency layer between reports-service and Superset. Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (semantic/field-catalog slice).
trial.license: Apache 2.0 (Cube Core). Cube Cloud = commercial SaaS.
trial.stack_fit: NestJS calls Cube REST/SQL API to resolve metric queries. Cube data model in TypeScript (cube.js files) defines entities and joins from the same Postgres DB that Drizzle manages. Pre-aggregations cached in CubeStore (Rust). Superset can connect to Cube via SQL API as a data source. No Drizzle-native integration but Postgres compatibility means Cube reads same tables. TypeSpec contract -> Cube data model generation is a generator gap that can be filled.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted Docker/Helm (Zarf-packageable). Cube Cloud as BYO managed option.
trial.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (cube-emit: generate .js cube data model stubs from .tsp entity contracts; wires into reports-service field-catalog generator closure)
trial.person_centric_fit: Person-facing KPI home ('my metrics': my outstanding balance, my care gaps count, my order status) served via Cube metric definitions scoped by tenant/user. Management: full BI metric governance preventing definitional drift across services.

trial.name: cqf-ruler (cqframework / HAPI FHIR)
trial.what: Self-hosted FHIR R4 server (HAPI JPA) with embedded CQL evaluation engine, CDS Hooks endpoint (/cds-services), Measure/$evaluate-measure, PlanDefinition/$apply, and ActivityDefinition/$apply operations. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS Hooks service + PlanDefinition apply), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (Measure evaluation), healthcare.interop.fhir (knowledge artifact repository). Acts as the BYO-3rd-party CDS provider - NestJS services POST to /cds-services/{id} and consume Card responses.
trial.taxonomy: Self-hosted FHIR R4 server (HAPI JPA) with embedded CQL evaluation engine, CDS Hooks endpoint (/cds-services), Measure/$evaluate-measure, PlanDefinition/$apply, and ActivityDefinition/$apply operations. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS Hooks service + PlanDefinition apply), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (Measure evaluation), healthcare.interop.fhir (knowledge artifact repository). Acts as the BYO-3rd-party CDS provider - NestJS services POST to /cds-services/{id} and consume Card responses.
trial.stack_fit: NestJS api-adapter: create a CdsHooksClient service that wraps HTTP POST to cqf-ruler /cds-services/{hookId}. Zod validates Card response shape. Deploy cqf-ruler as a Kubernetes Deployment (docker pull alphora/cqf-ruler). No Java in CuraOS build - purely runtime sidecar. Integrates with CNPG Postgres via HAPI JPA config.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: run cqf-ruler as k8s sidecar in healthstack namespace. BYO-3rd-party: point CdsHooksClient at a customer-provided SMART-on-FHIR CDS server (Epic, Cerner, etc.).
trial.generator_first_target: service (healthstack-cds-service): NestJS api-adapter service wrapping cqf-ruler HTTP; @curaos/cds-sdk exposes typed CdsHooksClient; Helm chart for cqf-ruler sidecar deployment.
trial.person_centric_fit: PlanDefinition/$apply can generate CarePlan + Task resources directly - consumable by patient-app care plan view. CDS Hooks Cards return patient-facing suggestions (InfoCard, SuggestionCard) that map to person nudges. Clinician surface receives full card deck (links, suggestions, overrides).

trial.name: json-rules-engine (CacheControl)
trial.what: Pure JSON rule engine for Node.js. Conditions (all/any/not), facts (sync or async), events. Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.rules-engine (simple table-driven reminders, eligibility conditions, care-gap predicates) as a lightweight in-process fallback when CQL is overkill. No FHIR awareness; facts are arbitrary JS objects.
trial.taxonomy: Pure JSON rule engine for Node.js. Conditions (all/any/not), facts (sync or async), events. Fills taxonomy gap: healthcare.care.rules-engine (simple table-driven reminders, eligibility conditions, care-gap predicates) as a lightweight in-process fallback when CQL is overkill. No FHIR awareness; facts are arbitrary JS objects.
trial.stack_fit: Zero deps, pure TS. NestJS: inject Engine as provider, load rules from DB at startup. Rules are plain JSON - store in Postgres, edit via builder-core no-code UI. Bun-compatible. Zod validates rule schema at ingestion boundary.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (embedded in-process). No SaaS option.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/cds-sdk, thin wrapper exposing typed RuleEngine factory); codegen emitter generates rule schema JSON stubs per entity type.
trial.person_centric_fit: Can drive simple person-facing nudges (immunization due, screening overdue) where full CQL is not warranted. Fast to author new rules for clinical staff via builder-core JSON editor.

trial.name: Element/Matrix (Synapse + matrix-js-sdk)
trial.what: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
trial.taxonomy: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
trial.stack_fit: matrix-js-sdk imports into NestJS for bot/bridge operations (programmatic message send from clinical workflows). Element Web embeds in CuraOS React frontend via iframe or as a standalone app. Significant ops footprint (Synapse + Postgres + media server). Not Zarf-trivial but Helm chart exists.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Synapse on CuraOS K8s. BYO-3rd-party: Matrix.org public homeserver or Element Cloud for teams that don't need on-prem. Federation means both options interoperate.
trial.generator_first_target: service: a new secure-messaging-service wraps matrix-js-sdk for server-side operations (room creation, bot messages for system notifications). UI: Element Web or a matrix-react-sdk embedding in patient-app.
trial.person_centric_fit: Patient owns their Matrix account (portable across homeservers). E2E encryption means even CuraOS cannot read messages at rest. Person-controlled key verification. Care team visible to patient as participants, not hidden from them.

trial.name: Dittofeed (open-source lifecycle messaging)
trial.what: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
trial.taxonomy: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
trial.stack_fit: TypeScript-native, Node.js SDK drops into NestJS as a service client. However, the self-host stack adds ClickHouse + Temporal on top of Postgres - significant infra expansion. For CuraOS v1 scope this is likely deferred. REST API usable without SDK.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose / Helm (lite mode: Postgres only, no ClickHouse). BYO-3rd-party: Dittofeed Cloud. Both paths use same SDK.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@dittofeed/sdk-node): notify-core-service tracks events to Dittofeed for journey triggers. TypeSpec events map to Dittofeed track() calls in service-seed emit.
trial.person_centric_fit: Journey builder models patient/user lifecycle (onboarding, re-engagement, care adherence). Person-level opt-out and preferences. Segment by clinical attributes (diagnosis group, care plan status) for targeted communications.

trial.name: Zitadel
trial.what: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
trial.taxonomy: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
trial.stack_fit: OIDC standard tokens consumed by NestJS via passport-jwt/JWKS. gRPC management API has Go/TypeScript SDKs. TypeScript Actions for lifecycle hooks. No native NestJS module but standard OIDC = minimal wrapper. Multi-org maps cleanly to CuraOS tenant model.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm, K8s) + managed Zitadel Cloud. Satisfies dual requirement -- but AGPL means if CuraOS modifies Zitadel source and exposes as SaaS, disclosure required.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emit Zitadel resource registration API calls from TypeSpec contract during codegen scaffold
trial.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first, self-service account management, GDPR-ready event log. Multi-org means each patient/customer org can have their own IdP branding/settings.

trial.name: OpenFGA
trial.what: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
trial.taxonomy: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
trial.stack_fit: @openfga/sdk npm + @rxap/nest-openfga module. NestJS guard delegates to OpenFGA check API. Tuples stored in Postgres (which CNPG already manages). Fits well for document/care-plan/consent record ownership. ABAC support is newer and less mature than ReBAC.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Docker/Helm) + FGA.dev managed cloud. Satisfies dual requirement.
trial.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec resource types should emit OpenFGA type definitions; SDK emit should generate NestJS FGA guard + tuple write helpers per resource type
