trial.person_centric_fit: ReBAC naturally encodes person-owns-their-record, patient-can-read-their-own-clinical-document, org-member-can-access-shared-resource. Consent as a relationship tuple is a clean model.

trial.name: better-auth
trial.what: TypeScript-first in-process auth framework. Plugin-based: passkey, OIDC provider (now OAuth 2.1 provider in v1.5+), multi-tenant organizations, Drizzle adapter, NestJS integration documented. Could replace or complement identity-service session/token layer. Fills: lightweight embedded auth for personal-* services or dev environment. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (lightweight embedded tier).
trial.taxonomy: TypeScript-first in-process auth framework. Plugin-based: passkey, OIDC provider (now OAuth 2.1 provider in v1.5+), multi-tenant organizations, Drizzle adapter, NestJS integration documented. Could replace or complement identity-service session/token layer. Fills: lightweight embedded auth for personal-* services or dev environment. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (lightweight embedded tier).
trial.stack_fit: Native Drizzle adapter (import from 'better-auth/adapters/drizzle'). NestJS integration: disable NestJS body parser, mount better-auth handler. Passkey plugin + OAuth 2.1 provider plugin cover authn + IdP needs in one npm package. Bun-compatible (framework-agnostic design).
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host always (in-process). No cloud variant -- all data stays in your Postgres via Drizzle. Perfect for air-gap.
trial.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen scaffold for identity-service should emit better-auth initialization + Drizzle schema migration + NestJS auth module wiring as optional embedded-auth mode
trial.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first (pre-auth registration in 1.6), magic-link, social login all available. OAuth 2.1 provider means CuraOS can issue its own tokens to patient-app and business apps without a separate IdP service for simpler deployments.

trial.name: FullCalendar (standard, MIT plugins)
trial.what: Mature multi-framework calendar: month/week/day/agenda/timegrid views, drag-drop, recurring events via rrule plugin, TS support. Standard plugins are MIT. Premium plugins (Timeline/Resource) are commercial ($480/dev). For CuraOS: standard tier covers CalendarBoard archetype and most scheduling views. Resource timeline (multi-provider, multi-room) needs premium.
trial.taxonomy: Mature multi-framework calendar: month/week/day/agenda/timegrid views, drag-drop, recurring events via rrule plugin, TS support. Standard plugins are MIT. Premium plugins (Timeline/Resource) are commercial ($480/dev). For CuraOS: standard tier covers CalendarBoard archetype and most scheduling views. Resource timeline (multi-provider, multi-room) needs premium.
trial.stack_fit: @fullcalendar/react wraps core cleanly. TS types built-in. Heavier bundle than react-big-calendar or schedule-x. Premium resource views require per-developer license + OEM license if distributed to tenants. Standard tier is the safer adoption scope.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: serves first-party self-host
trial.generator_first_target: ui-kit (@curaos/ui SchedulingBoard component for the management multi-resource view; CalendarBoard reuses react-big-calendar for person surface)
trial.person_centric_fit: Strong for clinician-app schedule views (timegrid week is the standard clinical scheduling view). Standard tier covers person-facing appointment list/calendar. Resource views (multi-room booking for management surface) need premium.

trial.name: TanStack Start (full fleet migration)
trial.what: Replace Next.js with TanStack Start across all 24 web apps. Addresses: runtime env (VITE_ vars still bake, but createServerFn escape hatch is cleaner); self-host/air-gap (bun build --compile = 132 MB alpine image vs 2-3 GB Next.js standalone); SSR/streaming (loaders + Suspense, 5.5x throughput benchmark vs Next.js); generator fit (file-based routes + routeTree.gen.ts is more emitter-friendly than App Router page.tsx conventions). Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator target replacement.
trial.taxonomy: Replace Next.js with TanStack Start across all 24 web apps. Addresses: runtime env (VITE_ vars still bake, but createServerFn escape hatch is cleaner); self-host/air-gap (bun build --compile = 132 MB alpine image vs 2-3 GB Next.js standalone); SSR/streaming (loaders + Suspense, 5.5x throughput benchmark vs Next.js); generator fit (file-based routes + routeTree.gen.ts is more emitter-friendly than App Router page.tsx conventions). Taxonomy: ui-app-emit generator target replacement.
trial.stack_fit: Native Bun support (bun.sh official guide). Vite-based build fits Turborepo. No Nx official generator yet (GitHub discussion #34273 open as of March 2026 - community templates only). TanStack Query + Drizzle patterns well-documented. shadcn/ui official support. Auth cookie + NestJS JWT bridge documented. No wrapper needed for Bun runtime.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host first - Nitro or custom Bun server; no Vercel dependency. Bun compile to single binary for air-gap/Zarf. BYO hosting fully supported.
trial.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit emitter - would emit src/routes/*.tsx files; Vite plugin auto-generates routeTree.gen.ts. More emitter-friendly than Next.js App Router because route discovery is explicit and generated file is stable.
trial.person_centric_fit: File-based routing with beforeLoad auth guards and loaders maps cleanly to person-centric journey flows (patient app, personal-* apps). RSC support (April 2026) enables server-rendered patient data without client-side waterfall.

trial.name: TanStack Start (selective - new apps only)
trial.what: Use TanStack Start for net-new apps (not migrating the existing 24) while keeping the current Next.js fleet on next-runtime-env fix. Allows generator (ui-app-emit) to emit two targets: Next.js for existing apps, TanStack Start for greenfield. Lets the ecosystem/Nx generator situation mature before committing the fleet. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit codegen-emitter-target.
trial.taxonomy: Use TanStack Start for net-new apps (not migrating the existing 24) while keeping the current Next.js fleet on next-runtime-env fix. Allows generator (ui-app-emit) to emit two targets: Next.js for existing apps, TanStack Start for greenfield. Lets the ecosystem/Nx generator situation mature before committing the fleet. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit codegen-emitter-target.
trial.stack_fit: Same Bun + Turborepo fit. ui-app-emit would need a --framework flag (nextjs | tanstack-start). Two emitter templates to maintain in tools/codegen, but no migration burden on existing apps.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host first, same as full migration
trial.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (add tanstack-start emitter target alongside nextjs target)
trial.person_centric_fit: Same as full migration. Allows person-centric new surfaces (e.g. patient-app v2, personal-health-app) to be built on TanStack Start with cleaner data loading patterns.

trial.name: TanStack Router (SPA mode, no Start)
trial.what: Use TanStack Router standalone (no TanStack Start SSR layer) for apps that do not need SSR - pure client-side SPAs served as static files. Runtime env is trivially solved: inject window.__ENV from a server-side config endpoint at container startup, or use a sidecar nginx that rewrites a config.js file. No build-time baking problem because there is no build-time SSR. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit for non-SSR apps.
trial.taxonomy: Use TanStack Router standalone (no TanStack Start SSR layer) for apps that do not need SSR - pure client-side SPAs served as static files. Runtime env is trivially solved: inject window.__ENV from a server-side config endpoint at container startup, or use a sidecar nginx that rewrites a config.js file. No build-time baking problem because there is no build-time SSR. Taxonomy: ui-app-emit for non-SSR apps.
trial.stack_fit: Excellent. Vite + Bun build. routeTree.gen.ts codegen. Turborepo-friendly. No SSR layer = simpler Helm/Zarf packaging (just a static asset bundle behind nginx). NestJS JWT auth via API calls, no cookie proxy complexity.
trial.local_vs_3rdparty: Static assets self-hosted (nginx/CDN); no managed dependency
trial.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (SPA variant emitter - simpler than SSR, no server entry)
trial.person_centric_fit: Suitable for dashboard/admin apps where SEO is not a concern and interactivity is high. Not suitable for public-facing patient-facing pages where SSR matters for perceived performance.

service_boundary.name: Trigger.dev v4 (@trigger.dev/sdk + @trigger.dev/nestjs)
service_boundary.what: Apache 2.0 durable task execution platform: retried long-running jobs, schedules, event-triggered tasks, real-time observability dashboard, self-host via Docker/K8s. Fills the execution durability gap for automation-core-service scheduled triggers and plugin-runtime-service async jobs - both confirmed stubs. @trigger.dev/nestjs is the official NestJS module.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 durable task execution platform: retried long-running jobs, schedules, event-triggered tasks, real-time observability dashboard, self-host via Docker/K8s. Fills the execution durability gap for automation-core-service scheduled triggers and plugin-runtime-service async jobs - both confirmed stubs. @trigger.dev/nestjs is the official NestJS module.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Bun runtime supported in v4. @trigger.dev/nestjs module for DI integration. Self-host on Postgres+Redis (same infra as CuraOS). Complements BullMQ already in use for recurrence cadence - Trigger.dev is the durability layer above BullMQ for multi-step workflows.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (no mandatory SaaS dependency when self-hosted). Cloud option available for BYO-infra teams.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: automation-core-service trigger dispatcher wires to Trigger.dev task definitions; SDK pkg: @trigger.dev/nestjs injected into automation-core NestJS module
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Long-running automations (maternity risk eval, claim submission, insurance eligibility check) run durably without timing out; person sees real-time job status.

service_boundary.name: Infisical (@infisical/sdk)
service_boundary.what: MIT open-source secrets management platform with NestJS integration guide, @infisical/sdk npm package, and nestjs-infisical-sdk community module. Fills the connector OAuth vault secrets gap in integrations-core-service: securely stores and injects API keys, OAuth tokens, and credentials for connector auth strategies at runtime rather than baking them into env files.
service_boundary.taxonomy: MIT open-source secrets management platform with NestJS integration guide, @infisical/sdk npm package, and nestjs-infisical-sdk community module. Fills the connector OAuth vault secrets gap in integrations-core-service: securely stores and injects API keys, OAuth tokens, and credentials for connector auth strategies at runtime rather than baking them into env files.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Official NestJS integration; configures with custom siteUrl for self-hosted instance. SDK injects secrets into process.env or returns them programmatically. Bun runs Node 20 APIs so SDK works. CNPG Postgres + Redis already in CuraOS infra - no additional ops.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Infisical for first-party secrets plane; BYO connector credentials point at any 3rd-party service through the vault.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: integrations-core-service InfisicalModule for connector credential injection; SDK pkg: @infisical/sdk imported into connector auth strategies
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person's connected-app OAuth tokens stored securely per-tenant; management surface = secrets audit log, rotation policy, access control.

service_boundary.name: OpenBao (MPL 2.0 Vault fork)
service_boundary.what: Linux Foundation MPL 2.0 fork of HashiCorp Vault (last OSS version), API-compatible with Vault. Backup secrets backend option for teams that already have Vault operational patterns or need dynamic secrets (DB credential rotation, PKI). Fills the same connector OAuth vault gap as Infisical but via Vault-style dynamic secrets and leases.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Linux Foundation MPL 2.0 fork of HashiCorp Vault (last OSS version), API-compatible with Vault. Backup secrets backend option for teams that already have Vault operational patterns or need dynamic secrets (DB credential rotation, PKI). Fills the same connector OAuth vault gap as Infisical but via Vault-style dynamic secrets and leases.
service_boundary.stack_fit: No TypeScript SDK of its own - use node-vault or raw HTTP calls from NestJS. More operational complexity than Infisical (Vault agent, policies, leases). Better fit if CuraOS needs dynamic credential rotation or PKI for air-gap deployments.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only. No SaaS option. Air-gap friendly.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: integrations-core-service VaultProvider behind the same connector-auth interface as Infisical (adapter pattern)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Transparent to person; management surface = secrets audit, policy engine, certificate lifecycle.

service_boundary.name: Windmill (AGPL - reference only)
service_boundary.what: AGPL workflow engine with Bun/Deno TypeScript runtime, sandboxed multi-language script execution, K8s Helm chart, and MCP endpoint generation. Strongest external reference for plugin-runtime-service sandboxed code-step executor (taxonomy_id: workflow-automation.catalog.script-steps - confirmed stub locally). Architecture of windmill-worker deno_executor/python_executor is the reference for how to run untrusted code safely.
service_boundary.taxonomy: AGPL workflow engine with Bun/Deno TypeScript runtime, sandboxed multi-language script execution, K8s Helm chart, and MCP endpoint generation. Strongest external reference for plugin-runtime-service sandboxed code-step executor (taxonomy_id: workflow-automation.catalog.script-steps - confirmed stub locally). Architecture of windmill-worker deno_executor/python_executor is the reference for how to run untrusted code safely.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Cannot import into CuraOS build (AGPL). Reference the sandbox isolation patterns and TypeScript executor architecture for plugin-runtime-service design. Run as a separate optional service only if CuraOS integrates it behind a service boundary without distributing.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (CE free, AGPL). EE commercial for white-label embed.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: reference-only: plugin-runtime-service sandboxed executor design
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Script steps enable person-centric automation (my script that reformats my health data). Reference only.

service_boundary.name: TigerBeetle (tigerbeetle-node)
service_boundary.what: Purpose-built financial transactions DB with two-phase debit/credit transfers and ACID guarantees at 10k+ TPS. Fills erp-accounting-gl (taxonomy: erp.accounting.general-ledger) as the atomic posting hot-path engine for the accounting-core-service's transfer execution layer, freeing Drizzle/Postgres to own CoA, journals, reports, and fiscal metadata.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Purpose-built financial transactions DB with two-phase debit/credit transfers and ACID guarantees at 10k+ TPS. Fills erp-accounting-gl (taxonomy: erp.accounting.general-ledger) as the atomic posting hot-path engine for the accounting-core-service's transfer execution layer, freeing Drizzle/Postgres to own CoA, journals, reports, and fiscal metadata.
service_boundary.stack_fit: tigerbeetle-node npm package, TypeScript types included, Node >= 18, Bun-compatible. NOT a Drizzle/SQL surface: fixed accounts+transfers schema only; Drizzle stays for CoA/journals/tax/fiscal metadata. Runs as a separate sidecar binary (Linux production, macOS dev). Wrapper needed to translate accounting-core domain events into TB account/transfer calls.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (single binary, air-gap compatible, no cloud offering yet). No BYO-3rd-party SaaS option. Satisfies self-host lane; BYO lane covered by Postgres Drizzle-based double-entry as fallback.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: accounting-core-service SDK (wraps TB client behind accounting.tsp Transfer operation); posting-rules.ts maps domain events to TB createTransfers calls.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Invisible to person; enables real-time accurate balances that power 'my account balance', 'payment posted instantly', and copay estimate accuracy across healthstack-billing and accounting-core.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Downgraded from "trial" to "downgrade-reference" (keep as a named option in the ADR, do not integrate now) for three compounding reasons: (1) ponytail ladder kills it - the need is already met by the existing implementation; (2) curaos-loca
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: YES - already installed and fully implemented. accounting-core-service already has a complete double-entry engine on Drizzle + Postgres (CNPG). Ponytail ladder rung 2: the codebase already has it. Adding TB introduces: a second stateful binary to operate and upgrade (TB version must match client exactly - tight coupling), a required Postgres mirror bridge for all reporting (every query goes to Postgres anyway), unconfirmed Bun napi compatibility, a bespoke NestJS wrapper not supported by any generator, and a two-DB consistency problem (TB + Postgres must stay in sync across crashes). The local-vs-3rdparty duality rule (curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule) requires both a self-hosted AND a BYO-3rd-party option. TB has no BYO-3rd-party/SaaS path today (cloud offering is invitation-only and immature as of mid-2026). The fallback being "Postgres Drizzle-based double-entry" is not a BYO lane - it is the same self-hosted stack. The rule is not met.

service_boundary.name: Lago (self-hosted billing engine)
service_boundary.what: Open-source metering + usage-based billing + subscription management + invoicing platform. Fills erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions). Covers: event ingestion, usage metering, plan/charge definitions, subscription lifecycle, invoice generation, dunning, payment orchestration, revenue analytics.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source metering + usage-based billing + subscription management + invoicing platform. Fills erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions). Covers: event ingestion, usage metering, plan/charge definitions, subscription lifecycle, invoice generation, dunning, payment orchestration, revenue analytics.
service_boundary.stack_fit: AGPL: never import Lago code into CuraOS build. Integration via REST SDK (@getlago/lago-node). NestJS accounting-core-service calls Lago REST API as a billing-state sidecar. Helm chart available for K8s air-gap deployment. Lago manages billing state; CuraOS maps Lago invoices back to accounting-core GL via event subscription. Zod validation on Lago response DTOs needed.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Lago pod in same K8s cluster (self-hosted lane) OR configure to call Lago Cloud (BYO-3rd-party lane). Satisfies dual requirement from [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
service_boundary.generator_first_target: accounting.tsp (subscription/invoice contract wraps Lago events); @curaos/lago-adapter package (thin NestJS module wrapping Lago Node SDK with Zod validation); codegen emitter for subscription-core-service.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person sees clean invoice + subscription self-management ('my plan', 'my usage this month', 'cancel or upgrade'). Lago's invoice PDF + webhook feed CuraOS personal-app billing view.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Corrections to candidate before trial: (1) SDK package name: lago-javascript-client (not @getlago/lago-node). Update codegen generator target. (2) ClickHouse and Kafka are scaling add-ons, not baseline requirements - trial starts with Postg
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: No credible simpler alternative covers the same surface. Flexprice (Go backend, AGPL-3.0 backend / Apache 2.0 frontend, ~3.5k stars) is younger and thinner. Kill Bill is Java/Apache 2.0 but architecturally heavier and decade-old design. Building usage metering + subscription lifecycle + invoice generation + dunning from scratch in NestJS is a multi-month L effort with high correctness risk (billing math, proration, tax, idempotency). Lago at trial scope (REST sidecar + adapter module) is the minimum-viable path. Ponytail ladder stops at rung 5: already-available dependency that solves it, integrated via thin adapter. No custom billing engine needed.

service_boundary.name: Kill Bill
service_boundary.what: 10-year production-grade open-source subscription billing + payments platform. Alternative BYO billing sidecar to Lago. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions) with stronger catalog/plugin model for complex tariff structures, native dunning, and built-in payment gateway plugins (Stripe, Adyen, Braintree).
service_boundary.taxonomy: 10-year production-grade open-source subscription billing + payments platform. Alternative BYO billing sidecar to Lago. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap (taxonomy: erp.accounting.invoicing, erp.accounting.subscriptions) with stronger catalog/plugin model for complex tariff structures, native dunning, and built-in payment gateway plugins (Stripe, Adyen, Braintree).
service_boundary.stack_fit: Apache-2.0: could theoretically import, but Java runtime makes npm-import nonsensical - treat as sidecar REST service. Community Node.js client covers 250+ REST endpoints. NestJS wraps via HttpService. Helm/Docker available. Kaui admin UI built-in (no custom admin UI needed for billing ops).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host on K8s (primary). No managed cloud offering for BYO lane - pair with Kill Bill cloud if needed. Slightly weaker dual-lane story vs Lago.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Same as Lago: accounting.tsp subscription/invoice contract; @curaos/killbill-adapter NestJS module.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Same as Lago: person sees invoice + subscription self-management; Kill Bill webhooks feed CuraOS personal-app views.

service_boundary.name: Meteroid
service_boundary.what: Open-source billing + metering + invoicing platform in Rust with Postgres + Clickhouse + Kafka. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap as Lago but is higher-performance and earlier stage. AGPL-3.0 service boundary.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source billing + metering + invoicing platform in Rust with Postgres + Clickhouse + Kafka. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap as Lago but is higher-performance and earlier stage. AGPL-3.0 service boundary.
service_boundary.stack_fit: AGPL service boundary: call via REST/gRPC only. Helm chart for K8s. Requires Clickhouse (new DB engine CuraOS does not currently run) - significant infra expansion. Rust binary = no language runtime conflict but adds binary dependency.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Helm). Meteroid Cloud for BYO lane. Satisfies dual requirement.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Same as Lago: accounting.tsp + @curaos/meteroid-adapter.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Same as Lago: person sees subscription + invoice views.

service_boundary.name: Twenty CRM (self-hosted API sidecar)
service_boundary.what: Full CRM pipeline system: leads, contacts, accounts/households, deals/opportunities, pipelines, activities, email sync, reporting. Covers crm-insurance-broker.sales.* and crm-insurance-broker.service.* taxonomy IDs. Fills the CRM gaps in crm-core-service (leads absent, thin deal model, no pipeline catalog, no cases). Two usage modes: (A) self-host Twenty alongside CuraOS and call its GraphQL/REST API from crm-core (api-adapter, BYO-3rd-party path); (B) reference its NestJS + metadata-driven architecture to accelerate crm-core-service build (reference-only).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full CRM pipeline system: leads, contacts, accounts/households, deals/opportunities, pipelines, activities, email sync, reporting. Covers crm-insurance-broker.sales.* and crm-insurance-broker.service.* taxonomy IDs. Fills the CRM gaps in crm-core-service (leads absent, thin deal model, no pipeline catalog, no cases). Two usage modes: (A) self-host Twenty alongside CuraOS and call its GraphQL/REST API from crm-core (api-adapter, BYO-3rd-party path); (B) reference its NestJS + metadata-driven architecture to accelerate crm-core-service build (reference-only).
service_boundary.stack_fit: Twenty backend = NestJS + GraphQL + PostgreSQL - identical to CuraOS stack. twenty-sdk npm package exists for extending Twenty's own model. As an API adapter: thin NestJS service wraps Twenty GraphQL calls behind a crm.tsp-defined interface. No Bun-specific issues (REST/GraphQL client). Wrapper needed to bridge TypeSpec contracts to Twenty's metadata-driven schema.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: BYO-3rd-party (run Twenty as a sidecar, call its API; or self-host for the operator's own CRM use)
service_boundary.generator_first_target: api-adapter (NestJS crm-core-service wraps Twenty REST/GraphQL as BYO-3rd-party CRM backend; crm.tsp contract is the stable internal interface)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Twenty is entirely org/rep-centric (Salesforce alternative framing). Person-centric reshape requires the same re-centering as the source-map analysis - mine its entity completeness, not its UX. Its metadata-driven object model is a strong reference for CuraOS crm-core contract design.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Mode B (reference-only) is confirmed-adopt: Twenty's entity completeness (leads, pipeline stages, activities, cases) is strong prior art for crm-core TypeSpec contract design. Mine it. Mode A (api-adapter sidecar) requires: (1) legal sign-o
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Yes. Generator-first extension of crm-core-service (emit leads + pipeline catalog + cases via curaos/tools/codegen producing NestJS service + TypeSpec contract + Drizzle schema + seed) avoids license ambiguity, CVE monitoring obligation, sidecar ops overhead, and schema-translation glue entirely. Ponytail ladder: the generator is already installed; the missing entities are well-defined domain objects; Mode A sidecar fails rungs 1 and 5 (YAGNI complexity + existing capability path). Mode B (reference-only) passes rung 5: mine Twenty's entity model to accelerate TypeSpec contract design for crm-core, do not run it.

service_boundary.name: Ballerine (KYC/KYB document collection + workflow)
service_boundary.what: Open-source risk management infrastructure: document collection SDK flows, KYC/KYB workflow engine, case management back office, 3rd-party plugin system. Fills the document-collection/KYC gap for insurance policy onboarding and lead qualification (crm-insurance-broker.sales.leads, insurance policy application intake). Covers both self-host (on-prem data ownership) and BYO-3rd-party (Ballerine cloud). Backend uses NestJS + TypeBox, directly stack-compatible.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source risk management infrastructure: document collection SDK flows, KYC/KYB workflow engine, case management back office, 3rd-party plugin system. Fills the document-collection/KYC gap for insurance policy onboarding and lead qualification (crm-insurance-broker.sales.leads, insurance policy application intake). Covers both self-host (on-prem data ownership) and BYO-3rd-party (Ballerine cloud). Backend uses NestJS + TypeBox, directly stack-compatible.
service_boundary.license: MIT (web SDK/flows) + AGPL-3.0 (backend case management service)
service_boundary.stack_fit: Web SDK (MIT) is a React component - embeds in CuraOS React apps directly. Backend (AGPL) runs as a sidecar NestJS service - calls via REST API from crm-core or a dedicated KYC service. TypeBox + NestJS alignment means the backend API shape is immediately familiar. Bun can run the web SDK in dev; no Bun-specific issues on the NestJS backend sidecar.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: both (self-host Ballerine backend for on-prem data ownership; web SDK imported as npm package for document collection flows)
service_boundary.generator_first_target: react-component (web SDK flows embedded in crm-app / policy-onboarding app via ui-app-emit); api-adapter (NestJS crm-core calls Ballerine case management REST API for review workflow)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Directly person-centric: the document collection flow is the person's onboarding journey (upload ID, provide proof of address, etc.). The back-office case management is the management surface. Strong fit for person-centric lens - the person owns their document submission journey.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Research value: high -- Three disqualifying findings confirmed from independent sources that the candidate's summary understated or missed. (1) Maintenance risk is acute: the OSS repo is in an officially-unsupported rebuild phase while the
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Yes, for the actual CuraOS use case (insurance policy onboarding document collection + lead qualification review workflow). The CuraOS workflow/BPM engine (the core platform primitive per AGENTS.md §5.0) is the correct host for KYC document-step orchestration - it already owns human task + automation + SLA. Document upload steps are form steps in a BPM flow, not a separate KYC infrastructure. The back-office review surface is the crm-insurance-broker app built on the App/Site Builder. The 3rd-party provider slot (per curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule) is better filled by a purpose-built identity verification vendor API (Persona, Onfido, Sumsub, or their self-hosted equivalents) called from a thin NestJS adapter in crm-core, rather than running a second full NestJS service with a competing Prisma schema. Insurance-specific document types (proof of insurability, policy applications) would need custom Ballerine flow definitions anyway - that work lands in CuraOS BPM config instead, which is the generator-first target.

service_boundary.name: Activepieces (self-hosted service)
service_boundary.what: MIT workflow automation platform (Zapier alternative). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (connector registry, AI-agent step, MCP servers, pause-resume, test-step). 280+ pieces auto-become MCP servers. createPiece pattern is the direct prior art for @curaos/connector-sdk. NOT an npm-importable React SDK - embedding is via iframe or API. Generator-first target: @curaos/connector-sdk port of createPiece pattern; automation-core-service connector registry.
service_boundary.taxonomy: MIT workflow automation platform (Zapier alternative). Covers gap `workflow-automation-primitives` (connector registry, AI-agent step, MCP servers, pause-resume, test-step). 280+ pieces auto-become MCP servers. createPiece pattern is the direct prior art for @curaos/connector-sdk. NOT an npm-importable React SDK - embedding is via iframe or API. Generator-first target: @curaos/connector-sdk port of createPiece pattern; automation-core-service connector registry.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Can run as a self-hosted sidecar automation engine (BYO-3rd-party mode) OR its createPiece/pause-resume source is port-adapted into @curaos/connector-sdk (first-party self-hosted mode). Iframe embed for the visual builder. REST/webhook API for workflow trigger from NestJS. No native NestJS SDK but REST coupling is straightforward.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Activepieces as the automation engine (BYO-3rd-party option) OR port createPiece into @curaos/connector-sdk for a fully first-party connector registry. curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule satisfied.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: @curaos/connector-sdk (port createPiece pattern) + automation-core-service AiAgentStep/McpServer contracts
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person sees 'my connections' + 'my automations' + 'waiting on you' inbox derived from automation runs. Management sees full flow designer + connector catalog + run logs. Person-centric: pre-built journey templates; management: full no-code canvas.

service_boundary.name: Appsmith (Apache 2.0, self-hosted)
service_boundary.what: Apache 2.0 internal-tool/admin-panel builder. Covers `platform-reporting-builder` gap (ad-hoc report/query builder, per-user dashboard layout) as a self-hosted sidecar. Not an npm-import; embedding via iframe. 25+ DB connectors; drag-drop widget canvas; custom React component support via iframe widget. Generator-first target: reports-service ReportDefinition surface (BYO-3rd-party mode = Appsmith; first-party = Puck-based builder-core).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 internal-tool/admin-panel builder. Covers `platform-reporting-builder` gap (ad-hoc report/query builder, per-user dashboard layout) as a self-hosted sidecar. Not an npm-import; embedding via iframe. 25+ DB connectors; drag-drop widget canvas; custom React component support via iframe widget. Generator-first target: reports-service ReportDefinition surface (BYO-3rd-party mode = Appsmith; first-party = Puck-based builder-core).
service_boundary.stack_fit: Runs as a Docker/K8s sidecar. NestJS services registered as REST datasources. Iframe embed into builder-studio. Not React-component native. Best as a BYO-3rd-party option for tenants who already use Appsmith, while Puck-based builder-core is the first-party path.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both modes: self-hosted Appsmith as BYO-3rd-party admin builder + Puck/builder-core as first-party. Satisfies dual-option requirement.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: reports-service ReportDefinition (Appsmith as optional BYO viewer for power users; first-party ReportDefinition entity feeds Gotenberg/Superset per gap-analysis)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Appsmith is management-surface-only by design (internal tools). Person surfaces remain in the CuraOS React apps. No person-facing use case for Appsmith directly.

service_boundary.name: Plasmic (AGPL Studio + MIT SDK)
service_boundary.what: Visual builder + headless CMS. Studio is AGPL; SDK is MIT. The editor itself is partly closed-source (codegen engine); self-hosting the full studio is limited. Covers same ui-kit-archetypes / builder-core gap as Puck.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Visual builder + headless CMS. Studio is AGPL; SDK is MIT. The editor itself is partly closed-source (codegen engine); self-hosting the full studio is limited. Covers same ui-kit-archetypes / builder-core gap as Puck.
service_boundary.stack_fit: SDK integrates with React but editor requires Plasmic cloud or limited self-hosted mode. AGPL studio creates service-boundary risk for CuraOS's builder-studio embedding use case.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Fails first-party self-hosted requirement: editor engine is not fully self-hostable under permissive terms.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: N/A - rejected
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: N/A - rejected on license/self-host grounds.

service_boundary.name: ToolJet (AGPL)
service_boundary.what: AGPL open-source internal tool / admin-panel builder. Same target as Appsmith. Has embed docs but AGPL license means embedding in CuraOS builder-studio (a non-AGPL product) requires commercial license purchase.
service_boundary.taxonomy: AGPL open-source internal tool / admin-panel builder. Same target as Appsmith. Has embed docs but AGPL license means embedding in CuraOS builder-studio (a non-AGPL product) requires commercial license purchase.
service_boundary.stack_fit: AGPL blocks embedding without commercial license. Appsmith (Apache 2.0) is a drop-in alternative that avoids this risk entirely.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host possible but license cost required for non-AGPL products.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: N/A - rejected
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: N/A - rejected on license grounds.

service_boundary.name: Martin (maplibre/martin)
service_boundary.what: Blazing-fast PostGIS-native vector tile server (auto-discovers geometry tables/functions, serves MVT from PostGIS, PMTiles, MBTiles). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles: the fleet/map UI needs real-time vector overlays from the same PostGIS instance geospatial-core-service already owns.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Blazing-fast PostGIS-native vector tile server (auto-discovers geometry tables/functions, serves MVT from PostGIS, PMTiles, MBTiles). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles: the fleet/map UI needs real-time vector overlays from the same PostGIS instance geospatial-core-service already owns.
service_boundary.stack_fit: No Node wrapper needed. Add as Docker sidecar (ghcr.io/maplibre/martin) reading the same CNPG PostGIS DB via DATABASE_URL. NestJS geospatial-core-service emits geometry tables; Martin auto-discovers them. Zero-config for most setups. Helm chart available; Zarf-compatible static binary for air-gap.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: first-party self-host; BYO tile CDN (MapTiler, Esri) covered by same MapLibre frontend pointing at a different tile URL
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service/geospatial-core-service: generator emits Drizzle geometry columns + GiST index + publishes Martin config snippet; ui-app-emit MapView archetype references tile URL env var
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Enables real-time map rendering (patient location, delivery tracking, facility finder, geofence overlays) in both personal-* apps and business management dashboards without building a tile pipeline from scratch.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Hard reject on architectural grounds, not quality grounds. Martin is an excellent tool - fast (2-3x faster than nearest competitor in benchmarks), well-maintained, correct license. The rejection is because ADR-0206 (Accepted 2026-05-24) alr
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Already decided. ADR-0206 (Accepted 2026-05-24) explicitly resolved tile serving as: PMTiles file archive + HTTP range-request proxy from MinIO/S3 + NestJS thin proxy + MapLibre GL JS client-side render. The existing Requirements.md for geospatial-core-service encodes this. The ponytail ladder stops at rung 2: the solution already exists in the codebase/ADR. Martin is a new process, new sidecar, new operational surface - none of which is needed given the resolved architecture.

service_boundary.name: Valhalla routing engine
service_boundary.what: Open-source OSM routing engine with multi-modal routing, isochrones, time-aware turn restrictions, map matching, matrix queries, and optimized routes. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location (routing leg of location service) and fleet-core dispatch (ETA, route optimization). `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (MIT, npm) is a typed HTTP client.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source OSM routing engine with multi-modal routing, isochrones, time-aware turn restrictions, map matching, matrix queries, and optimized routes. Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location (routing leg of location service) and fleet-core dispatch (ETA, route optimization). `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (MIT, npm) is a typed HTTP client.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Deploy as Docker sidecar. NestJS geospatial-core-service calls Valhalla HTTP API via `@iwpnd/valhalla-ts` (v1.0.29, MIT, 71 weekly downloads - thin but typed). Wrap into @curaos/routing-sdk to abstract self-host vs BYO provider. fleet-core-service emits dispatch.route.calculated events from routing results.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: self-host via Docker; BYO via HERE Routing, Google Routes, Mapbox Directions API - same @curaos/routing-sdk interface
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/routing-sdk) + service geospatial-core-service adds /routing endpoint wired to Valhalla; fleet-core-service consumes routing-sdk for ETA
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Powers patient/customer journey: 'get directions to clinic', 'track my delivery ETA', 'how long until driver arrives'. Management surface: fleet dispatcher sees optimal routes, driver app gets turn-by-turn. Isochrones enable 'show facilities within 30 min' person surface.
service_boundary.verify_notes:
**Research value: high** - Multiple independent sources confirm prior art and real deployment patterns.

ADVERSARIAL FINDINGS (net verdict: confirm-trial, not full adopt yet):

1. LICENSE - PASS. MIT confirmed, compatible with self-host + a
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Two alternatives worth flagging: (1) @routingjs/valhalla (from nilsnolde/routingjs, gis-ops maintained) is a better-maintained multi-engine TypeScript client with a unified interface across Valhalla, OSRM, GraphHopper, and ORS - it directly enables the BYO-provider duality the curaos-local-vs-3rdparty rule requires, making it a stronger pick than @iwpnd/valhalla-ts. (2) OSRM is faster for pure A-to-B ETA queries with no dynamic costing, but lacks isochrones and multi-modal - insufficient for 'show facilities within 30 min' requirement. No already-installed CuraOS dependency or stdlib covers geospatial routing. Valhalla is the correct OSS engine; the client library choice should be upgraded.

service_boundary.name: Photon (komoot)
service_boundary.what: OpenStreetMap geocoder built on OpenSearch (search-as-you-type, typo-tolerant, forward + reverse geocode). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location: the geocoding leg of geospatial-core-service (convert address <-> coordinates). GraphHopper publishes weekly updated Photon database dumps, drastically reducing import burden.
service_boundary.taxonomy: OpenStreetMap geocoder built on OpenSearch (search-as-you-type, typo-tolerant, forward + reverse geocode). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.location: the geocoding leg of geospatial-core-service (convert address <-> coordinates). GraphHopper publishes weekly updated Photon database dumps, drastically reducing import burden.
service_boundary.stack_fit: CuraOS already runs Elasticsearch/OpenSearch for search-service. Photon uses the same OpenSearch index. NestJS geospatial-core-service calls Photon HTTP REST via thin @curaos/geocoder-sdk wrapper. Zarf-bundleable. No npm package needed - plain HTTP.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: self-host via Docker + OpenSearch; BYO: Google Geocoding API, HERE Geocoder, Mapbox Geocoding - same @curaos/geocoder-sdk interface
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service geospatial-core-service: generator emits /geocode and /reverse endpoints wired to @curaos/geocoder-sdk; geocoder-sdk has self-hosted Photon + BYO provider implementations
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Powers 'find my nearest clinic', facility search, delivery address autocomplete, SOS address resolution in person apps. Management surface: driver address lookup, geofence definition by address.

service_boundary.name: Fleetbase (AGPL-3.0)
service_boundary.what: Full open-source logistics OS (dispatch, order management, driver app, warehouse, proof-of-delivery, routing). Much broader than Traccar. Laravel/PHP backend. Would cover fleet-core dispatch workflow surface comprehensively.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full open-source logistics OS (dispatch, order management, driver app, warehouse, proof-of-delivery, routing). Much broader than Traccar. Laravel/PHP backend. Would cover fleet-core dispatch workflow surface comprehensively.
service_boundary.stack_fit: AGPL means any modification must be open-sourced under AGPL or a commercial license purchased. PHP/Laravel stack does not compose into the NestJS/Bun monorepo. Would need full REST API adapter layer. Weight far exceeds the dispatch workflow value CuraOS needs.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: both (self-host or managed Fleetbase cloud)
service_boundary.generator_first_target: reference-only
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Driver app (Navigator) is strong; but CuraOS personal-tracking-service already handles driver-side positioning. Overlap creates duplication.

service_boundary.name: fhir-validator-js (Outburn) + fhir-validator-wrapper (FHIR/HL7)
service_boundary.what: Node.js wrappers around the canonical HL7 Java FHIR Validator CLI. fhir-validator-js (Apache-2.0, v1.4.0, Mar 2025) manages JVM sessions, supports multiple concurrent IG contexts, downloads JAR automatically. Fills gap [clinical-fhir-resource-set] test requirement: 'FHIR-R4 validation per resource (HAPI/fhir.js in CI)' and [interop-fhir-server] 'FHIR-validate every resource in CI'. Runs against US Core, SNOMED-backed IGs, and custom CuraOS profiles.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Node.js wrappers around the canonical HL7 Java FHIR Validator CLI. fhir-validator-js (Apache-2.0, v1.4.0, Mar 2025) manages JVM sessions, supports multiple concurrent IG contexts, downloads JAR automatically. Fills gap [clinical-fhir-resource-set] test requirement: 'FHIR-R4 validation per resource (HAPI/fhir.js in CI)' and [interop-fhir-server] 'FHIR-validate every resource in CI'. Runs against US Core, SNOMED-backed IGs, and custom CuraOS profiles.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Used in CI (just ci gate) and optionally as a sidecar in healthstack-interop-service for inbound FHIR resource validation. Requires JRE in the pod/CI runner - add openjdk:21-jre to Dockerfile. Bun-compatible for the JS wrapper layer. NestJS service calls it async before persisting inbound resources.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: JVM runs locally in CI or K8s pod. No external call; IGs loaded locally. Supports air-gap with pre-downloaded JAR + IG packages.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Codegen emitter: every healthstack service's CI test suite gets a FHIR validation step scaffolded by the service-core template.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Indirect: validated FHIR resources are trustworthy inputs to patient-facing read models; validation failures caught in CI not at patient query time.
service_boundary.verify_notes: SECURITY FLAG - three active CVEs in the underlying HL7 Java validator (org.hl7.fhir.validation / org.hl7.fhir.core): CVE-2026-34361 (CVSS 9.3, Critical) and CVE-2026-34360 (blind SSRF) both affect the /loadIG HTTP endpoint - fixed in 6.9.4
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Yes, with a critical scope distinction. @medplum/core validateResource / indexStructureDefinitionBundle provides JVM-free FHIR R4 schema + profile validation in pure TypeScript. It supports US Core and custom StructureDefinitions loaded locally. It is already a plausible dependency given CuraOS HealthStack's FHIR surface. For structural correctness checks and base-spec conformance it is sufficient and has zero JVM cost. The gap: @medplum/core does NOT replicate the HL7 reference validator's terminology-binding checks (SNOMED, LOINC value set expansion), FHIRPath invariant evaluation depth, or gold-standard IG conformance scoring. For interoperability certification-grade CI validation against US Core + CuraOS custom profiles, the HL7 Java validator is the only authoritative tool. The two are complementary, not interchangeable - use @medplum/core for fast unit-level checks and fhir-validator-js for conformance-gate CI runs.

service_boundary.name: Snowstorm (IHTSDO) - already in use as terminology-service backing
service_boundary.what: Apache-2.0 FHIR terminology server with deep SNOMED CT support plus LOINC, ICD-10, ICD-10-CM. CuraOS terminology-service is already backed by Snowstorm (verified: 'present-strong', taxonomy_id healthcare.interop.snomed-loinc-terminology). Gap is NOT a new server but value-set API adapter: healthstack-quality-service needs $expand + $validate-code calls for eCQM measure code binding, and healthstack-meds-service needs RxNorm $lookup. Fills [ecqm-quality-engine] value-set binding step and coded-field binding for all FHIR resource services.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Apache-2.0 FHIR terminology server with deep SNOMED CT support plus LOINC, ICD-10, ICD-10-CM. CuraOS terminology-service is already backed by Snowstorm (verified: 'present-strong', taxonomy_id healthcare.interop.snomed-loinc-terminology). Gap is NOT a new server but value-set API adapter: healthstack-quality-service needs $expand + $validate-code calls for eCQM measure code binding, and healthstack-meds-service needs RxNorm $lookup. Fills [ecqm-quality-engine] value-set binding step and coded-field binding for all FHIR resource services.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Already deployed. NestJS services call FHIR $expand/$lookup via HTTP. A thin @curaos/terminology-sdk client (wrapping fetch) is the only new code needed. No new infrastructure.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-hosted (Snowstorm on-cluster). BYO-3rd-party path: VSAC API (NLM) or Ontoserver for hosted value sets.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/terminology-sdk): thin codegen-emitted client for $expand/$lookup/$translate, consumed by healthstack service TypeSpec-generated code.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Enables plain-language display names for coded clinical values shown to patients (SNOMED preferred term, LOINC display). Required for correct AVS and patient-app clinical summaries.

service_boundary.name: OHIF Viewer v3 + Orthanc DICOM server
service_boundary.what: OHIF (MIT, v3.11, React/Cornerstone.js): zero-footprint browser DICOM viewer supporting DICOMweb (QIDO-RS/WADO-RS/STOW-RS), self-hosted as static bundle or Docker. Orthanc (GPL-3, C++): lightweight PACS server with DICOMweb plugin, REST API, widely used as self-hosted DICOM store. Together they fill gap [healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs) for healthstack-imaging-service DICOM/PACS integration. OHIF iframe-embedded into CuraOS imaging UI; Orthanc runs as K8s sidecar service.
service_boundary.taxonomy: OHIF (MIT, v3.11, React/Cornerstone.js): zero-footprint browser DICOM viewer supporting DICOMweb (QIDO-RS/WADO-RS/STOW-RS), self-hosted as static bundle or Docker. Orthanc (GPL-3, C++): lightweight PACS server with DICOMweb plugin, REST API, widely used as self-hosted DICOM store. Together they fill gap [healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs) for healthstack-imaging-service DICOM/PACS integration. OHIF iframe-embedded into CuraOS imaging UI; Orthanc runs as K8s sidecar service.
service_boundary.stack_fit: OHIF: iframe embed or separate static deployment - no import into NestJS. Orthanc: HTTP REST + WADO-RS from healthstack-imaging-service. DICOMweb client calls from NestJS (simple fetch wrapper). No deep integration needed. GPL-3 on Orthanc = service boundary safe (CuraOS code never inside Orthanc).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-hosted Orthanc (air-gap compatible) AND BYO-3rd-party PACS (Sectra, Change Healthcare, AWS HealthImaging - any DICOMweb-compliant store).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-imaging-service): DICOMweb adapter stub emitted by codegen; OHIF viewer URL rendered in imaging-app React component.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Patient 'my imaging' view: OHIF renders study thumbnails + viewer for person to see their own scans. Clinician worklist is the management surface. Same DICOMweb backend serves both.

service_boundary.name: GlitchTip
service_boundary.what: Self-hosted error tracking with 100% Sentry SDK wire protocol compatibility. Fills error tracking gap (curaos_error_tracking_rule names GlitchTip prod + Sentry SaaS dev). MIT licensed, 4 containers, 256-512MB RAM. GlitchTip 6 (2026) added improved stacktraces.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Self-hosted error tracking with 100% Sentry SDK wire protocol compatibility. Fills error tracking gap (curaos_error_tracking_rule names GlitchTip prod + Sentry SaaS dev). MIT licensed, 4 containers, 256-512MB RAM. GlitchTip 6 (2026) added improved stacktraces.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Client side: @sentry/nestjs SDK (unchanged) pointed at GlitchTip DSN. Zero NestJS code change. OTel-mode Sentry v8 SDK also works: same OTel pipeline feeding GlitchTip as error sink and LGTM as trace/metrics backend. Helm chart for K8s deploy.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host primary (GlitchTip self-hosted on homelab/k3d). BYO-3rd-party: swap DSN to Sentry SaaS or Bugsink - no client code change.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/observability fills scaffold-only; includes Sentry/GlitchTip init) + codegen-emitter (otel-init.ts Handlebars includes error reporting init)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Catches patient-facing errors (scheduling failures, auth loops, billing crashes) before users report them; error rate feeds SLO burn-rate alerting.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Three real risks worth tracking: (1) Postgres write saturation above ~20M events/month - acceptable for CuraOS homelab scale; upgrade path is self-hosted Sentry (same DSN swap, zero client change). (2) No pre-built Zarf bundle - must author
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: LGTM stack (already in CuraOS observability) does NOT cover structured error grouping, issue deduplication, release tracking, or user-impact correlation the way GlitchTip does. The ponytail ladder check: (1) YAGNI? No - error_tracking_rule already names GlitchTip explicitly. (2) Already in codebase? No error sink exists. (3) Stdlib? Not applicable. (4) Native platform? OTel exceptions exist but no grouping/dedup UI. (5) Already-installed dep? LGTM handles traces/metrics/logs but has no Sentry-protocol error grouping. GlitchTip fills a genuine gap LGTM does not close. Verdict: not replaceable by what is already present.

service_boundary.name: Langfuse
service_boundary.what: LLM observability, evals, prompt management, and cost tracking for AI/automation-core and CDS steps. Fills workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (absent) observability sub-gap and the LLM eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule. MIT core; EE features (SCIM, audit log, data retention) need license key.
service_boundary.taxonomy: LLM observability, evals, prompt management, and cost tracking for AI/automation-core and CDS steps. Fills workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (absent) observability sub-gap and the LLM eval dimension of curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule. MIT core; EE features (SCIM, audit log, data retention) need license key.
service_boundary.license: MIT (core) + commercial EE for advanced features
service_boundary.stack_fit: JS SDK: npm install langfuse. OTel-mode: point OTel OTLP exporter to Langfuse ingest endpoint - zero extra instrumentation code if OTel is already set up. NestJS: inject LangfuseService as provider. Self-host requires platform >= 3.95.0. Helm chart for K8s.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Helm on homelab (2 containers + Postgres + Redis). BYO-3rd-party: Langfuse Cloud (GDPR-compliant EU region available).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg + codegen-emitter (ai-llm step template in automation-core and workflow-core services)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: LLM steps in CDS, automation-core, and AI workflow nodes affect patient safety and user journeys; Langfuse's eval metrics (faithfulness, hallucination) catch regressions before they reach users.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Verdict is trial, not adopt, for three reasons: (1) EE paywall on audit logs is a hard constraint for HealthStack regulated deployments - this must be contractually resolved before production rollout, not deferred; (2) air-gap image-registr
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Ponytail check: OTel already in CuraOS stack. If the only need is LLM span tracing routed into the existing Grafana/Tempo pipeline, zero new service is needed - emit gen_ai.* semantic conventions from LLM call sites and they flow through the existing OTel collector. That covers the observability sub-gap with no new infrastructure. What Langfuse adds beyond that: structured evals (faithfulness, hallucination scoring), prompt management UI, cost tracking per trace, and dataset/experiment management. These are not covered by stdlib OTel + Grafana. So the simpler path handles tracing only; Langfuse is justified only if eval scoring and prompt management are real near-term requirements. Arize Phoenix (Apache 2.0, fully open, no EE paywall, OTel-native) is a cleaner air-gap alternative if audit-log EE requirement is a concern, though it lacks prompt management.

service_boundary.name: Pyrra
service_boundary.what: SLO management for Prometheus: reads OpenSLO/CRD definitions, generates multi-window multi-burn-rate Prometheus recording rules and alerts, provides a UI. Fills ops/slo gap - CuraOS already has OpenSLO YAML templates; Pyrra is the K8s operator that activates them as live alerts.
service_boundary.taxonomy: SLO management for Prometheus: reads OpenSLO/CRD definitions, generates multi-window multi-burn-rate Prometheus recording rules and alerts, provides a UI. Fills ops/slo gap - CuraOS already has OpenSLO YAML templates; Pyrra is the K8s operator that activates them as live alerts.
service_boundary.stack_fit: K8s operator mode reads ServiceLevelObjective CRDs; filesystem mode reads YAML (matches ops/slo OpenSLO files). Helm chart available. No NestJS/Bun integration needed - pure ops plane. Generator: service-catalog.json already generates OpenSLO; Pyrra consumes them.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (K8s operator or Docker). No managed cloud SaaS option.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts already emits OpenSLO; wire Pyrra CRD schema as the target format)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: SLO burn-rate alerts fire before SLO breach, protecting patient scheduling/billing journey P95 latency budgets defined in ops/slo.

service_boundary.name: Grafana LGTM (Loki + Grafana + Tempo + Mimir) via lgtm-distributed Helm chart
service_boundary.what: Full self-hosted observability backend: Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Grafana (dashboards). Single Helm umbrella chart lgtm-distributed v3.0.1. Fills the backend sink for OTel spans/metrics/logs that CuraOS services emit. CuraOS ops/observability already generates Grafana dashboards - this is the deployment target.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full self-hosted observability backend: Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Grafana (dashboards). Single Helm umbrella chart lgtm-distributed v3.0.1. Fills the backend sink for OTel spans/metrics/logs that CuraOS services emit. CuraOS ops/observability already generates Grafana dashboards - this is the deployment target.
service_boundary.license: Apache-2.0 (Grafana OSS) + AGPL for Grafana Enterprise features
service_boundary.stack_fit: NestJS services send OTLP to Alloy (Grafana Agent successor) which forwards to Mimir/Loki/Tempo. CuraOS ops/observability generate.ts already emits Grafana dashboard JSON - those dashboards import directly. Helm chart for homelab K8s.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: lgtm-distributed on homelab/k3d (monolithic mode for small cluster). BYO-3rd-party: Grafana Cloud (same OTLP endpoint, same dashboards).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts target format alignment with Mimir remote_write + Tempo OTLP endpoint)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Operational reliability for all person-centric journeys; Tempo distributed traces show which service in the patient scheduling or billing chain is slow.

service_boundary.name: Pact + Pact Broker (pact-js + pact-broker-docker)
service_boundary.what: Consumer-driven contract testing for HTTP APIs and async messaging between CuraOS microservices. pact-js v13 MIT; self-hosted Pact Broker via pactfoundation/pact-broker Docker image. AsyncAPI plugin (pact-asyncapi-comparator) covers Kafka/Redpanda event contracts from CuraOS AsyncAPI specs. Bi-directional contract mode (BDCT) accepts OpenAPI from TypeSpec output directly.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Consumer-driven contract testing for HTTP APIs and async messaging between CuraOS microservices. pact-js v13 MIT; self-hosted Pact Broker via pactfoundation/pact-broker Docker image. AsyncAPI plugin (pact-asyncapi-comparator) covers Kafka/Redpanda event contracts from CuraOS AsyncAPI specs. Bi-directional contract mode (BDCT) accepts OpenAPI from TypeSpec output directly.
service_boundary.stack_fit: pact-js npm-import in test files; pact-nestjs DI wrapper for provider verification. BDCT mode: TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> feed to Pact Broker as provider contract, no need to write consumer test boilerplate. AsyncAPI plugin covers Redpanda event contracts. Self-hosted broker: 1 Docker container + Postgres.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: pact-broker-docker + Postgres (homelab). BYO-3rd-party: Pactflow SaaS (same CLI tooling, just different broker URL).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (generate provider contract verification test stub per service from TypeSpec + AsyncAPI contracts)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Contract tests catch breaking API changes between patient-app/clinician-app consumers and backend services before they reach users; can-i-deploy gate blocks deploy of incompatible service versions.

service_boundary.name: Microsoft Presidio (Python service) + pii-vault (TS alternative)
service_boundary.what: PII/PHI detection and anonymization before LLM calls, log redaction, and audit scrubbing. Presidio = Python REST API (run as sidecar); pii-vault = pure TypeScript Presidio-compatible npm package (MIT, multi-language). Fills the PHI scrubbing requirement in curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule and the ConsentGuard/data-boundary rule before any LLM trace lands in Langfuse.
service_boundary.taxonomy: PII/PHI detection and anonymization before LLM calls, log redaction, and audit scrubbing. Presidio = Python REST API (run as sidecar); pii-vault = pure TypeScript Presidio-compatible npm package (MIT, multi-language). Fills the PHI scrubbing requirement in curaos_agent_eval_obs_rule and the ConsentGuard/data-boundary rule before any LLM trace lands in Langfuse.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Presidio: run as K8s sidecar (2 containers: analyzer + anonymizer); call via HTTP from NestJS services before emitting LLM traces to Langfuse. pii-vault: npm-import directly into NestJS if pure-TS is preferred; no Python sidecar needed. Wrapper needed: inject as NestJS provider via presidio-anonymizer-nestjs or custom service.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Presidio containers or pii-vault in-process. BYO-3rd-party: Azure AI Language PII detection service (same interface).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/observability or @curaos/phi-boundary package - extend existing healthstack-phi-boundary package with redaction pipeline)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Protects patient PHI from leaking into LLM telemetry/traces; critical for HIPAA compliance on HealthStack AI features and CDS steps.

service_boundary.name: Grafana k6 (v2.0)
service_boundary.what: Load/performance testing with SLO threshold codification. Already adopted in ops/perf with constant-arrival-rate baselines and SLO-mapped thresholds. k6 v2.0 (2026) adds AI-assisted test generation, Playwright browser module parity, and JSON summary output for CI consumption.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Load/performance testing with SLO threshold codification. Already adopted in ops/perf with constant-arrival-rate baselines and SLO-mapped thresholds. k6 v2.0 (2026) adds AI-assisted test generation, Playwright browser module parity, and JSON summary output for CI consumption.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Already real-working in ops/perf. Tests run as standalone binary (not imported into services); AGPL service boundary does not affect CuraOS application code. Results stream to Prometheus/Mimir via k6 Prometheus remote_write output.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: k6 binary in CI + ops/perf scripts. BYO-3rd-party: Grafana Cloud k6 for distributed load generation.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (ops/observability generate.ts already emits k6 baselines per service-catalog.json; extend to emit Playwright browser scenario stubs)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Validates that patient scheduling, billing, and appointment booking endpoints stay within SLO latency budgets under load.

service_boundary.name: Trigger.dev v3
service_boundary.what: Apache-2.0 durable background job + workflow platform with first-class human-in-the-loop (Waitpoints: pause task until human approves/rejects), NestJS SDK, self-host via Docker/K8s + PostgreSQL. Fills: BYO-3rd-party escape valve for teams that want managed workflow without Temporal Cloud; also fills long-running background task lane. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.background-jobs
service_boundary.taxonomy: Apache-2.0 durable background job + workflow platform with first-class human-in-the-loop (Waitpoints: pause task until human approves/rejects), NestJS SDK, self-host via Docker/K8s + PostgreSQL. Fills: BYO-3rd-party escape valve for teams that want managed workflow without Temporal Cloud; also fills long-running background task lane. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.human-in-loop, platform.workflow.background-jobs
service_boundary.stack_fit: @trigger.dev/sdk + @trigger.dev/nestjs. Tasks defined as TypeScript functions decorated with task(). Bun-compatible client path (task scheduling). Worker runs on dedicated isolated compute - same Bun/Node boundary issue as Temporal for worker runtime but managed by Trigger.dev's own runner. Self-host adds a Trigger.dev server pod + PostgreSQL + Redis. No Drizzle/Zod conflicts.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose / Helm + PostgreSQL + Redis, all open source under Apache-2.0. BYO-3rd-party: Trigger.dev Cloud. Both with identical feature set (no feature gate on self-host).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter could scaffold task() definitions from TypeSpec operation decorators for approval/notification workflows
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Waitpoints are a natural fit for patient/customer journey approvals (prior auth, claim review, care plan sign-off) - task literally pauses and resumes on human signal. Observability dashboard shows person journey progress.

service_boundary.name: Restate (@restatedev/restate-sdk + restate-nest)
service_boundary.what: Durable execution runtime: HTTP-native, virtual-object actors with built-in K/V store, exactly-once semantics, single Rust binary deployment. Integrates with XState as persistent event loop. Fills: lighter-weight alternative to Temporal for stateful per-entity workflows (per-patient state machines, per-claim saga). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.actor-model
service_boundary.taxonomy: Durable execution runtime: HTTP-native, virtual-object actors with built-in K/V store, exactly-once semantics, single Rust binary deployment. Integrates with XState as persistent event loop. Fills: lighter-weight alternative to Temporal for stateful per-entity workflows (per-patient state machines, per-claim saga). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.actor-model
service_boundary.stack_fit: TypeScript SDK (@restatedev/restate-sdk) is pure TS, Bun-compatible. NestJS adapter via restate-nest (decorator-based). Works as HTTP handler - Restate server calls YOUR NestJS endpoints with durability guarantees. No Node worker_threads dependency - avoids Bun blocker. Drizzle/Postgres can be used inside steps normally. Restate server adds one pod.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: single Restate binary / Docker / Helm - fully self-hostable. BYO-3rd-party: Restate Cloud (managed). BUSL means cannot resell Restate server as a managed service, but using it for CuraOS own workflows is fine.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter could scaffold VirtualObject class + handler methods from TypeSpec service definitions
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Virtual objects map naturally to per-person stateful journeys (one actor per patient/claim/order). Built-in K/V avoids polling patterns. XState + Restate combo gives BPMN-grade state modeling with Restate durability.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Research value: high - decisive prior art and a codebase blocker found. Key findings: (1) PONYTAIL LADDER FIRES AT RUNG 2: Temporal 1.17.2 is already installed in workflow-core-service. A second durable-execution runtime is YAGNI until Temp
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is decisive. CuraOS `workflow-core-service` already ships @temporalio/activity + @temporalio/client + @temporalio/worker + @temporalio/workflow at v1.17.2 plus nestjs-temporal-core 3.2.8. Temporal IS the installed durable-execution engine. Ponytail ladder rung 2 fires: the capability already exists in this codebase. Adding Restate adds a second durable-execution runtime with overlapping semantics, split ownership of actor/saga state, two sets of operational concerns, and a BUSL-licensed binary to every air-gap bundle. That is the opposite of ponytail. The Temporal TypeScript SDK does NOT run on Bun (issue #1334 is open, worker_threads dependency), which is the claimed advantage of Restate. However: CuraOS workflow workers can run under Node while the NestJS services run under Bun - the two runtimes are already separated at the service boundary. This is not a new problem requiring a new tool.

service_boundary.name: Inngest (inngest/inngest)
service_boundary.what: Event-driven durable step function platform: fan-out, parallel steps, retries, scheduled crons, Postgres or SQLite state. SSPL license (server) with DOSP to Apache-2.0 after 3 years. Fills: event-triggered background workflow alternative if Temporal ops burden is too high. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.event-driven, platform.workflow.background-jobs
service_boundary.taxonomy: Event-driven durable step function platform: fan-out, parallel steps, retries, scheduled crons, Postgres or SQLite state. SSPL license (server) with DOSP to Apache-2.0 after 3 years. Fills: event-triggered background workflow alternative if Temporal ops burden is too high. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.event-driven, platform.workflow.background-jobs
service_boundary.stack_fit: TypeScript SDK npm-importable. Inngest server is a binary/Docker image. Self-host with --postgres-uri (v1.4.0+). NestJS integration via direct SDK usage. Bun-compatible SDK. SSPL on server binary means using it for internal CuraOS workflows is fine; offering it as a hosted SaaS to customers without source disclosure would trigger SSPL.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker/Helm + Postgres (SSPL, legal review needed for on-prem product distribution). BYO-3rd-party: Inngest Cloud. Hybrid viable.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter scaffolds inngest.createFunction() from AsyncAPI event definitions
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Event-triggered workflows fire on domain events (patient.admitted, claim.submitted) and fan out to parallel steps - natural fit for care coordination automation.

service_boundary.name: Documenso (self-hosted e-sign service) + @documenso/sdk-typescript
service_boundary.what: Full-featured self-hosted DocuSign alternative with PKCS#12 digital signatures, multi-signer envelopes, embedded signing, webhooks, and eIDAS/UETA compliance. Fills the gap where esign-core-service already owns the contract layer but needs a BYO-3rd-party provider option (local_vs_3rdparty dual requirement). The sdk-typescript (MIT) is the api-adapter target. Covers taxonomy: erp.recurring.contracts esign surface + contract.signed_status projection.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full-featured self-hosted DocuSign alternative with PKCS#12 digital signatures, multi-signer envelopes, embedded signing, webhooks, and eIDAS/UETA compliance. Fills the gap where esign-core-service already owns the contract layer but needs a BYO-3rd-party provider option (local_vs_3rdparty dual requirement). The sdk-typescript (MIT) is the api-adapter target. Covers taxonomy: erp.recurring.contracts esign surface + contract.signed_status projection.
service_boundary.license: Service: AGPL-3.0 (Community) / Commercial $250/mo Platform tier for AGPL escape. SDK (@documenso/sdk-typescript): MIT.
service_boundary.stack_fit: sdk-typescript is a plain fetch-based TS SDK - direct npm import in NestJS esign-core-service with no wrapper needed. Service deploy = Helm chart + PostgreSQL (CNPG already present). Zod validation wraps SDK response types at the service boundary.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: run Documenso container (AGPL CE, PKCS#12 signing passphrase + PostgreSQL required). BYO-3rd-party: point same sdk-typescript at Documenso Cloud or DocuSign/HelloSign via api-adapter shim in esign-core. Dual requirement satisfied.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @documenso/sdk-typescript injected via esign-core-service NestJS module as the 'remote provider' branch of the PdfRendererProvider port pattern already established in reports-service. Contract: esign.tsp already owns the SigningRequest/Signature shapes; the sdk bridges to the external service without changing the TypeSpec.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: In-app 'Review & sign' step exposed via esign-core ops for the My Agreements / contract-renewal person journey. Shareable self-serve signing link for non-account counterparties. Clinician/admin envelope console for multi-signer status, void, chain-of-custody audit.

service_boundary.name: DocuSeal + @docuseal/api
service_boundary.what: Lighter-weight self-hosted e-sign alternative (Ruby on Rails backend, SQLite default). ~17k GitHub stars, TypeScript/JS SDK published as @docuseal/api (v1.0.23). Simpler deploy than Documenso; supports more field types, SMS delivery. AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b) additional terms: API access and embedding require paid Pro On-Premises license ($20/user/month). Best fit as the BYO-3rd-party adapter option behind the esign-core provider port, paired with Documenso as the default self-hosted provider.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Lighter-weight self-hosted e-sign alternative (Ruby on Rails backend, SQLite default). ~17k GitHub stars, TypeScript/JS SDK published as @docuseal/api (v1.0.23). Simpler deploy than Documenso; supports more field types, SMS delivery. AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b) additional terms: API access and embedding require paid Pro On-Premises license ($20/user/month). Best fit as the BYO-3rd-party adapter option behind the esign-core provider port, paired with Documenso as the default self-hosted provider.
service_boundary.license: AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b). Embedding/API require $20/user/month Pro On-Premises license.
service_boundary.stack_fit: sdk (@docuseal/api) is plain TS, no framework coupling. NestJS module wraps it at provider level. Zod wraps response types. Service is a separate Docker container.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host option: simpler deploy than Documenso (SQLite ok). BYO-3rd-party: point sdk at DocuSeal Cloud. However API is paywalled even on self-hosted - legal-review needed before production use.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @docuseal/api as alternative provider behind the esign-core 'provider port' - switchable via env config in NestJS DI (same contract, swap implementation).
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Same person journey as Documenso. API covers template-based submission creation, embedded signing widget, webhook events. Field-type breadth (signature, date, text, checkbox, radio, image) supports clinical consent forms.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Candidate recommendation was "trial" with self-hosted as primary. Downgraded to reference because: (1) API paywall on self-hosted makes it non-functional as a self-hosted provider without paid seats - violates local-vs-3rdparty-rule's self-
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Documenso is the strictly better self-hosted default: AGPL-3.0 with NO Section 7(b) paywall, full API access free on self-hosted, TypeScript/Next.js native stack (closer to Bun ecosystem), TypeScript SDK exists (github.com/documenso/sdk-typescript), PostgreSQL-native, community Helm chart issue open (#1345). Documenso satisfies the local-vs-3rdparty duality without a license key gate. DocuSeal can remain documented as the BYO-3rd-party cloud option ONLY (point SDK at DocuSeal Cloud for teams that already pay), but should not be the self-hosted provider behind the esign-core port.

service_boundary.name: Gotenberg (self-hosted PDF engine)
service_boundary.what: Docker-based stateless API wrapping Chromium + LibreOffice for HTML/CSS/Markdown -> PDF, DOCX -> PDF, PDF merge/split. Already the chosen PDF engine per ADR-0113 (InMemoryPdfRendererProvider stub confirms the real Gotenberg adapter is injected at modulith level). MIT license, 12k+ GitHub stars, version 8.32+ active. Fills: reports-service PDF rendering (erp.platform.documents 'present-strong'), AVS PDF render, CMS-1500/UB-04 paper claim forms, invoice PDF.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Docker-based stateless API wrapping Chromium + LibreOffice for HTML/CSS/Markdown -> PDF, DOCX -> PDF, PDF merge/split. Already the chosen PDF engine per ADR-0113 (InMemoryPdfRendererProvider stub confirms the real Gotenberg adapter is injected at modulith level). MIT license, 12k+ GitHub stars, version 8.32+ active. Fills: reports-service PDF rendering (erp.platform.documents 'present-strong'), AVS PDF render, CMS-1500/UB-04 paper claim forms, invoice PDF.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Already integrated via the PdfRendererProvider interface in reports-service. gotenberg-js-client npm package exists but is Gotenberg v6-era; direct fetch() calls to /forms/chromium/convert/html are simpler and already the pattern. No wrapper needed - the provider interface is the wrapper.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host primary: Helm chart + Docker image, air-gap safe (Zarf-packaged). BYO-3rd-party: swap the PdfRendererProvider NestJS port to point at any hosted HTML-to-PDF service (PDFBolt, WeasyPrint cloud) - the interface already exists in reports-service.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Codegen-emitter-target: the reports-service GotenbergHttpAdapterProvider (the real adapter injected at modulith) should be emitted as part of service scaffold rather than hand-written each time. Port interface already in codegen mold shape.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Invisible infrastructure. Enables: patient AVS PDF download, invoice PDF email, CMS-1500 download in patient-app (transparency over own billing), clinical document export.

service_boundary.name: Unstructured (self-hosted OCR/document ETL API)
service_boundary.what: Open-source Python/FastAPI service for partitioning 26+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, images, HTML, PPTX) into structured JSON elements with layout analysis, table detection, and multi-strategy OCR (hi_res/fast/ocr_only). Official unstructured-js-client (TypeScript) on npm. Self-hosted via Docker. Fills: document-core-service (clean slate) ingestion pipeline, OCR extraction for uploaded patient documents, consent form parsing, lab result PDF ingestion.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source Python/FastAPI service for partitioning 26+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, images, HTML, PPTX) into structured JSON elements with layout analysis, table detection, and multi-strategy OCR (hi_res/fast/ocr_only).
