Official unstructured-js-client (TypeScript) on npm. Self-hosted via Docker. Fills: document-core-service (clean slate) ingestion pipeline, OCR extraction for uploaded patient documents, consent form parsing, lab result PDF ingestion.
service_boundary.license: Apache-2.0 (open source core). Unstructured Platform (enterprise) is commercial SaaS.
service_boundary.stack_fit: unstructured-js-client is a plain TypeScript HTTP client - direct import in NestJS document-core-service. Service URL is env-configurable (local or Platform API). Partition results are JSON arrays of typed elements (Title/NarrativeText/Table/Image) - Zod schemas wrap them at ingestion boundary.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: unstructured-api Docker container on K8s. BYO-3rd-party: point js-client at Unstructured Platform API (SaaS). Dual satisfied.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: Service: document-core-service (currently clean slate) becomes the owner of the Unstructured provider port. SDK pkg: @unstructured-io/unstructured-client (or js-client) imported as the ingestion provider. Emits document.partitioned events to Kafka for downstream consumers.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Enables: ingest scanned patient records into structured data (problems, meds, allergies from faxed/uploaded documents), parse insurance cards, extract lab values from PDF results for the patient-experience read model.

service_boundary.name: Meilisearch (Community Edition)
service_boundary.what: Rust-based full-text + hybrid (BM25 + vector/semantic) search engine with typo tolerance, facets, tenant tokens for multi-tenancy. Fills taxonomy gaps: platform.search (search-service FHIR-param mapping enrichment from weaker-than-source-enrichments), patient/record search UX (patient-search), and the FilterBar/SearchShell ui-kit archetype contract lint gate (ui-kit-archetypes). Covers the `search-service` local module.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Rust-based full-text + hybrid (BM25 + vector/semantic) search engine with typo tolerance, facets, tenant tokens for multi-tenancy. Fills taxonomy gaps: platform.search (search-service FHIR-param mapping enrichment from weaker-than-source-enrichments), patient/record search UX (patient-search), and the FilterBar/SearchShell ui-kit archetype contract lint gate (ui-kit-archetypes). Covers the `search-service` local module.
service_boundary.license: MIT (Community Edition, v1.28+ binary). Enterprise Edition (sharding only) = BUSL-1.1, production-requires commercial. CE is safe-permissive for self-host.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Official `meilisearch` npm package (TypeScript). `nestjs-meilisearch` v3.0.0 NestJS module available. Self-hosted single binary or Docker. Multi-tenant via signed tenant tokens (JWT, scoped filter_by rules). Hybrid search (BM25 + vector via OpenAI/HuggingFace embeddings) is stable since v1.9+; semanticRatio tunable. Integrates directly with search-service NestJS module via HTTP SDK. Drizzle/Postgres stays source of truth; Meilisearch is the index layer.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted CE (first-party, Zarf/Helm/K8s compatible). BYO: Meilisearch Cloud SaaS at meilisearch.com. Both supported.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/search-sdk wrapping meilisearch-js) + codegen-emitter (search-emit: index definition per .tsp entity, FHIR search-param mapping table, FilterBar contract lint gate)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Patient/user-facing instant search (sub-50ms) with plain-language typo tolerance. Tenant tokens scope each person to their own data. FHIR search-param semantics mappable on top of the index layer.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Verdict rationale - confirmed-trial, not confirmed-adopt. Adopt is blocked by: (1) CVE-2026-57823 directly undermines the tenant token isolation claim for patient data - must be confirmed resolved at the pinned version before patient-facing
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is already in the stack. PostgreSQL (CNPG, already deployed) with pg_search (ParadeDB BM25 extension) + pgvector delivers hybrid search (BM25 + vector) without adding a new stateful service. Benchmarks show PG GIN/BM25 at P50 4ms latency for boolean queries. ParadeDB pg_search is production-stable as of 2025, runs inside CNPG, and CNPG already has Citus for multi-tenancy. This eliminates: a new binary to air-gap, a new Helm chart, a sync layer, tenant token JWT management, an embeddings sidecar, and the CE/EE binary tracking burden. The ponytail ladder stops here (rung 4: native platform feature / already-installed dependency). The case for Meilisearch must justify why pg_search + pgvector inside existing CNPG is insufficient - specifically typo tolerance and sub-50ms instant-search UX. pg_search does not provide typo tolerance (fuzzy match via trigrams is possible but inferior). For patient-facing instant search with typo tolerance, Meilisearch's UX delta is real. For internal FHIR parameter search and FilterBar contract linting, pg_search likely covers it.

service_boundary.name: Apache Superset (embedded analytics)
service_boundary.what: Self-hosted BI/dashboard platform with embedded SDK (iframe + guest token + RLS). Fills: reports-service Superset-embed path (already referenced locally but gaps exist in multi-tenant RLS wiring + NestJS token minting). Covers taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (Superset-embed slice of reports-service), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards, crm-insurance-broker.platform.reporting. Does NOT replace the ReportDefinition/field-catalog generator gap - that stays in reports-sdk.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Self-hosted BI/dashboard platform with embedded SDK (iframe + guest token + RLS). Fills: reports-service Superset-embed path (already referenced locally but gaps exist in multi-tenant RLS wiring + NestJS token minting). Covers taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (Superset-embed slice of reports-service), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards, crm-insurance-broker.platform.reporting. Does NOT replace the ReportDefinition/field-catalog generator gap - that stays in reports-sdk.
service_boundary.stack_fit: NestJS backend calls POST /api/v1/security/guest_token/ with service-account credentials, returns short-lived JWT scoped to tenant dashboards + RLS clause (e.g. tenant_id='X'). Frontend embeds via @superset-ui/embedded-sdk iframe. Wrapper needed: NestJS SupersetService that mints tokens per request. Does NOT plug into Drizzle/TypeSpec directly - it reads from its own DB (Postgres) that mirrors or connects to the data warehouse layer.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: Docker/Helm/K8s (Zarf-packageable). BYO: Preset.io managed Superset. Both viable.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service/ui-kit (SupersetService in reports-service + ui-app-emit ReportViewer archetype wired to embedded-sdk)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person-facing 'my analytics' views (patient outcome trends, claim history charts, invoice aging) served as scoped guest-token dashboards. Management surface: full BI explorer for admin/clinical users. Same Superset instance, different guest token scopes.
service_boundary.verify_notes:
DOWNGRADE FROM ADOPT TO TRIAL. Three issues make "adopt" premature:

1. RLS security track record is non-trivial in a HIPAA-adjacent system. OX Security found an RLS bypass via subquery injection in v4.1.3 (users can reference tables outsid
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: No drop-in simpler alternative exists for this specific use case. Grafana targets ops/metrics, not SQL-explorer BI; its multi-tenant embedded guest story is weaker. Metabase is simpler to operate but carries LGPL (commercial use requires paid Enterprise tier) and its embedded multi-tenant support is less mature. Scratch-build is infeasible at comparable feature depth. Superset is the correct pick on the build-vs-buy axis. The ponytail ladder stops here: no already-installed dep, no stdlib, no native platform feature covers embedded multi-tenant BI dashboards.

service_boundary.name: Typesense
service_boundary.what: High-QPS in-memory fuzzy search engine with scoped API keys for multi-tenancy, native vector + hybrid search, and Raft-based HA clustering. Alternative to Meilisearch for higher-throughput search (10K+ QPS) or when HA clustering is the primary constraint. Covers same taxonomy as Meilisearch (platform.search, patient-search). Reference/trial over adopt because Meilisearch is the better default for CuraOS's scale and license position.
service_boundary.taxonomy: High-QPS in-memory fuzzy search engine with scoped API keys for multi-tenancy, native vector + hybrid search, and Raft-based HA clustering. Alternative to Meilisearch for higher-throughput search (10K+ QPS) or when HA clustering is the primary constraint. Covers same taxonomy as Meilisearch (platform.search, patient-search). Reference/trial over adopt because Meilisearch is the better default for CuraOS's scale and license position.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Official `typesense` npm client (TypeScript, v3.0.6). NestJS integration via community packages or direct HTTP. Scoped API keys (HMAC-signed with embedded filter_by) = production-grade multi-tenancy. Hybrid search: keyword + vector in one query. HA via Raft (3-node). GPL-3 server: OK to self-host as a service, but must not link server code into CuraOS build.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted binary/Docker (first-party). Typesense Cloud SaaS as BYO option.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (same @curaos/search-sdk abstraction layer that can swap Meilisearch for Typesense backend)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Same as Meilisearch. Scoped keys let person search only their own records. Higher QPS headroom useful if patient/user concurrency is high.
service_boundary.verify_notes: Sole differentiators claimed by candidate -- higher QPS, Raft HA -- are either refuted (QPS) or overstated (Raft gives HA but not write-sharding; Meilisearch has equivalent read HA). Typesense's Raft limitation (entire dataset replicated pe
service_boundary.simpler_alternative: Meilisearch CE (MIT, already designated platform.search primary): independent 2026 benchmarks show Meilisearch outperforms Typesense at the relevant QPS range -- 1200 QPS saturation vs 800 QPS for Typesense on identical hardware (c5.xlarge), with 3.2x lower p99 latency at 500 QPS (28ms vs 91ms). The candidate's 10K+ QPS throughput-advantage premise is from Typesense's own marketing page, not third-party data, and is inverted by current evidence. Meilisearch also has scoped API key multi-tenancy (filter_by scope), same HA read-scaling story, and zero GPL distribution overhead. PostgreSQL tsvector on CNPG covers CuraOS's current scale with zero new service.

service_boundary.name: OpenSearch
service_boundary.what: Apache 2.0 fork of Elasticsearch 7.10 by AWS. Full-text + analytics + ML (neural search plugin) at large scale. Relevant for: FHIR search-param semantics at clinical scale, clinical document indexing, and the 'open search analytics' dimension of reports-service (if Superset is insufficient for operational analytics). Covers: search-service FHIR-param mapping, platform-reporting operational logs queries. Best when data volume, complex aggregations, or FHIR-native search behavior (like `_includes`, compartment search) are needed.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 fork of Elasticsearch 7.10 by AWS. Full-text + analytics + ML (neural search plugin) at large scale. Relevant for: FHIR search-param semantics at clinical scale, clinical document indexing, and the 'open search analytics' dimension of reports-service (if Superset is insufficient for operational analytics). Covers: search-service FHIR-param mapping, platform-reporting operational logs queries. Best when data volume, complex aggregations, or FHIR-native search behavior (like `_includes`, compartment search) are needed.
service_boundary.stack_fit: npm client `@opensearch-project/opensearch` Apache-2.0. NestJS wraps via service class. Multi-tenancy via index-level access control + document-level security plugin (Apache 2.0 in OpenSearch, unlike Elasticsearch's paid tier). JVM overhead significant: each node 2-4GB RAM minimum. Use when Meilisearch hits limits (complex aggregations, FHIR search `$everything`, analytics queries).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted Docker/K8s (Zarf-packageable). AWS OpenSearch Service as BYO cloud option. HAPI-FHIR natively integrates with both.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service (optional search-service backend adapter for OpenSearch; search-sdk abstracts Meilisearch vs OpenSearch behind same interface)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person-facing clinical search ('my records', 'find my encounter') at FHIR compartment scope. Management: clinical document search across patient population, quality measure cohort queries.

service_boundary.name: Qdrant
service_boundary.what: Rust vector DB with payload filtering, sparse+dense hybrid search, and tenant isolation via payload index. Fills the vector search gap when pgvector is insufficient (>5M vectors, higher recall requirements, dedicated vector service). Covers: taxonomy platform.search (semantic dimension), CDS embedding queries, terminology similarity beyond SNOMED tree. Upgrade path from pgvector, not the starting point.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Rust vector DB with payload filtering, sparse+dense hybrid search, and tenant isolation via payload index. Fills the vector search gap when pgvector is insufficient (>5M vectors, higher recall requirements, dedicated vector service). Covers: taxonomy platform.search (semantic dimension), CDS embedding queries, terminology similarity beyond SNOMED tree. Upgrade path from pgvector, not the starting point.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Official `@qdrant/js-client-rest` (Apache-2.0, TypeScript). NestJS wrapper as a thin service module. Payload filtering is part of the indexing pipeline (unlike Weaviate post-filter) = fast filtered vector search. Operates alongside Postgres: Postgres is source of truth, Qdrant is the vector index. Benchmark P99 at 10M vectors: ~12ms vs Weaviate ~16ms, Milvus ~18ms.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted single binary/Docker/Helm (Zarf-packageable). Qdrant Cloud as BYO cloud option.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/vector-sdk wrapping @qdrant/js-client-rest, switchable with pgvector adapter)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Semantic 'find similar clinical notes', 'recommended care actions', 'similar patients' (PHI-gated). Person-facing: 'related health topics' search. Management: cohort similarity for quality measures.

service_boundary.name: Cube (semantic layer / headless BI)
service_boundary.what: Open-source TypeScript+Rust semantic layer: defines metrics, dimensions, joins once in code; exposes SQL, REST, GraphQL, and MCP APIs to downstream consumers (Superset, embedded charts, AI agents). Fills: the field-catalog-from-contracts generator gap in platform-reporting-builder (ReportDefinition field resolution) and the semantic consistency layer between reports-service and Superset. Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (semantic/field-catalog slice).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-source TypeScript+Rust semantic layer: defines metrics, dimensions, joins once in code; exposes SQL, REST, GraphQL, and MCP APIs to downstream consumers (Superset, embedded charts, AI agents). Fills: the field-catalog-from-contracts generator gap in platform-reporting-builder (ReportDefinition field resolution) and the semantic consistency layer between reports-service and Superset. Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (semantic/field-catalog slice).
service_boundary.license: Apache 2.0 (Cube Core). Cube Cloud = commercial SaaS.
service_boundary.stack_fit: NestJS calls Cube REST/SQL API to resolve metric queries. Cube data model in TypeScript (cube.js files) defines entities and joins from the same Postgres DB that Drizzle manages. Pre-aggregations cached in CubeStore (Rust). Superset can connect to Cube via SQL API as a data source. No Drizzle-native integration but Postgres compatibility means Cube reads same tables. TypeSpec contract -> Cube data model generation is a generator gap that can be filled.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted Docker/Helm (Zarf-packageable). Cube Cloud as BYO managed option.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (cube-emit: generate .js cube data model stubs from .tsp entity contracts; wires into reports-service field-catalog generator closure)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person-facing KPI home ('my metrics': my outstanding balance, my care gaps count, my order status) served via Cube metric definitions scoped by tenant/user. Management: full BI metric governance preventing definitional drift across services.

service_boundary.name: Evidence (BI-as-code, embedded reports)
service_boundary.what: SQL + Markdown framework for building version-controlled, embedded data reports and dashboards. Fills: platform-reporting-builder's scheduled/static report delivery slice (ReportDefinition scheduled rendering), and the 'reports as code' pattern for internal/clinical operational reports. Alternative to fully dynamic Superset for the subset of reports that are best expressed as code artifacts (eCQM reports, billing summaries, operational KPIs). Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (scheduled/static slice).
service_boundary.taxonomy: SQL + Markdown framework for building version-controlled, embedded data reports and dashboards. Fills: platform-reporting-builder's scheduled/static report delivery slice (ReportDefinition scheduled rendering), and the 'reports as code' pattern for internal/clinical operational reports. Alternative to fully dynamic Superset for the subset of reports that are best expressed as code artifacts (eCQM reports, billing summaries, operational KPIs). Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (scheduled/static slice).
service_boundary.license: MIT (core framework). Evidence Studio (web IDE) = commercial.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Reports authored as .md + SQL files; compiled to static site or embedded via Evidence SDK. Row-level security and API-driven session management for per-tenant embedding. Deploys to any static host or own infra. Does NOT plug into Drizzle directly - queries Postgres via SQL. Weaker fit for dynamic user-authored reports (Superset is better there); stronger fit for developer-defined BI artifacts checked into version control alongside service code.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (static deploy or Node server). Evidence Cloud as BYO option.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (report-emit: scaffold .md report templates per service contract entity; wired into reports-service scheduled job output)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person-facing static reports: my encounter summary, my lab trends. Less suited for interactive ad-hoc queries. Management: quality measure reports, billing summaries checked into code alongside QRDA output.

service_boundary.name: Metabase
service_boundary.what: Self-hosted BI tool with a visual query builder and dashboards. Listed in research seeds for comparison. Covered here to document the rejection verdict. AGPL-3 license on the open-source version triggers legal review when embedded in a closed-source SaaS product. Multi-tenancy and white-labeling require the Enterprise plan (significant annual cost). No TypeSpec/contract-first integration path. Superset (Apache 2.0) + Cube (Apache 2.0) covers the same surface without the license and cost risks.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Self-hosted BI tool with a visual query builder and dashboards. Listed in research seeds for comparison. Covered here to document the rejection verdict. AGPL-3 license on the open-source version triggers legal review when embedded in a closed-source SaaS product. Multi-tenancy and white-labeling require the Enterprise plan (significant annual cost). No TypeSpec/contract-first integration path. Superset (Apache 2.0) + Cube (Apache 2.0) covers the same surface without the license and cost risks.
service_boundary.license: AGPL-3 (open source). Commercial Enterprise license for embedded/white-label.
service_boundary.stack_fit: No NestJS SDK. Embedded iframes similar to Superset but multi-tenant RLS needs Enterprise plan. No generator path. Heavier Java/Clojure runtime vs Superset Python.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted or Metabase Cloud. Both options exist but both have the license concern for embedded use.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: n/a - rejected
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Good BI UX but org-centric by default. No person-journey re-centering support. Superset + guest tokens achieves the same with better license.

service_boundary.name: cqf-ruler (cqframework / HAPI FHIR)
service_boundary.what: Self-hosted FHIR R4 server (HAPI JPA) with embedded CQL evaluation engine, CDS Hooks endpoint (/cds-services), Measure/$evaluate-measure, PlanDefinition/$apply, and ActivityDefinition/$apply operations. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS Hooks service + PlanDefinition apply), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (Measure evaluation), healthcare.interop.fhir (knowledge artifact repository). Acts as the BYO-3rd-party CDS provider - NestJS services POST to /cds-services/{id} and consume Card responses.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Self-hosted FHIR R4 server (HAPI JPA) with embedded CQL evaluation engine, CDS Hooks endpoint (/cds-services), Measure/$evaluate-measure, PlanDefinition/$apply, and ActivityDefinition/$apply operations. Fills taxonomy gaps: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support (CDS Hooks service + PlanDefinition apply), healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards (Measure evaluation), healthcare.interop.fhir (knowledge artifact repository). Acts as the BYO-3rd-party CDS provider - NestJS services POST to /cds-services/{id} and consume Card responses.
service_boundary.stack_fit: NestJS api-adapter: create a CdsHooksClient service that wraps HTTP POST to cqf-ruler /cds-services/{hookId}. Zod validates Card response shape. Deploy cqf-ruler as a Kubernetes Deployment (docker pull alphora/cqf-ruler). No Java in CuraOS build - purely runtime sidecar. Integrates with CNPG Postgres via HAPI JPA config.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: run cqf-ruler as k8s sidecar in healthstack namespace. BYO-3rd-party: point CdsHooksClient at a customer-provided SMART-on-FHIR CDS server (Epic, Cerner, etc.).
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service (healthstack-cds-service): NestJS api-adapter service wrapping cqf-ruler HTTP; @curaos/cds-sdk exposes typed CdsHooksClient; Helm chart for cqf-ruler sidecar deployment.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: PlanDefinition/$apply can generate CarePlan + Task resources directly - consumable by patient-app care plan view. CDS Hooks Cards return patient-facing suggestions (InfoCard, SuggestionCard) that map to person nudges. Clinician surface receives full card deck (links, suggestions, overrides).

service_boundary.name: Novu (@novu/framework + @novu/react)
service_boundary.what: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Multi-channel notification orchestration (email, SMS, push, chat, in-app) with code-first TypeScript workflow SDK for NestJS and a composable React <Inbox> UI component with real-time WebSocket feed. Covers taxonomy: notify-core-service (orchestration layer), in-app inbox UI, provider abstraction (50+ providers), preference center. Gaps filled: unified channel routing, in-app notification feed, subscriber preference management.
service_boundary.license: MIT (core) + commercial Enterprise Edition (SSO, audit logs)
service_boundary.stack_fit: @novu/framework/nest ships NovuModule.register() - zero wrapper needed for NestJS. @novu/react drops directly into CuraOS ui-kit. backendUrl+socketUrl overrides point React component at self-hosted API. Bun runtime works (bun compile is separate issue, not relevant for service usage). TypeSpec event contract can emit Novu workflow trigger shapes.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose + community Helm chart (not official). BYO-3rd-party: Novu Cloud ($30/mo per 30k runs). Both paths use identical SDK + workflow code - full [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] compliance.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@novu/framework): workflow definitions go in notify-core-service, emitted by codegen mold as .workflow.ts files alongside TypeSpec contracts. @novu/react plugs into ui-kit as InboxWidget.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Preference center per subscriber (person controls which channels/types they receive). In-app Inbox is person-facing real-time feed. Notification history queryable by subscriber. Strong dual-surface fit: person sees their feed; management surface triggers and monitors workflows.

service_boundary.name: Postal (self-hosted SMTP relay + HTTP API)
service_boundary.what: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
service_boundary.taxonomy: Self-hosted mail delivery platform: HTTP API + SMTP relay for transactional and bulk email with bounce processing, DKIM/SPF, click/open tracking, multi-org management UI, and webhook delivery events. Covers the local self-host SMTP tier for notify-core-service email channel - the 'first-party email server' option per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]].
service_boundary.stack_fit: No Node SDK needed - Postal exposes HTTP API; wrap in a thin NestJS provider (PostalProvider implements EmailProvider interface) using `fetch` or axios. Zarf-packageable as a container. Works alongside CuraOS's Postgres CNPG (Postal uses MySQL internally - no conflict, separate service).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Local: Postal self-hosted container. BYO-3rd-party: swap PostalProvider for ResendProvider/SES/Mailgun - same NestJS interface. Full dual compliance.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service EmailProvider port. Codegen emits PostalProvider class from a provider config schema in TypeSpec.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Delivery infrastructure only - person-facing value is reliable, tracked email delivery. Bounce/complaint suppression protects patient email from accidental spam-folder landing.

service_boundary.name: listmonk (self-hosted newsletter + transactional email)
service_boundary.what: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Single-binary Go server (Postgres only) covering subscriber management, list segmentation, campaign scheduling, transactional email API, and drag-and-drop template builder. Covers lifecycle/marketing email lane (appointment campaign sequences, wellness check-ins, bulk patient communications) that Novu is not optimized for. v6.1.0 (Mar 2026) adds multipart plaintext body in transactional API.
service_boundary.stack_fit: AGPL service boundary: CuraOS calls listmonk's REST API from notify-core-service, never imports code. Postgres-native (shares CNPG PostgreSQL cluster or separate instance). Zarf-packageable. No npm SDK but HTTP client wrapper is trivial - 50 lines in a NestJS ListmonkProvider.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only for listmonk itself; but it relays via any SMTP/provider (Postal, SES, Mailgun). Covers local self-host path; BYO-3rd-party is the SMTP relay config.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service ListmonkProvider and list-seeding scripts. TypeSpec contract emits campaign trigger events consumed by listmonk webhook handler.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Patient/customer opt-in/out management per list per person. Double opt-in flows protect patient autonomy. Subscription preference portal can be surfaced in patient-app as communication preferences. Unsubscribe is first-class, not an afterthought.

service_boundary.name: ntfy (self-hosted push notification server)
service_boundary.what: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Pub/sub push notification server via plain HTTP PUT/POST. Covers mobile/desktop push without FCM/APNs vendor lock: ntfy relays to APNs/FCM transparently. Self-hosted Go binary with topic-based ACL. Fills push notification gap for clinical alerts (appointment reminders, lab result ready, care plan updates) on mobile apps. [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]]: self-hosted ntfy OR BYO FCM/APNs direct.
service_boundary.stack_fit: Trivial HTTP integration from NestJS: POST to ntfy topic = push sent. No SDK required; if desired, a 20-line NestJS NtfyProvider wraps it. Works as Novu provider adapter so Novu orchestrates the channel while ntfy handles delivery. Zarf-packageable (single Go binary container).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: ntfy container. BYO-3rd-party: configure Novu's FCM/APNs provider instead - same NestJS interface. iOS relay mode in ntfy itself bridges to Apple APNs without requiring a self-hosted APNs cert.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: notify-core-service PushProvider port. Can also be a Novu custom provider definition.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Person installs ntfy app and subscribes to their patient-specific topic (scoped by tenant+patient ID with ACL). Push reaches them without CuraOS holding their FCM token. Topic-level unsubscribe is person-controlled.

service_boundary.name: Element/Matrix (Synapse + matrix-js-sdk)
service_boundary.what: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Federated, E2E-encrypted, self-hosted secure messaging protocol. Covers secure clinical messaging lane: clinician-to-patient direct messaging, care team group chats, HIPAA-capable audit trail, federated inter-org communication. Used in production for German national healthcare TI-Messenger (millions of users, Matrix Conference 2025). matrix-js-sdk (MIT) provides TypeScript client.
service_boundary.stack_fit: matrix-js-sdk imports into NestJS for bot/bridge operations (programmatic message send from clinical workflows). Element Web embeds in CuraOS React frontend via iframe or as a standalone app. Significant ops footprint (Synapse + Postgres + media server). Not Zarf-trivial but Helm chart exists.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Synapse on CuraOS K8s. BYO-3rd-party: Matrix.org public homeserver or Element Cloud for teams that don't need on-prem. Federation means both options interoperate.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: service: a new secure-messaging-service wraps matrix-js-sdk for server-side operations (room creation, bot messages for system notifications). UI: Element Web or a matrix-react-sdk embedding in patient-app.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Patient owns their Matrix account (portable across homeservers). E2E encryption means even CuraOS cannot read messages at rest. Person-controlled key verification. Care team visible to patient as participants, not hidden from them.

service_boundary.name: Dittofeed (open-source lifecycle messaging)
service_boundary.what: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Multi-channel lifecycle/marketing automation platform (email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack) with user journey builder, segmentation, and broadcasts. TypeScript-native with Node.js + web SDKs. Postgres + ClickHouse + Temporal stack. Fills the customer-lifecycle-messaging lane that Novu (product notifications) and listmonk (bulk campaigns) don't fully cover - specifically behavioral-trigger journeys (e.g., patient onboarding sequence, care plan adherence nudges).
service_boundary.stack_fit: TypeScript-native, Node.js SDK drops into NestJS as a service client. However, the self-host stack adds ClickHouse + Temporal on top of Postgres - significant infra expansion. For CuraOS v1 scope this is likely deferred. REST API usable without SDK.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker Compose / Helm (lite mode: Postgres only, no ClickHouse). BYO-3rd-party: Dittofeed Cloud. Both paths use same SDK.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@dittofeed/sdk-node): notify-core-service tracks events to Dittofeed for journey triggers. TypeSpec events map to Dittofeed track() calls in service-seed emit.
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Journey builder models patient/user lifecycle (onboarding, re-engagement, care adherence). Person-level opt-out and preferences. Segment by clinical attributes (diagnosis group, care plan status) for targeted communications.

service_boundary.name: pocket-id
service_boundary.what: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Passkey-only OIDC IdP (WebAuthn/FIDO2 exclusive, no passwords). Fills: identity-service OIDC front-end (taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles) + SMART-on-FHIR scope issuer. Already deployed locally. BSD-2-Clause Go binary, SQLite default, single container. Provides JWKS endpoint for NestJS JWT guard consumption.
service_boundary.stack_fit: NestJS consumes JWKS + standard OIDC tokens via @nestjs/passport + passport-jwt (no wrapper needed). Pocket-id REST API for user/group management. LDAP sync available. No NestJS-specific SDK but standard OIDC protocol = zero wrapper friction.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (no BYO-3rd-party option -- no cloud variant). Meets the local-first charter; BYO-3rd-party would use Keycloak or Zitadel path instead.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- codegen emitter should emit JWKS-aware JWT guard decorator + SMART scope decorators from TypeSpec contract rather than per-service hand-coded guards
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first auth is the best person-centric auth UX (no password, phishing-resistant). Persons authenticate once via Face ID/Touch ID; identity flows downstream to patient-app, business apps, clinician-app via OIDC. Aligns with person-owns-their-identity directive.

service_boundary.name: Keycloak
service_boundary.what: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full-featured OIDC/OAuth2/SAML IdP with passkey/WebAuthn (native since 26.4, GA), LDAP/AD federation, fine-grained authz services, multi-realm multi-tenant. Fills: BYO-3rd-party OIDC path + SAML federation + enterprise SSO + SMART-on-FHIR scope enforcement. Covers taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (enterprise/SAML tier).
service_boundary.stack_fit: nest-keycloak-connect npm adapter (guards, decorators, resource/scope protection). Multi-tenant via realm-per-tenant or per-org realm resolver. JWKS endpoint for standard JWT validation. Wrapper needed for multi-tenant realm routing but documented pattern exists.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm chart, K8s operator) AND can be used as BYO-3rd-party (managed Keycloak from Red Hat, cloud IAM providers). Satisfies [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] dual requirement.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emitter should generate Keycloak-compatible resource+scope declarations from TypeSpec contract ops, not hand-code resource_access claims per service
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Passkey/FIDO2 native (26.4+), social login, self-service account console for persons to manage their credentials, MFA. WebAuthn conditional UI now in default login forms. Directly supports person-owns-their-auth-devices.

service_boundary.name: Zitadel
service_boundary.what: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Go-based API-first OIDC/OAuth2 IdP with native multi-org model, passkey/WebAuthn, immutable event-sourced audit trail, and TypeScript Actions. Strong BYO-3rd-party candidate. Fills: enterprise OIDC IdP + multi-tenant org management. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (BYO tier).
service_boundary.stack_fit: OIDC standard tokens consumed by NestJS via passport-jwt/JWKS. gRPC management API has Go/TypeScript SDKs. TypeScript Actions for lifecycle hooks. No native NestJS module but standard OIDC = minimal wrapper. Multi-org maps cleanly to CuraOS tenant model.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host (Helm, K8s) + managed Zitadel Cloud. Satisfies dual requirement -- but AGPL means if CuraOS modifies Zitadel source and exposes as SaaS, disclosure required.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/identity-sdk) -- emit Zitadel resource registration API calls from TypeSpec contract during codegen scaffold
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Passkey-first, self-service account management, GDPR-ready event log. Multi-org means each patient/customer org can have their own IdP branding/settings.

service_boundary.name: Cerbos
service_boundary.what: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Open-core policy decision point (PDP) sidecar for ABAC+RBAC+ReBAC. Policies in YAML/Rego, evaluated via gRPC/HTTP. Externalizes authorization from every service. Fills: shared authz policy engine replacing per-service roles.guard sprawl. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (authz layer), workflow-automation.ops.rbac, crm-insurance-broker.platform.role-based-access.
service_boundary.stack_fit: cerbos npm SDK + nestjs-cerbos interceptor pattern. NestJS guard calls Cerbos PDP over gRPC with principal attributes + resource attributes; PDP evaluates YAML policy. ABAC lets policies use tenant context, clinical role, HIPAA consent flags. No wrapper beyond the SDK guard pattern (already documented).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host PDP (open-source, Docker/Helm). Hub (policy management UI) is SaaS-optional; air-gap deployments use local bundle endpoint. Meets dual requirement: self-host PDP always available, Hub as optional BYO-cloud control plane.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec contract resource ops should generate Cerbos resource definitions + policy scaffolds (derived resource.yaml per service), injected into service scaffold by ui-app-emit / emitServiceLive
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Centralizes who-can-see-what logic, enabling privacy-by-policy (clinician only sees consented patient records, person only sees their own data, HIPAA field-level rules as policy YAML). Simplifies adding consent-aware access rules without touching every service.

service_boundary.name: OpenFGA
service_boundary.what: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
service_boundary.taxonomy: CNCF Incubating Zanzibar-inspired ReBAC authorization engine (Auth0/Okta donated). Models authorization as relationship tuples + authorization model DSL. Strong for object-ownership hierarchies (document viewer, org member, folder editor). Fills: fine-grained ReBAC authz layer for document/record ownership and org hierarchy. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (ReBAC tier).
service_boundary.stack_fit: @openfga/sdk npm + @rxap/nest-openfga module. NestJS guard delegates to OpenFGA check API. Tuples stored in Postgres (which CNPG already manages). Fits well for document/care-plan/consent record ownership. ABAC support is newer and less mature than ReBAC.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Docker/Helm) + FGA.dev managed cloud. Satisfies dual requirement.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec resource types should emit OpenFGA type definitions; SDK emit should generate NestJS FGA guard + tuple write helpers per resource type
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: ReBAC naturally encodes person-owns-their-record, patient-can-read-their-own-clinical-document, org-member-can-access-shared-resource. Consent as a relationship tuple is a clean model.

service_boundary.name: SpiceDB
service_boundary.what: Most Zanzibar-faithful open-source permissions database. Zed schema language, caveated relationships (ABAC+ReBAC hybrid), Watch API for cache invalidation, strong consistency. Fills: fine-grained permissions with ABAC caveats if Zanzibar fidelity is required. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (Zanzibar tier).
service_boundary.taxonomy: Most Zanzibar-faithful open-source permissions database. Zed schema language, caveated relationships (ABAC+ReBAC hybrid), Watch API for cache invalidation, strong consistency. Fills: fine-grained permissions with ABAC caveats if Zanzibar fidelity is required. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (Zanzibar tier).
service_boundary.stack_fit: authzed-node npm (gRPC client). No native NestJS module -- requires manual guard wiring. Zed schema language has TypeScript type generator tools (community). More operational complexity than Cerbos (schema migrations, consistency tokens).
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Docker/Helm) + Authzed cloud managed. Satisfies dual requirement.
service_boundary.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- TypeSpec types could emit Zed schema fragments; SDK emitter generates NestJS SpiceDB guard + WriteRelationships helpers
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: Caveated relationships can encode consent state as an attribute directly in the authorization tuple -- patient consent revocation immediately invalidates access without policy rebuild.

service_boundary.name: Cal.com (AGPL v4.9.3 self-hosted)
service_boundary.what: Full scheduling platform: booking pages, availability rules, calendar sync (Google/Outlook), payment, webhooks, multi-user teams. Could serve as the BYO-3rd-party scheduling provider for tenants who want a managed booking page. Fills: external booking link for CuraOS patient-facing appointment self-scheduling (taxonomy: scheduling.patient-self-schedule). AGPL v4.9.3 is the last open-source version; future releases closed-source.
service_boundary.taxonomy: Full scheduling platform: booking pages, availability rules, calendar sync (Google/Outlook), payment, webhooks, multi-user teams. Could serve as the BYO-3rd-party scheduling provider for tenants who want a managed booking page. Fills: external booking link for CuraOS patient-facing appointment self-scheduling (taxonomy: scheduling.patient-self-schedule). AGPL v4.9.3 is the last open-source version; future releases closed-source.
service_boundary.license: AGPL-3.0 (v4.9.3 last open version; future releases proprietary)
service_boundary.stack_fit: Cal.com is a Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL monolith - not NestJS-native. Integration path: deploy Cal.com v4.9.3 as a sidecar service (per [[curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule]] BYO mode), integrate via webhooks into CuraOS Kafka/Redpanda event stream. @calcom/atoms and @calcom/embed-react npm packages call api.cal.com (NOT self-hosted) - not viable for self-hosted-first. Webhook-only integration is the clean path.
service_boundary.local_vs_3rdparty: BYO-3rd-party (tenant brings their Cal.com/Cal.diy instance; CuraOS holds event reference via webhook)
service_boundary.generator_first_target: api-adapter (webhook receiver in scheduling-service: cal.com booking.created -> scheduling.appointment.booked event on Kafka)
service_boundary.person_centric_fit: High for patient self-scheduling (person books their own appointment on a beautiful booking page). Management surface needs CuraOS scheduling-service to hold ground truth. Dual-surface: Cal.com handles the booking UX, CuraOS owns the appointment entity.

reference_only.name: Mastra (@mastra/core + @mastra/nestjs + @mastra/mcp)
reference_only.what: Apache 2.0 TypeScript AI orchestration framework with graph-based workflow engine (.then/.branch/.parallel), agents + tool calling, MCPClient/MCPServer, memory, RAG, and a first-class NestJS adapter that exposes agents/workflows/MCPs as NestJS endpoints with native DI/guards/interceptors. Fills taxonomy_ids: workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (fully absent locally - zero mcp/llm/aiagent matches in automation+workflow+plugin services) and workflow-automation.catalog.mcp-tooling-integration (also absent).
reference_only.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 TypeScript AI orchestration framework with graph-based workflow engine (.then/.branch/.parallel), agents + tool calling, MCPClient/MCPServer, memory, RAG, and a first-class NestJS adapter that exposes agents/workflows/MCPs as NestJS endpoints with native DI/guards/interceptors. Fills taxonomy_ids: workflow-automation.catalog.ai-llm-steps (fully absent locally - zero mcp/llm/aiagent matches in automation+workflow+plugin services) and workflow-automation.catalog.mcp-tooling-integration (also absent).
reference_only.stack_fit: Bun-compatible (deploys to any Node/Bun environment). @mastra/nestjs is a drop-in NestJS module. BYO LLM provider via @ai-sdk/* packages pointing at LiteLLM (workspace-standard). Zod-based tool schemas. No wrapper needed.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-host Mastra on-prem (no external service required); BYO model provider via LiteLLM gateway for any 3rd-party LLM. MCPClient consumes external MCP servers; MCPServer exposes CuraOS flows to external agents.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @mastra/core imported into automation-core-service; contract: add AiAgentStep + AgentTool union to automation.tsp then regenerate @curaos/automation-sdk; codegen-emitter: mcp-server entity emitter in tools/codegen
reference_only.person_centric_fit: AI step framed as consent-gated assistant; person sees intent + plain results; management surface = model config, tool bindings, guardrails, Langfuse observability.
reference_only.verify_notes: Four compounding blockers beyond the supply chain incident: (1) @mastra/nestjs is Express-only in v1 - hard bootstrap failure on any other adapter, constraining CuraOS adapter flexibility forward. (2) Confirmed Bun+Turborepo monorepo breaka
reference_only.simpler_alternative: Yes. CuraOS already has Temporal in workflow-core-service. AI steps as Temporal Activities + direct @ai-sdk/* (Vercel AI SDK) packages wired through LiteLLM cover the stated gap without a new framework layer. @ai-sdk/* is Zod-native, BYO-provider, TypeSpec-contract-friendly, and has no supply chain incidents. This satisfies the ponytail ladder at rung 4 (already-installed-adjacent) rather than importing a new framework with known friction and a fresh state-actor targeting history.

reference_only.name: OpenBao (MPL 2.0 Vault fork)
reference_only.what: Linux Foundation MPL 2.0 fork of HashiCorp Vault (last OSS version), API-compatible with Vault. Backup secrets backend option for teams that already have Vault operational patterns or need dynamic secrets (DB credential rotation, PKI). Fills the same connector OAuth vault gap as Infisical but via Vault-style dynamic secrets and leases.
reference_only.taxonomy: Linux Foundation MPL 2.0 fork of HashiCorp Vault (last OSS version), API-compatible with Vault. Backup secrets backend option for teams that already have Vault operational patterns or need dynamic secrets (DB credential rotation, PKI). Fills the same connector OAuth vault gap as Infisical but via Vault-style dynamic secrets and leases.
reference_only.stack_fit: No TypeScript SDK of its own - use node-vault or raw HTTP calls from NestJS. More operational complexity than Infisical (Vault agent, policies, leases). Better fit if CuraOS needs dynamic credential rotation or PKI for air-gap deployments.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only. No SaaS option. Air-gap friendly.
reference_only.generator_first_target: service: integrations-core-service VaultProvider behind the same connector-auth interface as Infisical (adapter pattern)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Transparent to person; management surface = secrets audit, policy engine, certificate lifecycle.

reference_only.name: Windmill (AGPL - reference only)
reference_only.what: AGPL workflow engine with Bun/Deno TypeScript runtime, sandboxed multi-language script execution, K8s Helm chart, and MCP endpoint generation. Strongest external reference for plugin-runtime-service sandboxed code-step executor (taxonomy_id: workflow-automation.catalog.script-steps - confirmed stub locally). Architecture of windmill-worker deno_executor/python_executor is the reference for how to run untrusted code safely.
reference_only.taxonomy: AGPL workflow engine with Bun/Deno TypeScript runtime, sandboxed multi-language script execution, K8s Helm chart, and MCP endpoint generation. Strongest external reference for plugin-runtime-service sandboxed code-step executor (taxonomy_id: workflow-automation.catalog.script-steps - confirmed stub locally). Architecture of windmill-worker deno_executor/python_executor is the reference for how to run untrusted code safely.
reference_only.stack_fit: Cannot import into CuraOS build (AGPL). Reference the sandbox isolation patterns and TypeScript executor architecture for plugin-runtime-service design. Run as a separate optional service only if CuraOS integrates it behind a service boundary without distributing.

CoverageEligibilityResponse powers up-front copay estimates surfaced in the patient app. ClaimResponse provides the denial reason in structured form that enables patient-readable denial explanations.

reference_only.simpler_alternative: Yes - @medplum/fhirtypes (types-only subset of the same monorepo, no runtime, no MedplumClient, zero dependencies) covers the entire stated need: Claim, ClaimResponse, ExplanationOfBenefit, CoverageEligibilityRequest/Response as strongly-typed R4 interfaces. This is the correct ponytail rung - use the types package, not the full SDK. Secondary alternative for Zod-native validation: @solarahealth/fhir-r4 (MIT), but maintenance is slower. No stdlib or already-installed package covers FHIR R4 contract shapes - an external types package IS the right call here; the question is which one.

reference_only.verify_notes: Downgraded from "trial" to "downgrade-reference" (keep as a named option in the ADR, do not integrate now) for three compounding reasons: (1) ponytail ladder kills it - the need is already met by the existing implementation; (2) curaos-loca
reference_only.simpler_alternative: YES - already installed and fully implemented. accounting-core-service already has a complete double-entry engine on Drizzle + Postgres (CNPG). Ponytail ladder rung 2: the codebase already has it. Adding TB introduces: a second stateful binary to operate and upgrade (TB version must match client exactly - tight coupling), a required Postgres mirror bridge for all reporting (every query goes to Postgres anyway), unconfirmed Bun napi compatibility, a bespoke NestJS wrapper not supported by any generator, and a two-DB consistency problem (TB + Postgres must stay in sync across crashes). The local-vs-3rdparty duality rule (curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule) requires both a self-hosted AND a BYO-3rd-party option. TB has no BYO-3rd-party/SaaS path today (cloud offering is invitation-only and immature as of mid-2026). The fallback being "Postgres Drizzle-based double-entry" is not a BYO lane - it is the same self-hosted stack. The rule is not met.

reference_only.verify_notes: Verdict rationale: downgrade to reference, not adopt. Three converging reasons. (1) Internal prior art wins: gap-analysis.json already recommended port-adapt from OpenEMR as the implementation path. That decision pre-dates this candidate an
reference_only.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is already documented in the codebase. The gap-analysis.json (/.ai-analysis/gap-analysis.json, id: x12-revenue-cycle) explicitly recommends: best_source = OpenEMR (GPL) X125010837P.php + X125010837I.php + ParseERA.php + EDI270.php; suggested_mode = 'E port-adapt (X12 logic) into a NEW @curaos/x12-sdk generator-first package'. X12 segments are ANSI standard (not copyrightable), so porting logic from OpenEMR to fresh TypeScript carries no GPL contamination. This path: (a) was already evaluated and chosen before this candidate appeared, (b) produces the same @curaos/x12-sdk generator-first package, (c) starts from battle-tested segment loop logic used in production EHRs, (d) eliminates the v0.x API-change risk entirely since the SDK owns the contract, (e) eliminates bus-factor dependency on a 5-month-old solo project. EDIFlow is a reference for how to structure the TypeScript adapter layer (clean architecture: Domain format-agnostic, X12 as adapter) - not a dependency to adopt. Additionally, for 270/271 eligibility specifically, Stedi offers a production-grade managed API option that satisfies the local-vs-3rdparty duality rule as the BYO-3rd-party-provider path, while the @curaos/x12-sdk covers the self-hosted path.

Ponytail ladder: the generator is already installed; the missing entities are well-defined domain objects; Mode A sidecar fails rungs 1 and 5 (YAGNI complexity + existing capability path). Mode B (reference-only) passes rung 5: mine Twenty's entity model to accelerate TypeSpec contract design for crm-core, do not run it.

The back-office case management is the management surface. Strong fit for person-centric lens - the person owns their document submission journey.
reference_only.verify_notes: Research value: high -- Three disqualifying findings confirmed from independent sources that the candidate's summary understated or missed. (1) Maintenance risk is acute: the OSS repo is in an officially-unsupported rebuild phase while the
reference_only.simpler_alternative: Yes, for the actual CuraOS use case (insurance policy onboarding document collection + lead qualification review workflow). The CuraOS workflow/BPM engine (the core platform primitive per AGENTS.md §5.0) is the correct host for KYC document-step orchestration - it already owns human task + automation + SLA. Document upload steps are form steps in a BPM flow, not a separate KYC infrastructure. The back-office review surface is the crm-insurance-broker app built on the App/Site Builder. The 3rd-party provider slot (per curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule) is better filled by a purpose-built identity verification vendor API (Persona, Onfido, Sumsub, or their self-hosted equivalents) called from a thin NestJS adapter in crm-core, rather than running a second full NestJS service with a competing Prisma schema. Insurance-specific document types (proof of insurability, policy applications) would need custom Ballerine flow definitions anyway - that work lands in CuraOS BPM config instead, which is the generator-first target.

reference_only.verify_notes: Primary kill reasons: (1) License ambiguity on the renderer itself (OSLv3 vs MIT unresolved; network-copyleft is a hard blocker for CuraOS's self-hosted SaaS distribution model). (2) The drag-drop builder - the stated use-case for the 'work
reference_only.simpler_alternative: YES - already installed. @curaos/ui already ships react-hook-form 7.79.0 + @hookform/resolvers 5.4.0 + zod 4.4.3 + Radix UI primitives. Multi-step wizard = a state machine over RHF steps + Radix Tabs/Dialog - no new dependency. TypeSpec entity -> Zod schema -> RHF form is already the natural generator target and is TypeScript-native, tree-shakeable, and zero OSLv3 risk. RJSF (react-jsonschema-form, MIT, 46k weekly downloads, v5 stable, v6 in beta 2025) is the correct JSON-schema-to-form drop-in if a schema-driven renderer is truly needed - it does NOT require a commercial module for the builder, and its TypeScript types are complete. SurveyJS Form Library is MIT for rendering but survey-creator (drag-drop builder) requires a commercial developer license - same commercial trap as formio's Enterprise Form Builder Module.

reference_only.name: Appsmith (Apache 2.0, self-hosted)
reference_only.what: Apache 2.0 internal-tool/admin-panel builder. Covers `platform-reporting-builder` gap (ad-hoc report/query builder, per-user dashboard layout) as a self-hosted sidecar. Not an npm-import; embedding via iframe. 25+ DB connectors; drag-drop widget canvas; custom React component support via iframe widget. Generator-first target: reports-service ReportDefinition surface (BYO-3rd-party mode = Appsmith; first-party = Puck-based builder-core).
reference_only.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 internal-tool/admin-panel builder. Covers `platform-reporting-builder` gap (ad-hoc report/query builder, per-user dashboard layout) as a self-hosted sidecar. Not an npm-import; embedding via iframe. 25+ DB connectors; drag-drop widget canvas; custom React component support via iframe widget. Generator-first target: reports-service ReportDefinition surface (BYO-3rd-party mode = Appsmith; first-party = Puck-based builder-core).
reference_only.stack_fit: Runs as a Docker/K8s sidecar. NestJS services registered as REST datasources. Iframe embed into builder-studio. Not React-component native. Best as a BYO-3rd-party option for tenants who already use Appsmith, while Puck-based builder-core is the first-party path.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Both modes: self-hosted Appsmith as BYO-3rd-party admin builder + Puck/builder-core as first-party. Satisfies dual-option requirement.
reference_only.generator_first_target: reports-service ReportDefinition (Appsmith as optional BYO viewer for power users; first-party ReportDefinition entity feeds Gotenberg/Superset per gap-analysis)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Appsmith is management-surface-only by design (internal tools). Person surfaces remain in the CuraOS React apps. No person-facing use case for Appsmith directly.

reference_only.name: DBOS Transact TypeScript (dbos-inc/dbos-transact-ts)
reference_only.what: Lightweight durable execution library that uses YOUR existing PostgreSQL as the workflow state store - no separate workflow server. Decorator-based (@DBOS.workflow, @DBOS.step, @DBOS.transaction). v3 adds decorator-free API. Fills: zero-ops durable execution alternative where adding a Temporal cluster is too heavy; fills saga/compensation pattern at service layer. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.saga
reference_only.taxonomy: Lightweight durable execution library that uses YOUR existing PostgreSQL as the workflow state store - no separate workflow server. Decorator-based (@DBOS.workflow, @DBOS.step, @DBOS.transaction). v3 adds decorator-free API. Fills: zero-ops durable execution alternative where adding a Temporal cluster is too heavy; fills saga/compensation pattern at service layer. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.saga
reference_only.stack_fit: Integrates as a NestJS provider via dbos-nest. Uses Drizzle-compatible PostgreSQL (same CNPG cluster). Decorators align with NestJS DI pattern. Bun-compatible (pure TS, no native modules). No separate server to deploy. Checkpoints steps in Postgres tables co-located with application DB. Drizzle schema coexistence needs coordination on schema namespace.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-host only (library runs inside app process, Postgres is state store). No managed cloud offering needed. DBOS Cloud exists but optional.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter can scaffold @DBOS.workflow class + @DBOS.step methods from TypeSpec operation sets
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Saga/compensation pattern maps well to multi-step person journeys (e.g. claim submission -> eligibility check -> auth -> submit -> ERA posting as durable steps that resume after crash).
reference_only.verify_notes: Three blocking issues, not risks: (1) local-vs-3rdparty rule violation: DBOS is baked into application code via decorators; it cannot be swapped for Temporal or another provider via runtime config. The rule requires every workflow area to e
reference_only.simpler_alternative: YES - ponytail ladder yields two better-fit options already in stack. (1) Outbox + Kafka/Redpanda + idempotency keys: already installed, covers saga/compensation at service layer without a new library dependency. Sufficient for most multi-step journeys. Ceiling: no step-level replay on crash; requires consumer idempotency discipline. (2) Temporal self-hosted: already named in curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule as the canonical Workflow Execution local option. Provides human-task signals, per-namespace tenant isolation, battle-hardened multi-tenant primitives, and a 3rd-party provider path (Temporal Cloud) via config - exactly what the local-vs-3rdparty rule requires. DBOS fills a niche between these two but does not satisfy the provider-abstraction duality the rule demands.

reference_only.name: Inngest (inngest/inngest)
reference_only.what: Event-driven durable step function platform: fan-out, parallel steps, retries, scheduled crons, Postgres or SQLite state. SSPL license (server) with DOSP to Apache-2.0 after 3 years. Fills: event-triggered background workflow alternative if Temporal ops burden is too high. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.event-driven, platform.workflow.background-jobs
reference_only.taxonomy: Event-driven durable step function platform: fan-out, parallel steps, retries, scheduled crons, Postgres or SQLite state. SSPL license (server) with DOSP to Apache-2.0 after 3 years. Fills: event-triggered background workflow alternative if Temporal ops burden is too high. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.event-driven, platform.workflow.background-jobs
reference_only.stack_fit: TypeScript SDK npm-importable. Inngest server is a binary/Docker image. Self-host with --postgres-uri (v1.4.0+). NestJS integration via direct SDK usage. Bun-compatible SDK. SSPL on server binary means using it for internal CuraOS workflows is fine; offering it as a hosted SaaS to customers without source disclosure would trigger SSPL.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: Docker/Helm + Postgres (SSPL, legal review needed for on-prem product distribution). BYO-3rd-party: Inngest Cloud. Hybrid viable.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter scaffolds inngest.createFunction() from AsyncAPI event definitions
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Event-triggered workflows fire on domain events (patient.admitted, claim.submitted) and fan out to parallel steps - natural fit for care coordination automation.

reference_only.name: BullMQ (@nestjs/bull + bullmq)
reference_only.what: Redis-backed job queue with priorities, delayed jobs, rate limiting, repeatable jobs, flows/dependencies, Bull Board UI. NestJS first-class via @nestjs/bull / @nestjs/bullmq. Fills: high-throughput background job processing where pg-boss hits throughput ceiling. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.background-jobs, platform.workflow.high-throughput-queue
reference_only.taxonomy: Redis-backed job queue with priorities, delayed jobs, rate limiting, repeatable jobs, flows/dependencies, Bull Board UI. NestJS first-class via @nestjs/bull / @nestjs/bullmq. Fills: high-throughput background job processing where pg-boss hits throughput ceiling. Taxonomy: platform.workflow.background-jobs, platform.workflow.high-throughput-queue
reference_only.stack_fit: First-class NestJS support (@nestjs/bullmq). Bun-compatible. Requires Redis (adds infra if not already present). Flows feature supports parent/child job dependencies (lightweight saga). Drizzle/Postgres used for application data; Redis purely for queue state. CuraOS already uses PostgreSQL-first strategy - adds Redis dependency.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: BullMQ library + self-hosted Redis. BYO-3rd-party: Redis Cloud / AWS ElastiCache / Upstash. Both standard.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter scaffolds @Processor() + @Process() handlers from TypeSpec async operation definitions
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Rate-limited notification queues prevent alert fatigue for patients. Repeatable jobs drive medication adherence reminders and care-gap outreach on schedule.

reference_only.name: DocuSeal + @docuseal/api
reference_only.what: Lighter-weight self-hosted e-sign alternative (Ruby on Rails backend, SQLite default). ~17k GitHub stars, TypeScript/JS SDK published as @docuseal/api (v1.0.23). Simpler deploy than Documenso; supports more field types, SMS delivery. AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b) additional terms: API access and embedding require paid Pro On-Premises license ($20/user/month). Best fit as the BYO-3rd-party adapter option behind the esign-core provider port, paired with Documenso as the default self-hosted provider.
reference_only.taxonomy: Lighter-weight self-hosted e-sign alternative (Ruby on Rails backend, SQLite default). ~17k GitHub stars, TypeScript/JS SDK published as @docuseal/api (v1.0.23). Simpler deploy than Documenso; supports more field types, SMS delivery. AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b) additional terms: API access and embedding require paid Pro On-Premises license ($20/user/month). Best fit as the BYO-3rd-party adapter option behind the esign-core provider port, paired with Documenso as the default self-hosted provider.
reference_only.license: AGPL-3.0 + Section 7(b). Embedding/API require $20/user/month Pro On-Premises license.
reference_only.stack_fit: sdk (@docuseal/api) is plain TS, no framework coupling. NestJS module wraps it at provider level. Zod wraps response types. Service is a separate Docker container.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host option: simpler deploy than Documenso (SQLite ok). BYO-3rd-party: point sdk at DocuSeal Cloud. However API is paywalled even on self-hosted - legal-review needed before production use.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: @docuseal/api as alternative provider behind the esign-core 'provider port' - switchable via env config in NestJS DI (same contract, swap implementation).
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Same person journey as Documenso. API covers template-based submission creation, embedded signing widget, webhook events. Field-type breadth (signature, date, text, checkbox, radio, image) supports clinical consent forms.
reference_only.verify_notes: Candidate recommendation was "trial" with self-hosted as primary. Downgraded to reference because: (1) API paywall on self-hosted makes it non-functional as a self-hosted provider without paid seats - violates local-vs-3rdparty-rule's self-
reference_only.simpler_alternative: Documenso is the strictly better self-hosted default: AGPL-3.0 with NO Section 7(b) paywall, full API access free on self-hosted, TypeScript/Next.js native stack (closer to Bun ecosystem), TypeScript SDK exists (github.com/documenso/sdk-typescript), PostgreSQL-native, community Helm chart issue open (#1345). Documenso satisfies the local-vs-3rdparty duality without a license key gate. DocuSeal can remain documented as the BYO-3rd-party cloud option ONLY (point SDK at DocuSeal Cloud for teams that already pay), but should not be the self-hosted provider behind the esign-core port.

reference_only.name: Tesseract.js (pure-JS OCR, lightweight)
reference_only.what: WebAssembly port of Tesseract OCR engine, pure JavaScript/TypeScript, 100+ languages, no native deps. 36k GitHub stars, MIT license. Fills: lightweight OCR for CuraOS in environments where Unstructured container is overkill (e.g., extracting text from uploaded images in personal-app, processing single-page insurance cards, extracting handwritten field values from scanned forms).
reference_only.taxonomy: WebAssembly port of Tesseract OCR engine, pure JavaScript/TypeScript, 100+ languages, no native deps. 36k GitHub stars, MIT license. Fills: lightweight OCR for CuraOS in environments where Unstructured container is overkill (e.g., extracting text from uploaded images in personal-app, processing single-page insurance cards, extracting handwritten field values from scanned forms).
reference_only.stack_fit: Direct npm import in document-core-service or inline in any NestJS service that needs quick OCR. Works in Bun (WASM). Accuracy significantly lower than Unstructured hi_res for complex layouts. Use only for single-column text / simple image extraction.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (npm import, WASM). No network dependency.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: secondary OCR provider behind the document-core-service provider port (fast/simple path, Unstructured as hi_res path).
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Edge case: extract text from scanned insurance card photo uploaded via personal-app. Not suitable for clinical document ingestion pipeline (use Unstructured for that).

Fills: PDF form filling for CMS-1500/UB-04 (overlay field values onto existing government form PDFs), PDF merging for report assembly, watermarking signed documents. Distinct from generation (Gotenberg/pdfme) - this is for surgical manipulation of existing PDF bytes.

reference_only.name: Typesense
reference_only.what: High-QPS in-memory fuzzy search engine with scoped API keys for multi-tenancy, native vector + hybrid search, and Raft-based HA clustering. Alternative to Meilisearch for higher-throughput search (10K+ QPS) or when HA clustering is the primary constraint. Covers same taxonomy as Meilisearch (platform.search, patient-search). Reference/trial over adopt because Meilisearch is the better default for CuraOS's scale and license position.
reference_only.taxonomy: High-QPS in-memory fuzzy search engine with scoped API keys for multi-tenancy, native vector + hybrid search, and Raft-based HA clustering. Alternative to Meilisearch for higher-throughput search (10K+ QPS) or when HA clustering is the primary constraint. Covers same taxonomy as Meilisearch (platform.search, patient-search). Reference/trial over adopt because Meilisearch is the better default for CuraOS's scale and license position.
reference_only.stack_fit: Official `typesense` npm client (TypeScript, v3.0.6). NestJS integration via community packages or direct HTTP. Scoped API keys (HMAC-signed with embedded filter_by) = production-grade multi-tenancy. Hybrid search: keyword + vector in one query. HA via Raft (3-node). GPL-3 server: OK to self-host as a service, but must not link server code into CuraOS build.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted binary/Docker (first-party). Typesense Cloud SaaS as BYO option.
reference_only.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (same @curaos/search-sdk abstraction layer that can swap Meilisearch for Typesense backend)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Same as Meilisearch. Scoped keys let person search only their own records. Higher QPS headroom useful if patient/user concurrency is high.
reference_only.verify_notes: Sole differentiators claimed by candidate -- higher QPS, Raft HA -- are either refuted (QPS) or overstated (Raft gives HA but not write-sharding; Meilisearch has equivalent read HA). Typesense's Raft limitation (entire dataset replicated pe
reference_only.simpler_alternative: Meilisearch CE (MIT, already designated platform.search primary): independent 2026 benchmarks show Meilisearch outperforms Typesense at the relevant QPS range -- 1200 QPS saturation vs 800 QPS for Typesense on identical hardware (c5.xlarge), with 3.2x lower p99 latency at 500 QPS (28ms vs 91ms). The candidate's 10K+ QPS throughput-advantage premise is from Typesense's own marketing page, not third-party data, and is inverted by current evidence. Meilisearch also has scoped API key multi-tenancy (filter_by scope), same HA read-scaling story, and zero GPL distribution overhead. PostgreSQL tsvector on CNPG covers CuraOS's current scale with zero new service.

reference_only.name: OpenSearch
reference_only.what: Apache 2.0 fork of Elasticsearch 7.10 by AWS. Full-text + analytics + ML (neural search plugin) at large scale. Relevant for: FHIR search-param semantics at clinical scale, clinical document indexing, and the 'open search analytics' dimension of reports-service (if Superset is insufficient for operational analytics). Covers: search-service FHIR-param mapping, platform-reporting operational logs queries. Best when data volume, complex aggregations, or FHIR-native search behavior (like `_includes`, compartment search) are needed.
reference_only.taxonomy: Apache 2.0 fork of Elasticsearch 7.10 by AWS. Full-text + analytics + ML (neural search plugin) at large scale. Relevant for: FHIR search-param semantics at clinical scale, clinical document indexing, and the 'open search analytics' dimension of reports-service (if Superset is insufficient for operational analytics). Covers: search-service FHIR-param mapping, platform-reporting operational logs queries. Best when data volume, complex aggregations, or FHIR-native search behavior (like `_includes`, compartment search) are needed.
reference_only.stack_fit: npm client `@opensearch-project/opensearch` Apache-2.0. NestJS wraps via service class. Multi-tenancy via index-level access control + document-level security plugin (Apache 2.0 in OpenSearch, unlike Elasticsearch's paid tier). JVM overhead significant: each node 2-4GB RAM minimum. Use when Meilisearch hits limits (complex aggregations, FHIR search `$everything`, analytics queries).
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted Docker/K8s (Zarf-packageable). AWS OpenSearch Service as BYO cloud option. HAPI-FHIR natively integrates with both.
reference_only.generator_first_target: service (optional search-service backend adapter for OpenSearch; search-sdk abstracts Meilisearch vs OpenSearch behind same interface)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Person-facing clinical search ('my records', 'find my encounter') at FHIR compartment scope. Management: clinical document search across patient population, quality measure cohort queries.

reference_only.name: Evidence (BI-as-code, embedded reports)
reference_only.what: SQL + Markdown framework for building version-controlled, embedded data reports and dashboards. Fills: platform-reporting-builder's scheduled/static report delivery slice (ReportDefinition scheduled rendering), and the 'reports as code' pattern for internal/clinical operational reports. Alternative to fully dynamic Superset for the subset of reports that are best expressed as code artifacts (eCQM reports, billing summaries, operational KPIs). Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (scheduled/static slice).
reference_only.taxonomy: SQL + Markdown framework for building version-controlled, embedded data reports and dashboards. Fills: platform-reporting-builder's scheduled/static report delivery slice (ReportDefinition scheduled rendering), and the 'reports as code' pattern for internal/clinical operational reports. Alternative to fully dynamic Superset for the subset of reports that are best expressed as code artifacts (eCQM reports, billing summaries, operational KPIs). Taxonomy: platform-reporting-builder (scheduled/static slice).
reference_only.license: MIT (core framework). Evidence Studio (web IDE) = commercial.
reference_only.stack_fit: Reports authored as .md + SQL files; compiled to static site or embedded via Evidence SDK. Row-level security and API-driven session management for per-tenant embedding. Deploys to any static host or own infra. Does NOT plug into Drizzle directly - queries Postgres via SQL. Weaker fit for dynamic user-authored reports (Superset is better there); stronger fit for developer-defined BI artifacts checked into version control alongside service code.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted (static deploy or Node server). Evidence Cloud as BYO option.
reference_only.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (report-emit: scaffold .md report templates per service contract entity; wired into reports-service scheduled job output)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Person-facing static reports: my encounter summary, my lab trends. Less suited for interactive ad-hoc queries. Management: quality measure reports, billing summaries checked into code alongside QRDA output.

reference_only.taxonomy: Experimental Go CQL execution engine from Google with Beam job for large-population eCQM runs. Fills healthcare.platform.reports-dashboards at massive scale (BigQuery-backed). Not a Node/TS library.
