reference_only.person_centric_fit: Excellent reference for the patient self-scheduling journey (person picks type, location, provider, time slot). Management surface: provider availability config, multi-location scheduling admin.

reference_only.name: Vite (standalone, framework-agnostic)
reference_only.what: Use Vite directly (not via TanStack Start wrapper) as the build tool for custom SSR or SPA apps. TanStack Router plugs in via @tanstack/router-vite-plugin. Vite handles HMR, code-splitting, and asset bundling. Runtime env: no NEXT_PUBLIC analog - server reads process.env, passes to client however the app chooses. Reference point for understanding what TanStack Start adds on top. Taxonomy: build tooling for ui-app-emit.
reference_only.taxonomy: Use Vite directly (not via TanStack Start wrapper) as the build tool for custom SSR or SPA apps. TanStack Router plugs in via @tanstack/router-vite-plugin. Reference point for understanding what TanStack Start adds on top. Taxonomy: build tooling for ui-app-emit.
reference_only.stack_fit: Excellent Bun compatibility. Turborepo pipeline integration well-documented. No Nx-specific plugin needed. Custom SSR entry point gives full control over env injection.
reference_only.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted build; no managed service
reference_only.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit (vite.config.ts template)
reference_only.person_centric_fit: Neutral build tool; app architecture determines person-centric fit

rejected.name: rdpcrystal-edi-library
rejected.what: Commercial-licensed EDI library for Node.js with X12 HIPAA objects (X125010Document), used by Fortune 100/500 healthcare companies. Has per-developer annual subscription. Covers the same x12-revenue-cycle gap as node-x12 but with commercial support and broader HIPAA validation.
rejected.taxonomy: Commercial-licensed EDI library for Node.js with X12 HIPAA objects (X125010Document), used by Fortune 100/500 healthcare companies. Has per-developer annual subscription. Covers the same x12-revenue-cycle gap as node-x12 but with commercial support and broader HIPAA validation.
rejected.stack_fit: Node.js compatible; Bun compatibility unverified. Commercial license complicates air-gap bundling. No TS types means Zod schema must be hand-written for all outputs.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host library (no payer connectivity). Annual license cost per developer is ongoing OPEX.
rejected.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk) - same role as node-x12.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Same as node-x12 - transport layer, person surfaces built above.

rejected.name: Meteroid
rejected.what: Open-source billing + metering + invoicing platform in Rust with Postgres + Clickhouse + Kafka. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap as Lago but is higher-performance and earlier stage. AGPL-3.0 service boundary.
rejected.taxonomy: Open-source billing + metering + invoicing platform in Rust with Postgres + Clickhouse + Kafka. Covers same erp-invoice-subscription-recurring gap as Lago but is higher-performance and earlier stage. AGPL-3.0 service boundary.
rejected.stack_fit: AGPL service boundary: call via REST/gRPC only. Helm chart for K8s. Requires Clickhouse (new DB engine CuraOS does not currently run) - significant infra expansion. Rust binary = no language runtime conflict but adds binary dependency.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host (Helm). Meteroid Cloud for BYO lane. Satisfies dual requirement.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Same as Lago: person sees subscription + invoice views.

rejected.name: Commission calculation engine (scratch build)
rejected.what: No viable open-source TypeScript commission calculation engine exists on npm with meaningful adoption. Commercial SaaS (CaptivateIQ, Performio, Core Commissions) dominate; none are self-hostable. The @curaos/commission-engine package must be built from scratch. Gaps covered: commission calculation for sales (crm-insurance-broker.sales.commissions) and insurance broker commissions (premium-based, tiered, split, clawback on cancellation). The aposin openinsuranceplatform commission module and EspoCRM/SuiteCRM commission concepts serve as data model references only.
rejected.taxonomy: No viable open-source TypeScript commission calculation engine exists on npm with meaningful adoption. Commercial SaaS (CaptivateIQ, Performio, Core Commissions) dominate; none are self-hostable. The @curaos/commission-engine package must be built from scratch. Gaps covered: commission calculation for sales (crm-insurance-broker.sales.commissions) and insurance broker commissions (premium-based, tiered, split, clawback on cancellation). The aposin openinsuranceplatform commission module and EspoCRM/SuiteCRM commission concepts serve as data model references only.
rejected.stack_fit: Scratch build: TypeSpec contract (Commission rule: type enum flat/tiered/split, rate, clawback window) -> Drizzle schema -> pure TS calculation engine -> NestJS service. Inputs from sales-core (deal closed, amount), insurance-overlay (policy premium, effective date). Fold every edge case back into the generator per [[curaos-generator-evolution-rule]].
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: first-party self-host (build @curaos/commission-engine; BYO-3rd-party = commercial SaaS like CaptivateIQ via API)
rejected.generator_first_target: sdk-package (@curaos/commission-engine: rule model + calculation + clawback; folded into codegen so crm-core and insurance-overlay services share one canonical calc path)
rejected.person_centric_fit: Dual surface: person = the agent/broker sees their earned commission statement ('my earnings this month, this quarter, clawback alerts'); management = admin commission console (rule config, batch calc, payout batch, override, audit). Same commission engine, two re-centered surfaces.

rejected.name: Puck (@puckeditor/core)
rejected.what: MIT React visual editor that outputs clean JSON to any backend; drag-drop composition of your own React components; multi-zone DropZone/slot model; plugin API; theming via CSS custom properties. Directly covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` (builder-studio surface emission) and `platform-reporting-builder` (dashboard/workspace surface type + SavedLayout). Generator-first target: ui-app-emit / builder-core-service surface type.
rejected.taxonomy: MIT React visual editor that outputs clean JSON to any backend; drag-drop composition of your own React components; multi-zone DropZone/slot model; plugin API; theming via CSS custom properties. Directly covers gap `ui-kit-archetypes` (builder-studio surface emission) and `platform-reporting-builder` (dashboard/workspace surface type + SavedLayout). Generator-first target: ui-app-emit / builder-core-service surface type.
rejected.stack_fit: Pure React component - drops into any React app with zero wrapper. JSON output stored in any Postgres table via NestJS endpoint. No Bun/Drizzle/Zod friction. Plugin model extends without forking. Slot fields replace old DropZone API (minor migration note in v0.22 docs).
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only - no cloud dependency; data plane is your own backend (NestJS + Drizzle). Satisfies curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule: first-party self-hosted by default, no 3rd-party option needed since there is no vendor cloud to BYO.
rejected.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit + builder-core-service (SavedLayout entity stores Puck JSON; surface type 'dashboard' added to builder)
rejected.person_centric_fit: Builder-studio is the management surface for tenant admins authoring pages/layouts. Person-centric surfaces (patient-app, personal-* apps) consume the rendered JSON output. Dual-surface: admin authors with Puck -> person sees generated layout. Satisfies no-feature-loss: full admin control retained, person gets published result.
rejected.verify_notes: Local-vs-3rdparty rule (curaos-local-vs-3rdparty-rule) requires every integratable area to offer BOTH a first-party self-hosted option AND a BYO-third-party-provider option. Puck is self-hosted-only by design; there is no third-party manage
rejected.simpler_alternative: BLOCKING - ponytail ladder rung 2 fires: GrapesJS (v0.22.13) is already installed AND already integrated in builder-studio. A full working SitesCanvas component (storage-adapter.ts + SitesCanvas.tsx + basic-blocks.ts) exists, wired to the live builder-core-service via BuilderClient, with a real grapesjs JSONB field in the SurfaceInput DSL already persisted to Postgres. Adding Puck would introduce a SECOND visual editor with a different JSON schema, a different drag-drop model, and a different component registration API into the same app. That is net negative: more bundle, two data model dialects in SavedLayout, doubled maintenance surface, and a breaking migration for any surface already authored in GrapesJS. GrapesJS also ships a Style Manager, Device Manager, and Asset Manager out of the box - capabilities Puck explicitly does NOT include, requiring @curaos/ui components to supply everything. For the dashboard/reporting-builder surface type gap, GrapesJS's existing plugin API and block registration covers the same composition need. Extend the existing integration; do not add Puck.

rejected.name: Craft.js (@craftjs/core)
rejected.what: MIT React drag-drop page editor framework. Same target as Puck (ui-kit-archetypes / builder-studio surface). Predates Puck; more low-level; slower maintenance cadence.
rejected.taxonomy: MIT React drag-drop page editor framework. Same target as Puck (ui-kit-archetypes / builder-studio surface). Predates Puck; more low-level; slower maintenance cadence.
rejected.stack_fit: React only; no Bun/NestJS friction but more boilerplate than Puck for the same outcome.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Same dual-surface potential as Puck but maintenance risk makes it a liability.

rejected.name: Plasmic (AGPL Studio + MIT SDK)
rejected.what: Visual builder + headless CMS. Studio is AGPL; SDK is MIT. The editor itself is partly closed-source (codegen engine); self-hosting the full studio is limited. Covers same ui-kit-archetypes / builder-core gap as Puck.
rejected.taxonomy: Visual builder + headless CMS. Studio is AGPL; SDK is MIT. The editor itself is partly closed-source (codegen engine); self-hosting the full studio is limited. Covers same ui-kit-archetypes / builder-core gap as Puck.
rejected.stack_fit: SDK integrates with React but editor requires Plasmic cloud or limited self-hosted mode. AGPL studio creates service-boundary risk for CuraOS's builder-studio embedding use case.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Fails first-party self-hosted requirement: editor engine is not fully self-hostable under permissive terms.

rejected.name: ToolJet (AGPL)
rejected.what: AGPL open-source internal tool / admin-panel builder. Same target as Appsmith. Has embed docs but AGPL license means embedding in CuraOS builder-studio (a non-AGPL product) requires commercial license purchase.
rejected.taxonomy: AGPL open-source internal tool / admin-panel builder. Same target as Appsmith. Has embed docs but AGPL license means embedding in CuraOS builder-studio (a non-AGPL product) requires commercial license purchase.
rejected.stack_fit: AGPL blocks embedding without commercial license. Appsmith (Apache 2.0) is a drop-in alternative that avoids this risk entirely.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host possible but license cost required for non-AGPL products.

rejected.name: Martin (maplibre/martin)
rejected.what: Blazing-fast PostGIS-native vector tile server (auto-discovers geometry tables/functions, serves MVT from PostGIS, PMTiles, MBTiles). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles: the fleet/map UI needs real-time vector overlays from the same PostGIS instance geospatial-core-service already owns.
rejected.taxonomy: Blazing-fast PostGIS-native vector tile server (auto-discovers geometry tables/functions, serves MVT from PostGIS, PMTiles, MBTiles). Covers taxonomy_id curaos_cross_cutting.geospatial.tiles: the fleet/map UI needs real-time vector overlays from the same PostGIS instance geospatial-core-service already owns.
rejected.stack_fit: No Node wrapper needed. Add as Docker sidecar (ghcr.io/maplibre/martin) reading the same CNPG PostGIS DB via DATABASE_URL. NestJS geospatial-core-service emits geometry tables; Martin auto-discovers them. Zero-config for most setups. Helm chart available; Zarf-compatible static binary for air-gap.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: first-party self-host; BYO tile CDN (MapTiler, Esri) covered by same MapLibre frontend pointing at a different tile URL
rejected.generator_first_target: service/geospatial-core-service: generator emits Drizzle geometry columns + GiST index + publishes Martin config snippet; ui-app-emit MapView archetype references tile URL env var
rejected.person_centric_fit: Enables real-time map rendering (patient location, delivery tracking, facility finder, geofence overlays) in both personal-* apps and business management dashboards without building a tile pipeline from scratch.
rejected.verify_notes: Hard reject on architectural grounds, not quality grounds. Martin is an excellent tool - fast (2-3x faster than nearest competitor in benchmarks), well-maintained, correct license. The rejection is because ADR-0206 (Accepted 2026-05-24) alr
rejected.simpler_alternative: Already decided. ADR-0206 (Accepted 2026-05-24) explicitly resolved tile serving as: PMTiles file archive + HTTP range-request proxy from MinIO/S3 + NestJS thin proxy + MapLibre GL JS client-side render. The existing Requirements.md for geospatial-core-service encodes this. The ponytail ladder stops at rung 2: the solution already exists in the codebase/ADR. Martin is a new process, new sidecar, new operational surface - none of which is needed given the resolved architecture.

rejected.name: Fleetbase (AGPL-3.0)
rejected.what: Full open-source logistics OS (dispatch, order management, driver app, warehouse, proof-of-delivery, routing). Much broader than Traccar. Laravel/PHP backend. Would cover fleet-core dispatch workflow surface comprehensively.
rejected.taxonomy: Full open-source logistics OS (dispatch, order management, driver app, warehouse, proof-of-delivery, routing). Much broader than Traccar. Laravel/PHP backend. Would cover fleet-core dispatch workflow surface comprehensively.
rejected.stack_fit: AGPL means any modification must be open-sourced under AGPL or a commercial license purchased. PHP/Laravel stack does not compose into the NestJS/Bun monorepo. Would need full REST API adapter layer. Weight far exceeds the dispatch workflow value CuraOS needs.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Driver app (Navigator) is strong; but CuraOS personal-tracking-service already handles driver-side positioning. Overlap creates duplication.

rejected.name: @medplum/hl7 (+ panates/hl7v2 as fallback)
rejected.what: @medplum/hl7 v4.3.13 (Apache-2.0) provides HL7v2 parsing, serialization, and utilities as part of the Medplum monorepo. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] HL7v2 ADT ingestion sub-gap and [healthcare.interop.hl7] taxonomy_id. Ingests ADT (A01/A02/A03/A08), ORU (lab results), ORM (orders) messages and converts to normalized domain events. panates/hl7v2 (MIT, v1.8.0, April 2026, 762 stars) is the standalone alternative with full parser + serializer + TCP server + client classes if Medplum dependency is unwanted.
rejected.taxonomy: @medplum/hl7 v4.3.13 (Apache-2.0) provides HL7v2 parsing, serialization, and utilities as part of the Medplum monorepo. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] HL7v2 ADT ingestion sub-gap and [healthcare.interop.hl7] taxonomy_id. Ingests ADT (A01/A02/A03/A08), ORU (lab results), ORM (orders) messages and converts to normalized domain events. panates/hl7v2 (MIT, v1.8.0, April 2026, 762 stars) is the standalone alternative with full parser + serializer + TCP server + client classes if Medplum dependency is unwanted.
rejected.stack_fit: Both are pure TS, Bun-compatible. NestJS service (healthstack-interop-service) imports parser, wraps in a message-handler that emits normalized FHIR resources as Kafka/Redpanda events via AsyncAPI contract. No wrapper layer needed beyond the message-routing NestJS module.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted only: ingests HL7v2 messages from on-prem hospital systems (Mirth Connect, direct TCP). No 3rd-party cloud variant needed for parsing itself; routing to external clearinghouses handled separately.
rejected.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-interop-service): HL7v2 ingest handler wired to AsyncAPI event emitter; codegen-emitter adds HL7MessageHandler scaffold per resource type.
rejected.person_centric_fit: ADT A08 patient-update messages feed real-time patient record sync to patient-app; ORU results close the care loop for lab/imaging notifications to the person.
rejected.verify_notes: Reject on two independent grounds: (1) ADR-0115 §4.4.2 is already RESOLVED with HAPI HL7v2 JVM sidecar - adopting a TS npm package for HL7v2 parsing contradicts the locked architecture without opening a new ADR. (2) @medplum/hl7's hard dep
rejected.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is already the locked decision. ADR-0115 SD-4 (§4.4.2) resolved this: HAPI HL7v2 (JVM, Apache 2.0) + Spring Integration MLLP adapter is the canonical HL7v2 stack. This is reflected verbatim in healthstack-interop-service CONTEXT.md stack table and Requirements.md F-01. The architecture is: MLLP TCP 2575 -> HAPI JVM sidecar -> NATS relay -> NestJS translation worker. The NestJS layer consumes already-parsed NATS messages and never touches raw HL7 bytes. Neither @medplum/hl7 nor panates/hl7v2 is needed in this architecture. The candidate does not mention the ADR lock.

rejected.name: @asymmetrik/node-fhir-server-core (bluehalo fork)
rejected.what: MIT-licensed pluggable Express FHIR REST server framework for Node.js. You register resource-typed handler modules (Patient, Observation, etc.) against a spec-enforced FHIR interface; the framework handles routing, capability statement, search parameter parsing, versioning, and auth. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 resource provider framework) as the Node-native alternative to running a full HAPI Java server. Provides the outer FHIR REST shell; CuraOS owns the persistence adapters (Drizzle/Postgres).
rejected.taxonomy: MIT-licensed pluggable Express FHIR REST server framework for Node.js. You register resource-typed handler modules (Patient, Observation, etc.) against a spec-enforced FHIR interface; the framework handles routing, capability statement, search parameter parsing, versioning, and auth. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 resource provider framework) as the Node-native alternative to running a full HAPI Java server. Provides the outer FHIR REST shell; CuraOS owns the persistence adapters (Drizzle/Postgres).
rejected.stack_fit: Runs on Express; needs a thin NestJS adapter or can run as a standalone Express app alongside NestJS on a different port. Bun can run Express apps. Resource handlers map 1:1 to NestJS service methods. Weaker TS story than Medplum - types via DefinitelyTyped. Capability statement and search-param parsing are the primary value.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted only: provides the server layer; CuraOS controls storage.
rejected.generator_first_target: Codegen emitter: healthstack-interop-service resource handler scaffold (one handler class per FHIR resource in .tsp contract). Translator-pair pattern from gap-analysis [interop-fhir-server] maps cleanly to handler->domain-service call.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Exposes FHIR patient compartment read correctly; supports SMART scoping for person 'my record' access pattern.
rejected.verify_notes: Candidate recommendation was "trial." Actual finding is reject on three independent grounds: (1) maintenance overstated -- last real release Sep 2024, fragmented into 5+ forks, Express 4 dependency with no ESM path; (2) integration claim op
rejected.simpler_alternative: Yes -- @medplum/fhir-router (Apache 2.0, pure TypeScript, ESM-native as of Medplum v5, no mongodb dep, framework-agnostic FhirRepository interface, FHIR R4 routing + capability statement, actively maintained through 2025-2026). Implement a NestJS service that satisfies FhirRepository; the router handles FHIR URL dispatch. This is the same architectural pattern the candidate proposes (handler -> domain-service call) but with a cleaner TS contract, no CJS shim, no mongodb baggage, and a healthier upstream. Cost: write a ~50-line NestJS adapter against FhirRepository instead of a parallel Express port.

rejected.name: fhir-kit-client (Vermonster)
rejected.what: Node.js FHIR R4/STU3/DSTU2 client library (MIT). Covers CRUD, search, batch/transaction bundles, versioned history. Would fill @curaos/fhir-client HTTP adapter role. However: last release was 3 years ago (v1.9.2, 2022), effectively unmaintained. Listed for completeness and to explicitly rule it out in favor of @medplum/core.
rejected.taxonomy: Node.js FHIR R4/STU3/DSTU2 client library (MIT). Covers CRUD, search, batch/transaction bundles, versioned history. Would fill @curaos/fhir-client HTTP adapter role. However: last release was 3 years ago (v1.9.2, 2022), effectively unmaintained. Listed for completeness and to explicitly rule it out in favor of @medplum/core.
rejected.stack_fit: Works but stale. @medplum/core is a drop-in superior replacement with active maintenance.

rejected.name: Restate (@restatedev/restate-sdk + restate-nest)
rejected.what: Durable execution runtime: HTTP-native, virtual-object actors with built-in K/V store, exactly-once semantics, single Rust binary deployment. Integrates with XState as persistent event loop. Fills: lighter-weight alternative to Temporal for stateful per-entity workflows (per-patient state machines, per-claim saga). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.actor-model
rejected.taxonomy: Durable execution runtime: HTTP-native, virtual-object actors with built-in K/V store, exactly-once semantics, single Rust binary deployment. Integrates with XState as persistent event loop. Fills: lighter-weight alternative to Temporal for stateful per-entity workflows (per-patient state machines, per-claim saga). Taxonomy: platform.workflow.durable-execution, platform.workflow.actor-model
rejected.stack_fit: TypeScript SDK (@restatedev/restate-sdk) is pure TS, Bun-compatible. NestJS adapter via restate-nest (decorator-based). Works as HTTP handler - Restate server calls YOUR NestJS endpoints with durability guarantees. No Node worker_threads dependency - avoids Bun blocker. Drizzle/Postgres can be used inside steps normally. Restate server adds one pod.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host: single Restate binary / Docker / Helm - fully self-hostable. BYO-3rd-party: Restate Cloud (managed). BUSL means cannot resell Restate server as a managed service, but using it for CuraOS own workflows is fine.
rejected.generator_first_target: SDK pkg: codegen emitter could scaffold VirtualObject class + handler methods from TypeSpec service definitions
rejected.person_centric_fit: Virtual objects map naturally to per-person stateful journeys (one actor per patient/claim/order). Built-in K/V avoids polling patterns. XState + Restate combo gives BPMN-grade state modeling with Restate durability.
rejected.verify_notes: Research value: high - decisive prior art and a codebase blocker found. Key findings: (1) PONYTAIL LADDER FIRES AT RUNG 2: Temporal 1.17.2 is already installed in workflow-core-service. A second durable-execution runtime is YAGNI until Temp
rejected.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is decisive. CuraOS `workflow-core-service` already ships @temporalio/activity + @temporalio/client + @temporalio/worker + @temporalio/workflow at v1.17.2 plus nestjs-temporal-core 3.2.8. Temporal IS the installed durable-execution engine. Ponytail ladder rung 2 fires: the capability already exists in this codebase. Adding Restate adds a second durable-execution runtime with overlapping semantics, split ownership of actor/saga state, two sets of operational concerns, and a BUSL-licensed binary to every air-gap bundle. That is the opposite of ponytail. The Temporal TypeScript SDK does NOT run on Bun (issue #1334 is open, worker_threads dependency), which is the claimed advantage of Restate. However: CuraOS workflow workers can run under Node while the NestJS services run under Bun - the two runtimes are already separated at the service boundary. This is not a new problem requiring a new tool.

rejected.name: Metabase
rejected.what: Self-hosted BI tool with a visual query builder and dashboards. Listed in research seeds for comparison. Covered here to document the rejection verdict. AGPL-3 license on the open-source version triggers legal review when embedded in a closed-source SaaS product. Multi-tenancy and white-labeling require the Enterprise plan (significant annual cost). No TypeSpec/contract-first integration path. Superset (Apache 2.0) + Cube (Apache 2.0) covers the same surface without the license and cost risks.
rejected.taxonomy: Self-hosted BI tool with a visual query builder and dashboards. Listed in research seeds for comparison. Covered here to document the rejection verdict. AGPL-3 license on the open-source version triggers legal review when embedded in a closed-source SaaS product. Multi-tenancy and white-labeling require the Enterprise plan (significant annual cost). No TypeSpec/contract-first integration path. Superset (Apache 2.0) + Cube (Apache 2.0) covers the same surface without the license and cost risks.
rejected.license: AGPL-3 (open source). Commercial Enterprise license for embedded/white-label.
rejected.stack_fit: No NestJS SDK. Embedded iframes similar to Superset but multi-tenant RLS needs Enterprise plan. No generator path. Heavier Java/Clojure runtime vs Superset Python.
rejected.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted or Metabase Cloud. Both options exist but both have the license concern for embedded use.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Good BI UX but org-centric by default. No person-journey re-centering support. Superset + guest tokens achieves the same with better license.

rejected.name: nools (noolsjs/nools)
rejected.what: Rete-based forward-chaining rules engine in JavaScript (Node.js). Drools analog in JS. Fills healthcare.care.rules-engine with inference chain support.
rejected.taxonomy: Rete-based forward-chaining rules engine in JavaScript (Node.js). Drools analog in JS. Fills healthcare.care.rules-engine with inference chain support.
rejected.stack_fit: No TypeScript types. No recent releases. No active maintainer.
rejected.person_centric_fit: N/A - abandoned project introduces technical debt risk.

rejected.name: Casbin (node-casbin + nest-authz + casbin-drizzle-adapter)
rejected.what: In-process policy enforcement library (ACL/RBAC/ABAC via policy model + adapter pattern). nest-authz provides NestJS guard/decorator. casbin-drizzle-adapter loads/saves policies from Drizzle-managed tables. Fills: lightweight in-process authz without a sidecar if Cerbos is overkill. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (in-process authz).
rejected.taxonomy: In-process policy enforcement library (ACL/RBAC/ABAC via policy model + adapter pattern). nest-authz provides NestJS guard/decorator. casbin-drizzle-adapter loads/saves policies from Drizzle-managed tables. Fills: lightweight in-process authz without a sidecar if Cerbos is overkill. Taxonomy: healthcare.platform.rbac-clinical-roles (in-process authz).
rejected.stack_fit: npm-import into any NestJS service. casbin-drizzle-adapter lets policy table live in same Postgres schema. No sidecar. However, no centralized policy management across services -- each service loads its own policy (vs Cerbos single PDP).
rejected.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter-target -- could emit Casbin policy model + adapter init per service, but centralized Cerbos PDP is a better generator target
rejected.person_centric_fit: Neutral -- policies can encode person-owns-their-data rules but no built-in consent-awareness or clinical-role semantics; those must be hand-coded in policy model.

rejected.name: rrule (jkbrzt/rrule)
rejected.what: De-facto JS recurrence rule library, RFC-5545 RRULE parsing and date expansion. Still widely used (1.7M weekly downloads) but last published 3 years ago (v2.8.1). Open issues accumulating since 2025 with no maintainer responses. Reference for migration path - existing codebases using it should plan migration to rrule-temporal.
rejected.taxonomy: De-facto JS recurrence rule library, RFC-5545 RRULE parsing and date expansion. Still widely used (1.7M weekly downloads) but last published 3 years ago (v2.8.1). Open issues accumulating since 2025 with no maintainer responses. Reference for migration path - existing codebases using it should plan migration to rrule-temporal.
rejected.stack_fit: Works with Bun/NestJS. No resource changes needed. But timezone handling has known bugs and Date-based (not Temporal). The CuraOS calendar-core schema already uses Luxon for IANA tz - rrule.js Date objects create an impedance mismatch.
rejected.person_centric_fit: Would serve same use cases as rrule-temporal but with tz bugs that affect cross-timezone patient scheduling.

note: Synth agent crashed (large ctx); register built deterministically from 17 lane results + 47 adversarial verifications. | Upgraded: full workflow synth landed (86 agents) with authored backlog_enrichment(37)/decision_enrichment(13)/coverage_gaps(7) grafted under authored_synth; my deterministic 150-candidate register retained as the raw register.

register_curated.name: @medplum/fhirtypes + @medplum/core
register_curated.what: R4 type definitions (compile-time FHIR schema) + MedplumClient FHIR HTTP adapter. @medplum/core accepts custom fetch (Bun-compatible), no React peer-dep.

service_boundary.name: Zitadel
service_boundary.adapter: @zitadel/node (Apache-2.0) behind @curaos/identity-sdk CuraOSEnterpriseIdPProvider; OIDC standard tokens. Never fork+ship modified Zitadel.

service_boundary.name: Langfuse

service_boundary.name: Postal
service_boundary.adapter: HTTP API ~50 LOC NestJS PostalProvider behind notify-core EmailProvider port. Ruby+MariaDB run as isolated containers.

service_boundary.name: Apache Superset
service_boundary.adapter: NestJS SupersetService mints guest tokens with RLS; @superset-ui/embedded-sdk iframe. Reads its own Postgres, not CuraOS schemas.

service_boundary.name: cql-translation-service / fqm-execution-service

reference_only.mine: Underlying @ai-sdk/* approach (which Mastra wraps) trialed directly. Revisit when Bun+Turbo fixed + scope audit confirmed + Fastify adapter.

reference_only.name: Puck
reference_only.reason: GrapesJS already installed AND integrated in builder-studio (SitesCanvas wired to builder-core). Puck would add a second JSON schema + drag-drop model. Pre-1.0 breaking changes; no BYO-3rd-party path.
reference_only.mine: None - extend existing GrapesJS integration. Reference Puck slot data model only.

reference_only.name: GrapesJS (notify-service use case)
reference_only.verdict: confirmed-trial (incumbent for builder-core; reference for notify)
reference_only.reason: Core BSD-3 already installed + in production for builder-core HTML template surface. Studio SDK is commercial (air-gap blocked). React 19 interop bug requires require() workaround.
reference_only.mine: For notify-service NotificationTemplate authoring consider EmailBuilder.js (MIT, JSON-first) before extending GrapesJS to a second use case. Resolve wrapper identity (@grapesjs/react vs grapesjs-react).

reference_only.name: Form.io
reference_only.reason: Renderer license contested (OSLv3 vs MIT - network-copyleft risk); drag-drop builder is commercial-only (Enterprise Form Builder Module); RHF+Zod+Radix already installed cover wizards; TS types regressed in v5.
reference_only.mine: JSON schema definition format + conditional-logic model as prior art for generator's form-schema design. RJSF (MIT) is the correct trial candidate if schema-driven rendering beyond RHF is needed.

reference_only.name: TanStack Start / TanStack Router SPA
reference_only.reason: CVE-2026-45321 supply-chain (42 @tanstack/* packages, react-start in affected set, SLSA attestation failed); ui-app-emit 7.6k lines hardwired to Next.js (emitter fork, not a flag); no Nx generator; Vite 8 SSR regression; RSC 2.5mo old.
reference_only.mine: File-based routing + Virtual File Routes API as more-emitter-friendly pattern. Revisit when Nx plugin ships + Vite8 fixed + SLSA attestation published + concrete greenfield app that Next.js cannot serve.

reference_only.name: next-runtime-env
reference_only.reason: Inactive 15mo (no Next 15 release, no Next 16 peer dep); ADR-0220 already leans in-house; implementation is ~25 LOC of standard RSC.
reference_only.mine: Implement @curaos/runtime-config (~35 LOC: PublicEnvScript RSC + isomorphic env() + useRuntimeConfig hook + Zod boot validator), emit from ui-app-emit. Close ADR-0220 open question as in-house. Reference next-runtime-env as prior-art pattern.

reference_only.name: TigerBeetle
reference_only.reason: accounting-core-service already has complete double-entry GL on Drizzle/Postgres (balanced trigger + idempotent consume + outbox + immutability trigger). No BYO-3rd-party leg (rule violation). Bun NAPI async-I/O compat unconfirmed.
reference_only.mine: Hot-path balance-snapshot pattern. Revisit only if profiler shows Postgres GL insert latency as actual bottleneck at real load AND Bun NAPI confirmed.

reference_only.name: cqf-ruler
reference_only.reason: Canonical alphora/cqf-ruler Docker image ~3yr stale; FHIR R5 broken (#797); superseded by HAPI FHIR native support.
reference_only.mine: CDS Hooks sidecar + Measure-eval + PlanDefinition/$apply architecture pattern. Trial candidate is hapifhir/hapi-fhir-jpaserver-starter with cdshooks.enabled=true (evaluate after @gorules/zen-engine for synchronous inline CDS).

reference_only.name: DBOS Transact / Restate / Inngest / BullMQ
reference_only.reason: DBOS: Conductor license ceiling + Citus schema unvalidated + local-vs-3rdparty violation. Restate: Temporal already installed (rung 2); BUSL air-gap unresolved. Inngest: SSPL air-gap blocker. BullMQ: pg-boss covers same lane without Redis.
reference_only.mine: Temporal (installed) + pg-boss + XState cover durable/queue/state lanes. Add BullMQ only if pg-boss throughput ceiling is proven by profiler.

reference_only.name: Typesense / OpenSearch / Qdrant / SpiceDB / Cube / Evidence / Metabase
reference_only.reason: Typesense GPL-3 distribution + benchmarks favor Meilisearch. OpenSearch heavyweight JVM (upgrade path not start). Qdrant: pgvector covers <5M vectors. SpiceDB/Cube/Evidence overkill now. Metabase AGPL embed + Enterprise multi-tenant cost.
reference_only.mine: pgvector+Meilisearch+Superset cover v1 search/reporting. Qdrant when pgvector recall insufficient at >5M vectors. OpenSearch when FHIR $everything/complex aggregations needed.

decision_enrichment.finding: @medplum/hl7 carries hard dep on full Medplum SDK (vendor coupling). Verdict reject - the JVM HAPI HL7v2 sidecar + Spring MLLP decision stands. NestJS consumes parsed NATS messages, never raw HL7 bytes.
decision_enrichment.change: Sharpens: no TS HL7v2 npm package; keep locked architecture. Re-open only via new ADR if eliminating the JVM sidecar, then eval panates/hl7v2 (after Z-segment/timezone/Bun-TCP verification).

decision_enrichment.finding: @asymmetrik/node-fhir-server-core stale/CJS/mongodb-coupled. @medplum/fhir-router (Apache-2.0, ESM, FhirRepository interface, no mongodb) is the correct framework; @medplum/fhirtypes+core the type+client layer.

decision_enrichment.finding: Keycloak rejected (ADR-0120 binding + JVM 1.25GB + stale NestJS adapter). pocket-id (self-host leg, already deployed) + Zitadel (BYO enterprise leg) form the dual-IdP. @simplewebauthn/server already integrated for passkeys.
decision_enrichment.change: Sharpens ADR-0120: confirms no Keycloak; dual-IdP = pocket-id + Zitadel via @curaos/identity-sdk provider abstraction (ADR-0154). SMART-on-FHIR scope extension is separate work; clinical-user fallback auth is a gap to design.

decision_enrichment.finding: xyflow confirmed-adopt (ADR accepted). Use ELK.js not dagre for layout. May 2026 supply-chain CVE - current versions clean, requires pin+SHA+SBOM gate.
decision_enrichment.change: Sharpens: generator MUST emit proOptions hideAttribution:true default; pin exact + SHA verify per version-pinning rule; ELK.js (EPL, layout-only) co-selection confirmed.

decision_enrichment.finding: Martin rejected - ADR-0206 PMTiles + range-proxy decision stands. Martin has no auth/RLS/wildcard-CORS issues for multi-tenant; no official Helm.
decision_enrichment.change: No change - reaffirms PMTiles. Open new ADR only if live dynamic PostGIS overlays (patient/vehicle positions) become a concrete need, then weigh PMTiles-static vs Martin-dynamic with real traffic.

coverage_gaps.capability: X12 EDI 837/835/270/271 generation + parsing in TS
coverage_gaps.why_scratch: No healthy maintained TS X12 library exists in 2026. node-x12 (837 gen) and x12-parser (835) are both maintenance-stale since ~2022 (single maintainers, js-edi successor abandoned 5yr). EDIFlow is a 5-month-old pre-1.0 solo project with unverifiable claims. Build @curaos/x12-sdk by vendoring node-x12 v1.7.1 + x12-parser pinned, OR port-adapt OpenEMR (GPL) X12 logic to fresh TS (X12 5010 segment layout is ANSI standard, not copyrightable). Conformance-test vs real payer fixtures.

coverage_gaps.capability: Compound/repartition/withholding tax + multi-currency FX gain/loss posting
coverage_gaps.why_scratch: node-sales-tax covers only flat-rate VAT/GST lookup; compound tax, repartition, and withholding have no importable TS option - must port-adapt Odoo (LGPL) account_tax logic into @curaos/tax-engine. dinero.js covers money arithmetic but FX gain/loss balancing entries are domain logic with no library - build in @curaos/currency.

coverage_gaps.capability: Insurance commission engine (splits, multi-level override, clawback)
coverage_gaps.why_scratch: No viable open-source TS commission-calc engine exists with meaningful adoption (commercial SaaS dominates: CaptivateIQ/Performio, none self-hostable). Build @curaos/commission-engine from scratch with money-path discipline (integer minor units, audit trail, clawback attribution windows). Reference data models from aposin openinsuranceplatform + openIMIS.

@gorules/zen-engine eval is MIT but its BRMS authoring UI is commercial + air-gap-blocked (Enterprise Gov plan). No air-gap-clean open-source decision-table editor with the needed clinical semantics exists. Defer to JDM JSON in DB + repo as the ceiling, then build a minimal JDM table editor as a CuraOS app/site-builder surface (generator-first, no external license dependency).

react-big-calendar has no resource axis; FullCalendar resource/timeline views are premium OEM (custom-priced for tenant distribution); schedule-x resource scheduler is premium per-project. No MIT multi-resource calendar exists. Either gate behind explicit OEM license conversation (FullCalendar premium) OR build a resource-grid SchedulingBoard in @curaos/ui from headless primitives.

pocket-id and Zitadel issue OIDC tokens but neither natively implements SMART App Launch Framework scopes (scp, fhirUser, patient context, launch parameter). fhirclient (SMART client) consumes them but a SMART-compliant scope issuer must be built on top of the chosen IdP - extend identity-service with SMART scope semantics (custom work, no drop-in).

GrapesJS Studio SDK (visual builder) is commercial + air-gap-blocked; GrapesJS core outputs HTML blobs not structured templates with locale slots. Form.io builder is commercial-only. EmailBuilder.js (MIT, JSON-first) is the candidate to evaluate, else build a minimal template-variable editor in the CuraOS app/site-builder for notify-service NotificationTemplate authoring.

candidates.name: @medplum/fhirtypes + @medplum/core + @medplum/fhirpath
candidates.what: Decomposable TypeScript npm packages from the Medplum monorepo: @medplum/fhirtypes = generated R4 type definitions (compile-time FHIR schema enforcement); @medplum/core = MedplumClient (FHIR CRUD/search/subscription), resource validators, FHIRPath evaluator client; @medplum/fhirpath = standalone FHIRPath engine. Together they fill gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 client + type-safe contract base for @curaos/fhir-client package) and [clinical-fhir-resource-set] (R4 type safety for Observation/Condition/MedicationRequest/AllergyIntolerance etc. contracts). These packages can be npm-imported WITHOUT running the Medplum server.
candidates.stack_fit: Native TS, Bun-compatible, no wrapper needed. @medplum/core client talks to any FHIR R4 server (Medplum, HAPI, or CuraOS own server). @medplum/fhirtypes provides the type layer for TypeSpec-generated FHIR contracts. Zod schemas can wrap fhirtypes for runtime validation. NestJS service can inject MedplumClient as a provider.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: first-party self-host (point client at CuraOS interop-service FHIR endpoint) AND BYO third-party (point at external HAPI or cloud FHIR server). Same client, different base URL.
candidates.maintenance_health: ~9k+ GitHub stars (medplum/medplum monorepo); v5.x released Oct 2025, v4 in maintenance; weekly npm publishes (last @medplum/hl7 publish 7 days before search date); Y Combinator backed; active contributor community.
candidates.person_centric_fit: R4 types enforce correct FHIR resource shape for patient-facing APIs (Patient compartment, Observation, CarePlan); FHIRPath enables declarative 'my record' queries across resources without extra service code.
candidates.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/fhir-client): @medplum/fhirtypes as the type layer + @medplum/core client as the HTTP adapter. TypeSpec emitter for healthstack services imports fhirtypes as the canonical R4 shape.
candidates.risks: Medplum types track R4; R5 support paused as of 2025 (company focused on US Core/USCDI). Importing @medplum/core pulls React peer-dep transitively in some package configs - use @medplum/fhirtypes standalone if that is a concern. Package versioning must stay aligned across @medplum/* imports (semver lock all at same major).

candidates.name: @medplum/hl7 (+ panates/hl7v2 as fallback)
candidates.what: @medplum/hl7 v4.3.13 (Apache-2.0) provides HL7v2 parsing, serialization, and utilities as part of the Medplum monorepo. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] HL7v2 ADT ingestion sub-gap and [healthcare.interop.hl7] taxonomy_id. Ingests ADT (A01/A02/A03/A08), ORU (lab results), ORM (orders) messages and converts to normalized domain events. panates/hl7v2 (MIT, v1.8.0, April 2026, 762 stars) is the standalone alternative with full parser + serializer + TCP server + client classes if Medplum dependency is unwanted.
candidates.stack_fit: Both are pure TS, Bun-compatible. NestJS service (healthstack-interop-service) imports parser, wraps in a message-handler that emits normalized FHIR resources as Kafka/Redpanda events via AsyncAPI contract. No wrapper layer needed beyond the message-routing NestJS module.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted only: ingests HL7v2 messages from on-prem hospital systems (Mirth Connect, direct TCP). No 3rd-party cloud variant needed for parsing itself; routing to external clearinghouses handled separately.
candidates.maintenance_health: @medplum/hl7: published weekly with rest of Medplum monorepo (v4.3.13, 7 days old at search time). panates/hl7v2: v1.8.0 April 2026, ~762 GitHub stars, maintained by Panates org, actively tested (CI passing).
candidates.person_centric_fit: ADT A08 patient-update messages feed real-time patient record sync to patient-app; ORU results close the care loop for lab/imaging notifications to the person.
candidates.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-interop-service): HL7v2 ingest handler wired to AsyncAPI event emitter; codegen-emitter adds HL7MessageHandler scaffold per resource type.
candidates.risks: @medplum/hl7 is utilities-level (not a full standalone HL7 server); for high-volume MLLP server needs, panates/hl7v2 server class is stronger. Choose based on whether CuraOS needs to accept raw MLLP connections (panates) vs process already-parsed messages (either).

candidates.name: fhir-validator-js (Outburn) + fhir-validator-wrapper (FHIR/HL7)
candidates.what: Node.js wrappers around the canonical HL7 Java FHIR Validator CLI. fhir-validator-js (Apache-2.0, v1.4.0, Mar 2025) manages JVM sessions, supports multiple concurrent IG contexts, downloads JAR automatically. Fills gap [clinical-fhir-resource-set] test requirement: 'FHIR-R4 validation per resource (HAPI/fhir.js in CI)' and [interop-fhir-server] 'FHIR-validate every resource in CI'. Runs against US Core, SNOMED-backed IGs, and custom CuraOS profiles.
candidates.stack_fit: Used in CI (just ci gate) and optionally as a sidecar in healthstack-interop-service for inbound FHIR resource validation. Requires JRE in the pod/CI runner - add openjdk:21-jre to Dockerfile. Bun-compatible for the JS wrapper layer. NestJS service calls it async before persisting inbound resources.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted: JVM runs locally in CI or K8s pod. No external call; IGs loaded locally. Supports air-gap with pre-downloaded JAR + IG packages.
candidates.maintenance_health: v1.4.0 published March 2025 by Outburn (Israeli digital health firm); actively maintained. Underlying HL7 Java Validator is the gold-standard reference maintained by HL7 International.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Indirect: validated FHIR resources are trustworthy inputs to patient-facing read models; validation failures caught in CI not at patient query time.
candidates.generator_first_target: Codegen emitter: every healthstack service's CI test suite gets a FHIR validation step scaffolded by the service-core template.
candidates.risks: JVM dependency adds ~200MB to CI image and pod footprint. Startup latency (warm JVM ~5-10s). For lightweight structural validation only (no IG profile checks), @medplum/core's built-in schema validator is sufficient and JVM-free. Use fhir-validator-js for conformance testing against US Core/CuraOS profiles in CI, not in hot request path.

candidates.name: @asymmetrik/node-fhir-server-core (bluehalo fork)
candidates.what: MIT-licensed pluggable Express FHIR REST server framework for Node.js. You register resource-typed handler modules (Patient, Observation, etc.) against a spec-enforced FHIR interface; the framework handles routing, capability statement, search parameter parsing, versioning, and auth. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] (FHIR R4 resource provider framework) as the Node-native alternative to running a full HAPI Java server. Provides the outer FHIR REST shell; CuraOS owns the persistence adapters (Drizzle/Postgres).
candidates.stack_fit: Runs on Express; needs a thin NestJS adapter or can run as a standalone Express app alongside NestJS on a different port. Bun can run Express apps. Resource handlers map 1:1 to NestJS service methods. Weaker TS story than Medplum - types via DefinitelyTyped. Capability statement and search-param parsing are the primary value.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted only: provides the server layer; CuraOS controls storage.
candidates.maintenance_health: Last npm publish Feb 2025; multiple community forks (@projecttacoma, @monosolution etc.) active. Original repo at bluehalo (formerly Asymmetrik). Mature, stable, widely deployed in US healthcare.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Exposes FHIR patient compartment read correctly; supports SMART scoping for person 'my record' access pattern.
candidates.generator_first_target: Codegen emitter: healthstack-interop-service resource handler scaffold (one handler class per FHIR resource in .tsp contract). Translator-pair pattern from gap-analysis [interop-fhir-server] maps cleanly to handler->domain-service call.
candidates.risks: CommonJS-first; ESM/Bun compatibility needs test. Less actively maintained than Medplum's server. For a greenfield, Medplum's server packages are more modern TS; this is better as a pattern reference + lightweight FHIR shell if full Medplum server adoption is undesirable.

candidates.name: fhirclient (SMART on FHIR JS/TS client)
candidates.what: Official SMART on FHIR JS/TS client library (Apache-2.0). Handles EHR-launch and standalone-launch OAuth2 + PKCE flows, token refresh, FHIR resource fetching with scoping. Fills gap [interop-fhir-server] SMART patient compartment scoping and the 'my record' patient-portal export path. Required for any external EHR integration (Epic, Cerner, Athena) and for CuraOS's patient-app acting as a SMART app against external EHRs.
candidates.stack_fit: Node 18+ / Bun compatible. Used in NestJS backend for server-side SMART launch; in React patient-app for browser-side launch. No wrapper needed. Pairs with @medplum/fhirtypes for typed resource access.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: first-party (CuraOS SMART server via identity-service + healthstack-interop-service) AND BYO-3rd-party (Epic/Cerner SMART launch for external EHR connection).
candidates.maintenance_health: Maintained by SMART Health IT (Boston Children's Hospital + Harvard); active GitHub; npm package fhirclient widely used across health IT ecosystem.
candidates.person_centric_fit: The primary mechanism for 'my record' patient access: person authenticates via SMART, client fetches their compartment resources from any FHIR R4 server. Directly enables [healthcare.registration.patient-portal-phr] gap.
candidates.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/fhir-client): SMART launch adapter wrapping fhirclient, exposed as a NestJS provider.
candidates.risks: Requires SMART/OAuth2 server on CuraOS side (identity-service must implement SMART scopes - likely medium lift). External EHR SMART registrations are manual/credentialed.

candidates.name: Snowstorm (IHTSDO) - already in use as terminology-service backing
candidates.what: Apache-2.0 FHIR terminology server with deep SNOMED CT support plus LOINC, ICD-10, ICD-10-CM. CuraOS terminology-service is already backed by Snowstorm (verified: 'present-strong', taxonomy_id healthcare.interop.snomed-loinc-terminology). Gap is NOT a new server but value-set API adapter: healthstack-quality-service needs $expand + $validate-code calls for eCQM measure code binding, and healthstack-meds-service needs RxNorm $lookup. Fills [ecqm-quality-engine] value-set binding step and coded-field binding for all FHIR resource services.
candidates.language: Java (server) / REST API consumed from TS
candidates.stack_fit: Already deployed. NestJS services call FHIR $expand/$lookup via HTTP. A thin @curaos/terminology-sdk client (wrapping fetch) is the only new code needed. No new infrastructure.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: First-party self-hosted (Snowstorm on-cluster). BYO-3rd-party path: VSAC API (NLM) or Ontoserver for hosted value sets.
candidates.maintenance_health: IHTSDO-maintained; active 2025-2026 releases; community-wide adoption for SNOMED CT.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Enables plain-language display names for coded clinical values shown to patients (SNOMED preferred term, LOINC display). Required for correct AVS and patient-app clinical summaries.
candidates.generator_first_target: SDK pkg (@curaos/terminology-sdk): thin codegen-emitted client for $expand/$lookup/$translate, consumed by healthstack service TypeSpec-generated code.
candidates.risks: RxNorm requires separate NLM license/load into Snowstorm. Snow Owl (Apache-2.0, ~500 stars) is the alternative if full RxNorm + UMLS authoring is needed. LOINC requires LOINC license (free but registration-gated). Air-gap: pre-load release packages.

candidates.name: OHIF Viewer v3 + Orthanc DICOM server
candidates.what: OHIF (MIT, v3.11, React/Cornerstone.js): zero-footprint browser DICOM viewer supporting DICOMweb (QIDO-RS/WADO-RS/STOW-RS), self-hosted as static bundle or Docker. Orthanc (GPL-3, C++): lightweight PACS server with DICOMweb plugin, REST API, widely used as self-hosted DICOM store. Together they fill gap [healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.orders.dicom-pacs) for healthstack-imaging-service DICOM/PACS integration. OHIF iframe-embedded into CuraOS imaging UI; Orthanc runs as K8s sidecar service.
candidates.stack_fit: OHIF: iframe embed or separate static deployment - no import into NestJS. Orthanc: HTTP REST + WADO-RS from healthstack-imaging-service. DICOMweb client calls from NestJS (simple fetch wrapper). No deep integration needed. GPL-3 on Orthanc = service boundary safe (CuraOS code never inside Orthanc).
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-hosted Orthanc (air-gap compatible) AND BYO-3rd-party PACS (Sectra, Change Healthcare, AWS HealthImaging - any DICOMweb-compliant store).
candidates.maintenance_health: OHIF: MIT, ~4.5k stars, v3.11 released 2025-2026, Open Health Imaging Foundation backed. Orthanc: UCLouvain/Orthanc Team, stable production-grade, used globally in hospitals.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Patient 'my imaging' view: OHIF renders study thumbnails + viewer for person to see their own scans. Clinician worklist is the management surface. Same DICOMweb backend serves both.
candidates.generator_first_target: Service (healthstack-imaging-service): DICOMweb adapter stub emitted by codegen; OHIF viewer URL rendered in imaging-app React component.
candidates.risks: Orthanc GPL-3: must stay strictly service-isolated; no Orthanc code in CuraOS source. DICOMweb plugin for Orthanc has a separate (more permissive) license but confirm before packaging for air-gap Zarf bundle. OHIF iframe deprecation warning for v3 embedded-viewer script-tag mode - use iframe approach.

candidates.name: CDS Hooks spec + sandbox-cds-services (HL7 open)
candidates.what: CDS Hooks is the HL7-standard REST protocol for embedding clinical decision support into EHR workflows (hook -> prefetch context -> CDS service returns cards/suggestions). cds-hooks/sandbox-cds-services provides Node/Express reference implementations. Fills gap [cds-rules-engine] (taxonomy_id: healthcare.care.clinical-decision-support) integration protocol: CuraOS healthstack-quality-service exposes CDS Hooks endpoints so external EHRs and its own clinician-app can call it. Enables drug-allergy checks, reminders, and risk-score cards to surface in any SMART-enabled EHR.
candidates.stack_fit: CDS Hooks = HTTP contract, not a library. healthstack-quality-service (NestJS) adds CDS Hooks REST endpoints (GET /cds-services, POST /cds-services/{id}). No npm package to import; implement against spec. @curaos/cds-hooks-sdk can be a thin NestJS decorator that maps CDS Hook request/response to TypeSpec contract types.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both: self-hosted (CuraOS serves hooks to its own clinician-app) AND BYO-3rd-party (Epic/Cerner EHR calls CuraOS CDS service via CDS Hooks standard).
candidates.maintenance_health: HL7 standard (active), sandbox updated Oct 2025, spec at cds-hooks.org. Mature and required for US EHR interoperability.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Indirect: CDS alerts from hooks become patient-facing nudges in patient-app (notify + tasks surfaces). Clinician gets inline decision support at point of care.
candidates.generator_first_target: Contract-typespec (CDS rule + hook contract in healthstack-quality.tsp) + codegen-emitter for CDS Hooks endpoint scaffold.
candidates.risks: Protocol-only; implementation effort for actual rule logic is separate (the gap-analysis CDS work). Registration with external EHRs is a credentialing process. FHIR prefetch must be scoped correctly to avoid PHI leakage.

candidates.name: fhir-kit-client (Vermonster)
candidates.what: Node.js FHIR R4/STU3/DSTU2 client library (MIT). Covers CRUD, search, batch/transaction bundles, versioned history. Would fill @curaos/fhir-client HTTP adapter role. However: last release was 3 years ago (v1.9.2, 2022), effectively unmaintained. Listed for completeness and to explicitly rule it out in favor of @medplum/core.
candidates.stack_fit: Works but stale. @medplum/core is a drop-in superior replacement with active maintenance.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both (client library, server-agnostic).
candidates.maintenance_health: Last publish 2022 (3 years stale). No recent issues resolved. Effectively abandoned.
candidates.person_centric_fit: N/A - replaced by @medplum/core.
candidates.generator_first_target: N/A - reject.
candidates.risks: Unmaintained. Security vulnerabilities unfixed. No R5 path.

verified.name: @medplum/fhirtypes + @medplum/core + @medplum/fhirpath
verified.license_confirmed: Apache-2.0 confirmed across all three packages via npm registry. Clean permissive license; compatible with self-hosted, air-gap, and BYO-provider distribution. No viral or copyleft conditions. No contributor-license surprises found.
verified.maintenance_confirmed: Active: v5.1.21 published June 2026 (3 days before review date). Weekly publish cadence verified across @medplum/fhirtypes, @medplum/core, @medplum/hl7. v5.0.0 shipped Q4 2025; v4.x in maintenance. YC-backed, ~9k GitHub stars, active contributor community. No public CVEs or GHSA advisories found for client packages. Socket.dev lists @medplum/core as "no external dependencies" - no supply-chain width concern. v5 ESM-by-default is a real breaking change but already shipped and stable.
verified.simpler_alternative: @types/fhir (DefinitelyTyped) for type-only use: active, zero-dep, no version lock to client. REJECTED: known open defects (circular FhirResource reference, missing constraint profiles), not co-evolved with a FHIR client, would require a separate FHIR HTTP client anyway. Savings do not outweigh the completeness gap in a HIPAA-adjacent codebase. @medplum/fhirtypes wins on correctness. For the HTTP client layer: hand-rolling a thin fetch wrapper against a FHIR R4 OpenAPI spec is feasible but produces a maintenance surface that @medplum/core already solved with weekly patches and validator parity. Ponytail verdict: adopt the library, not the scratch implementation.
verified.notes:
Verdict is confirmed-trial (not confirmed-adopt) because:
1. ESM-in-NestJS-11 integration path is unproven in the CuraOS repo; needs a one-off spike to validate tsconfig + Bun + NestJS 11 ESM interop before generator templates are written against it.
2. @medplum/fhirpath is lower confidence without a concrete FHIRPath query use case in HealthStack v1 scope - leave it out of the initial @curaos/fhir-client scaffold and add when a concrete consumer is written.
3. Semver lock requirement is real: all @medplum/* imports must pin to the same major. Renovate grouping rule needed or drift between fhirtypes and core versions will cause type mismatches.

Adopt @medplum/fhirtypes + @medplum/core as the base for @curaos/fhir-client. Run a one-sprint ESM compatibility spike first. Promote to confirmed-adopt after spike green. @medplum/fhirpath: defer to when a FHIRPath consumer is identified in HealthStack requirements.

Sources confirmed:
- @medplum/core npm (v5.1.21, Apache-2.0, no external deps per socket.dev)
- @medplum/fhirtypes npm (v5.1.21, Apache-2.0)
- Medplum v5 release blog (ESM-default, React 19 required in @medplum/react only)
- Medplum FHIR R5 blog (R5 paused intentionally; R6 tracked)
- @types/fhir DefinitelyTyped open issues (circular ref + missing types)
- Socket.dev @medplum/core scan (no supply-chain alerts, no external deps)
- Medplum 2025 roadmap (US Core / USCDI focus confirmed)

verified.name: @medplum/hl7 (+ panates/hl7v2 as fallback)
verified.license_confirmed: Apache-2.0 (@medplum/hl7) confirmed. MIT (panates/hl7v2) confirmed. Both permissive, compatible with self-hosted + air-gap distribution. No copyleft risk. License claim is accurate.
verified.maintenance_confirmed: @medplum/hl7: ACTIVE - but version claim is stale. Candidate states v4.3.13; actual latest is v5.1.22 (published 2026-06-25), a full major jump. Weekly cadence confirmed - legitimate concern is that a monorepo-versioned package at v5.x with a hard runtime dep on @medplum/core@5.x means semver churn is constant. panates/hl7v2: candidate claims v1.8.0 and 762 stars. Actual: v1.9.0 published 2026-04-20, star count is 24-25 (not 762 - likely confused with another repo). Single maintainer (panates org, one active contributor). Open unfixed issues: Z-segment parse throws (issue #15, unresolved), timezone default handling bug (issue #29). 8 transitive deps including obscure panates-internal packages (valgen, ts-gems, @jsopen/objects, node-events-async) - non-trivial supply-chain surface for a healthcare parsing path.
verified.integration_reality: Candidate claims @medplum/hl7 is utilities-level with no wrapper needed. Reality: @medplum/hl7@5.1.22 carries a HARD runtime dependency on @medplum/core@5.1.22 (3.86 MB unpacked, 0 external deps of its own but is the entire Medplum platform SDK). Importing it drags the full Medplum client SDK into healthstack-interop-service. That is significant coupling to a competing healthcare platform's SDK inside CuraOS's own healthstack service - a vendor-lock signal, not a clean utility import. The MLLP server class in panates/hl7v2 (which the candidate concedes is the relevant component for raw TCP ingest) would run under Bun's Node.js compatibility layer - not natively tested, and Bun's net.createServer compatibility is partial for some TCP patterns. Neither package has documented NestJS integration or TypeSpec/AsyncAPI code-gen hooks.
verified.simpler_alternative: YES - and it is already the locked decision. ADR-0115 SD-4 (§4.4.2) resolved this: HAPI HL7v2 (JVM, Apache 2.0) + Spring Integration MLLP adapter is the canonical HL7v2 stack. This is reflected verbatim in healthstack-interop-service CONTEXT.md stack table and Requirements.md F-01. The architecture is: MLLP TCP 2575 -> HAPI JVM sidecar -> NATS relay -> NestJS translation worker. The NestJS layer consumes already-parsed NATS messages and never touches raw HL7 bytes. Neither @medplum/hl7 nor panates/hl7v2 is needed in this architecture. The candidate does not mention the ADR lock.
verified.notes: Reject on two independent grounds: (1) ADR-0115 §4.4.2 is already RESOLVED with HAPI HL7v2 JVM sidecar - adopting a TS npm package for HL7v2 parsing contradicts the locked architecture without opening a new ADR. (2) @medplum/hl7's hard dep on the full Medplum SDK is an unacceptable coupling vector inside CuraOS's own healthstack. If the JVM sidecar architecture is ever revisited (e.g., to eliminate the sidecar), open a new ADR against SD-4 and evaluate panates/hl7v2 at that point - after verifying Z-segment + timezone bugs are resolved and Bun TCP compat is confirmed. The candidate's star count for panates/hl7v2 (762 claimed vs 24-25 actual) is a significant credibility error that should trigger re-evaluation of other claims in the research dispatch that produced this candidate.

verified.name: fhir-validator-js (Outburn) + fhir-validator-wrapper (FHIR/HL7)
verified.license_confirmed: Apache-2.0 confirmed on both packages. Compatible with self-hosted + air-gap distribution and local-vs-3rdparty duality. No copyleft concern.
verified.maintenance_confirmed: fhir-validator-js v1.4.0 published March/April 2025 by Outburn; no newer release as of June 2026 (15 months stale on the JS wrapper). The underlying HL7 Java validator (hapifhir/org.hl7.fhir.core) is actively maintained with 2025-2026 releases. fhir-validator-wrapper (FHIR/HL7 org) released v1.0.70 in August 2025. The JS wrapper layer is low-churn by design (thin shim), but the 15-month gap on the JS package warrants watching. npm download volume for the FHIR validator family is in the tens-per-week range, indicating niche adoption - not zero, but not battle-hardened at scale.
verified.integration_reality: Bun compatibility is functional: Bun implements Node child_process fully including spawn, which is how fhir-validator-js shells out to the JVM. No documented Bun-specific incompatibilities found. NestJS integration requires a thin wrapper service - no native NestJS module exists, so you write an @Injectable() that manages the validator session lifecycle. Not complex, but it is glue code (~50-100 LOC) that the candidate description glosses over as trivial. The generator-first claim holds: a codegen emitter can scaffold the NestJS service + CI step per healthstack service without bespoke hand-edits. Air-gap: supported via FHIR_VALIDATOR_JAR_PATH + FHIR_VALIDATOR_NO_AUTO_INSTALL env vars with pre-bundled JAR - works with Zarf as long as the JAR and IGs are included in the bundle. JVM footprint is real: openjdk:21-jre adds ~180-200MB to CI images and a ~5-10s cold-start per session. For CI-only usage (not hot path) this is acceptable.
verified.simpler_alternative: Yes, with a critical scope distinction. @medplum/core validateResource / indexStructureDefinitionBundle provides JVM-free FHIR R4 schema + profile validation in pure TypeScript. It supports US Core and custom StructureDefinitions loaded locally. It is already a plausible dependency given CuraOS HealthStack's FHIR surface. For structural correctness checks and base-spec conformance it is sufficient and has zero JVM cost. The gap: @medplum/core does NOT replicate the HL7 reference validator's terminology-binding checks (SNOMED, LOINC value set expansion), FHIRPath invariant evaluation depth, or gold-standard IG conformance scoring. For interoperability certification-grade CI validation against US Core + CuraOS custom profiles, the HL7 Java validator is the only authoritative tool. The two are complementary, not interchangeable - use @medplum/core for fast unit-level checks and fhir-validator-js for conformance-gate CI runs.
verified.notes: SECURITY FLAG - three active CVEs in the underlying HL7 Java validator (org.hl7.fhir.validation / org.hl7.fhir.core): CVE-2026-34361 (CVSS 9.3, Critical) and CVE-2026-34360 (blind SSRF) both affect the /loadIG HTTP endpoint - fixed in 6.9.4. CVE-2026-55470 (ReDoS in DSTU2 FHIRPathEngine) - fixed in 6.9.10. The SSRF CVEs are HTTP-service-mode specific (the /loadIG endpoint); CLI batch mode is not directly exposed to network SSRF. However, fhir-validator-js v1.4.0 internally starts the fhir-validator-wrapper HTTP service to handle sessions - meaning the /loadIG endpoint IS reachable on localhost when the wrapper is running. The blast radius in a CI pod is limited (no external auth tokens configured typically), but in a K8s sidecar adjacent to services that hold FHIR server credentials, CVE-2026-34361 is a real token-theft risk if the sidecar port is reachable by other pods. MITIGATION REQUIRED before adopting the sidecar deployment model: (1) pin the JAR to 6.9.10+ (FHIR_VALIDATOR_JAR_PATH with pre-fetched fixed version), (2) bind the HTTP service to 127.0.0.1 only, (3) add NetworkPolicy to block pod-to-pod access to the validator port. For CI-only use (no sidecar), risk is low with a fixed JAR. The fhir-validator-js JS wrapper layer does not itself pin the JAR version - it downloads latest by default, which means auto-upgrade to fixed versions happens automatically in online mode. Air-gap mode requires manual JAR pinning to a patched version. VERDICT: trial, not full adopt, because (a) the sidecar model has active CVE exposure requiring explicit mitigations before production, (b) the JS wrapper is 15 months stale with no pin to a patched JAR version, (c) @medplum/core covers the unit-test lane without JVM cost. Correct adoption scope: fhir-validator-js in CI conformance gate only (not sidecar), JAR pinned to 6.9.10+, NetworkPolicy applied, @medplum/core for in-process structural validation. Generator emitter scaffolds both. Revisit full adopt when fhir-validator-js cuts a release that pins a 6.9.10+ JAR by default.

verified.name: @asymmetrik/node-fhir-server-core (bluehalo fork)
verified.license_confirmed: MIT confirmed. Compatible with self-hosted + air-gap distribution. No CLA or copyleft. However, the candidate misstates the active package: the @asymmetrik scope (2.2.5) was last published Feb 2023. The @bluehalo scope rename (2.3.0) was last published Sep 2024, not "Feb 2025" as stated in maintenance_health. The Feb 2025 activity is user-opened bug reports, not a maintainer release.
verified.maintenance_confirmed: Stale. @bluehalo/node-fhir-server-core 2.3.0 published Sep 2024, no release since. Five+ community forks on npm (@projecttacoma, @monosolution, @ahryman40k, @wiredkode, @soinsalud) -- fragmentation signal that upstream cannot absorb contributions. Open issues from early 2025 are unresponded user bug reports. No CVEs found against the package directly, but helmet@7 + jsonwebtoken@9 dependencies are current; express@4 is EOL-adjacent.
verified.integration_reality: Worse than claimed. This is a monolithic Express 4 CJS app scaffold, not a pluggable routing library. Hard facts: (1) No ESM export map, no "type":"module" -- Bun can require() CJS but this means no tree-shaking and interop friction with the ESM-first CuraOS Bun/NestJS stack. (2) mongodb@6 is a hard production dependency in the shipped package.json even though the framework claims "bring your own persistence" -- a Postgres/Drizzle shop inherits a MongoDB driver unconditionally. (3) NestJS integration requires either a second Express port running in parallel or a custom HTTP adapter bridge; there is no NestJS module, no NestJS lifecycle hooks, no DI integration. That is non-trivial glue, not a "thin adapter." (4) 25+ runtime dependencies (moment, moment-timezone, passport, snyk, serve-favicon, superagent, xss, sanitize-html) bloat the image and expand the attack surface. (5) TypeSpec has no emitter that targets this framework's handler interface; building the generator-first codegen target described in the candidate would require authoring the emitter from scratch against an underdocumented, CJS-only hook API.
verified.simpler_alternative: Yes -- @medplum/fhir-router (Apache 2.0, pure TypeScript, ESM-native as of Medplum v5, no mongodb dep, framework-agnostic FhirRepository interface, FHIR R4 routing + capability statement, actively maintained through 2025-2026). Implement a NestJS service that satisfies FhirRepository; the router handles FHIR URL dispatch. This is the same architectural pattern the candidate proposes (handler -> domain-service call) but with a cleaner TS contract, no CJS shim, no mongodb baggage, and a healthier upstream. Cost: write a ~50-line NestJS adapter against FhirRepository instead of a parallel Express port.
verified.notes: Candidate recommendation was "trial." Actual finding is reject on three independent grounds: (1) maintenance overstated -- last real release Sep 2024, fragmented into 5+ forks, Express 4 dependency with no ESM path; (2) integration claim optimistic -- mongodb hard dep, no NestJS module, parallel-port or adapter glue required, CJS-only; (3) simpler already-available alternative exists (@medplum/fhir-router) that covers the same FHIR routing + capability statement value prop with better TS story, ESM, no dead-weight deps, and generator-first codegen is equally feasible against its FhirRepository interface. Adopt @medplum/fhir-router for the [interop-fhir-server] gap instead. Retain node-fhir-server-core as a reference-only pattern source for FHIR REST handler design -- hence downgrade-reference is the closest structural verdict, but the candidate as submitted (npm-import integration mode) is a reject.

candidates.name: node-x12
candidates.what: ASC X12 parser, generator, query engine, and mapper with stream support. Covers the entire @curaos/x12-sdk gap (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle): ISA/GS envelope, 837P/837I generation, 835 ERA parsing, 270/271 eligibility round-trip. The query language (dot-path selectors) can extract segment values without writing loop code. JS-EDI-Notation provides a JSON-friendly interchange format that maps cleanly to TypeSpec contracts.
candidates.maintenance_health: ~54 stars; last substantive release v1.6.1 (2022-09-03); author declared maintenance-only in 2021, waiting on js-edi v1.0. No 2023-2026 commits visible. Low bus-factor (solo). Tested on 835 production data but stale on 5010 edge cases.
candidates.stack_fit: Pure TypeScript, ESM-compatible; works under Bun. No NestJS adapter needed - import into @curaos/x12-sdk package directly. Drizzle schema sits upstream of it. Zod can validate the parsed JS objects. Generator-first: wrap in sdk-emit output so service code never calls node-x12 directly.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (library). No payer connectivity. Must pair with a clearinghouse adapter (Stedi/Claim.MD/Availity) for the 3rd-party leg.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Underlying transport only. Person surfaces (claim-status timeline, up-front copay estimate, EOB plain-language statement) are built above it in claims-service. The library enables real 270 eligibility checks that power patient cost estimates before service.
candidates.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk) - node-x12 is a vendored dep inside the SDK; sdk-emit exposes typed TS facades, never raw X12 API.
candidates.risks: Maintenance stale since 2022; 5010 IG edge cases (NPI qualifiers, loop 2300 CLM05-3, NDC format) may need patches. Solo author, no backing org. Version 2.x (js-edi) has been 'in development' since 2021 with no stable release. Recommend locking to v1.6.1 + wrapping tightly so upgrade path is encapsulated.

candidates.name: x12-parser (tastypackets)
candidates.what: Streaming X12 parser built on Node Transform streams - no generation, query, or mapping. Tested against 1M+ 835 records in production. Pure stream-in / JSON-out: ideal for high-volume 835 ERA ingestion pipelines (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle, 835 parse leg only). Zero non-dev dependencies.
candidates.maintenance_health: ~60 stars; ~9,500 weekly npm downloads (higher than node-x12); v1.3.0 last release; last significant commit circa 2021. Small but active download base suggests stable production use.
candidates.stack_fit: Works under Bun (Node streams compatible). No native TS types - need a thin .d.ts wrapper. Does not generate X12, so must pair with node-x12 or scratch-written generator for 837 emission. Best as the 835 stream ingestion path inside @curaos/x12-sdk.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-host only (library). Parse leg only.
candidates.person_centric_fit: Enables ERA auto-posting speed - faster 835 processing directly benefits patient EOB delivery and balance reconciliation on the person-app.
candidates.generator_first_target: SDK package (@curaos/x12-sdk, 835 stream parser path).
candidates.risks: Parse-only; no generation or eligibility. No TS types. Maintenance similarly stale. Use as complement to node-x12, not replacement.

candidates.name: Medplum (@medplum/core + FHIR billing model)
candidates.what: Apache-2.0 FHIR R4 platform with full Claim/ClaimResponse/ExplanationOfBenefit/CoverageEligibilityRequest resource models in strongly-typed TypeScript. Covers taxonomy x12-revenue-cycle at the FHIR contract layer: Claim maps to 837 CLM, ExplanationOfBenefit maps to 835 ERA, CoverageEligibilityRequest/Response maps to 270/271. Bot architecture (TypeScript lambdas) used for clearinghouse bridge. Self-hostable via Docker/K8s.

candidates.stack_fit: Pure TypeScript, Bun-compatible. @medplum/core imports cleanly into NestJS services. The FHIR resource types replace or augment hand-authored TypeSpec for billing contracts - use as reference types or adopt directly as the billing contract shape. Drizzle would store a serialized FHIR blob or a relational projection. Generator-first: codegen emitter can output Medplum-typed DTOs instead of custom ones.
candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both. Full self-hosted deployment (Docker/K8s, same as CuraOS stack). Also has managed SaaS. The Bot/Subscription model is the BYO-3rd-party bridge - one Bot per clearinghouse adapter (Stedi, Waystar, Availity) with a config-driven clearinghouse-id.
candidates.person_centric_fit: ExplanationOfBenefit is natively patient-centric (it IS the EOB the patient receives). CoverageEligibilityResponse powers up-front copay estimates surfaced in the patient app. ClaimResponse provides the denial reason in structured form that enables patient-readable denial explanations.

candidates.risks: Full platform adoption is overkill if only the billing type system is wanted - CuraOS should cherry-pick @medplum/core types, not run the Medplum server alongside its own services. Bot architecture is Medplum-hosted lambdas by default; for CuraOS self-host, the equivalent is NestJS event handlers. Need to verify @medplum/core tree-shaking is clean under Bun.

candidates.what: API-first commercial clearinghouse with free EDI Inspector tool and open-source awesome-edi resources. Stedi's production API provides X12 translate (EDI to JSON), eligibility (270/271), and claims (837/835) via REST - the BYO-clearinghouse option for CuraOS. EDI Inspector at edi.stedi.com is a free validation tool useful for @curaos/x12-sdk conformance testing during development.

candidates.name: @lowlysre/icd-10-cm (+ NLM Clinical Tables API)
candidates.what: Zero-dependency npm package bundling the full ICD-10-CM code set (74,000+ codes) for offline local lookup with TypeScript types. Covers medical-coding lookup gap (taxonomy: x12-revenue-cycle, diagnosis pointer on 837 claim). Pairs with NLM Clinical Tables free API (clinicaltables.nlm.nih.gov) for CPT/HCPCS/SNOMED when online - the self-host vs BYO split for code lookup.

candidates.local_vs_3rdparty: Both paths: @lowlysre/icd-10-cm is the local/air-gap self-host path. NLM Clinical Tables is the BYO-3rd-party free public API path. CPT codes require AMA license - no free local option; AutoICD API ($49/mo Reference tier) is the BYO path for CPT/HCPCS.

candidates.person_centric_fit: Same as node-x12 - transport layer, person surfaces built above.

Blocks air-gap deployment if license requires online activation. Recommend only if node-x12 proves insufficient and commercial support is required.

verified.name: node-x12
verified.license_confirmed: MIT confirmed. Permissive, compatible with self-hosted + air-gap distribution and the local-vs-3rdparty duality. No CLA, no copyleft, no network-service restriction. Safe.
verified.maintenance_confirmed: STALE, worse than claimed. npm shows latest version 1.7.1, not 1.6.1 as stated in the candidate brief (minor discrepancy but suggests the submitter was working from incomplete data). Last publish ~4 years ago (i.e. ~2022). No commits or releases since. js-edi (the promised successor) remains explicitly "work in progress, not fit for production use" as of 2026 - the successor has not shipped in 5 years of development, which is a strong signal the author has effectively abandoned the ecosystem. 27K weekly npm downloads indicates real production use but not active maintenance. No CVEs or security advisories found. Bus factor = 1, no backing org.
verified.integration_reality: Broadly accurate but optimistic on one point. Pure TypeScript, CJS + ESM outputs, no native deps - runs under Bun without shims. No NestJS adapter needed; wraps cleanly as an internal dep of @curaos/x12-sdk. Zod validation of parsed objects is straightforward. The generator-first wrapping (sdk-emit facades, never raw API exposed to service code) is the correct architecture and isolates the upgrade surface. The TypeSpec/OpenAPI contract sits above the SDK layer and is unaffected by the library internals. However: the candidate claims Drizzle "sits upstream" - this is irrelevant framing; Drizzle handles persistence, node-x12 handles wire format, they never touch. No direct integration friction there but also no actual integration benefit claimed is real. The dot-path query language is real and documented.
